items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Tribase: A Vector Data Query Engine for Reliable and Lossless Pruning Compression using Triangle Inequalities'.
items.abstract: The authors of the paper provide detailed descriptions and comprehensive facilities to setup the required software system and perform most of their experiments. We are able to reproduce and verify the results sufficiently to support the core thesis of the paper. Although running experiments with the same configurations as in the paper is straightforward, there are limitations in flexibility and portability.
items.abstract_zh: 该论文的作者提供了详细的描述和全面的设施来设置所需的软件系统并执行大部分实验。我们能够充分重现和验证结果以支持论文的核心论文。尽管使用与论文中相同的配置运行实验很简单，但在灵活性和可移植性方面存在限制。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Memento Filter: A Fast, Dynamic, and Robust Range Filter'.
items.abstract: We evaluate the reproducibility of ''Memento Filter: A Fast, Dynamic, and Robust Range Filter''. The paper proposes a dynamic range filter with robust false-positive guarantees. Using the authors' public artifact and scripts (repository: https://github.com/n3slami/Memento_Filter), we reproduced the paper's main experimental results (paper Figures 8–14). The reproduced trends closely match the original claims.
items.abstract_zh: 我们评估了“Memento Filter：快速、动态且鲁棒的范围过滤器”的再现性。该论文提出了一种具有鲁棒误报保证的动态范围滤波器。使用作者的公共工件和脚本（存储库：https://github.com/n3slami/Memento_Filter），我们重现了论文的主要实验结果（论文图 8-14）。复制的趋势与原始声明非常吻合。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'LpBound: Pessimistic Cardinality Estimation Using ?p-Norms of Degree Sequences'.
items.abstract: We successfully reproduced the core findings and performance results of the paper ''LpBound: Pessimistic cardinality Estimation Using ℓ p -Norms of Degree Sequences''. The paper proposed an intriguing bridge between information theory and cardinality estimation and derives tight upper bounds of the intermediate cardinalities by combining Shannon inequalities, ℓ p -norms, and linear programming. The authors provided scripts to execute the experiments, the original datasets, and comprehensive, well-written documentation that allowed to reproduce of the core results.
items.abstract_zh: 我们成功地重现了论文“LpBound：使用 ℓ p -度数序列范数进行悲观基数估计”的核心发现和性能结果。该论文提出了信息论和基数估计之间的有趣桥梁，并通过结合香农不等式、ℓ p 范数和线性规划推导了中间基数的严格上限。作者提供了执行实验的脚本、原始数据集以及全面且编写良好的文档，可以重现核心结果。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Galley: Modern Query Optimization for Sparse Tensor Programs'.
items.abstract: The core thesis of the paper has been successfully reproduced. The authors provided a functional artifact containing the implementation of Galley together with scripts for running the experiments and generating the corresponding figures. With minor adjustments to the environment and paths, the vast majority of results were reproduced and the generated plots closely matched those presented in the submitted paper. The artifact enabled reliable replication of the main findings and validated the performance claims of the original work.
items.abstract_zh: 论文核心论文已成功转载。作者提供了一个功能工件，其中包含 Galley 的实现以及用于运行实验和生成相应图形的脚本。通过对环境和路径进行细微调整，可以重现绝大多数结果，并且生成的图与提交的论文中呈现的图非常匹配。该工件能够可靠地复制主要发现，并验证原始工作的性能声明。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'H-Rocks: CPU-GPU accelerated Heterogeneous RocksDB on Persistent Memory'.
items.abstract: This reproducibility report evaluates the artifact accompanying H-Rocks, a heterogeneous key–value store that accelerates per- sistent RocksDB operations using GPU and Persistent Memory. We reproduce the key experimental results of the paper on the authors' original hardware configuration, including Intel Optane Persistent Memory and an NVIDIA Titan RTX GPU. During the initial reproduction phase, we encountered minor ambiguities and missing components in the artifact documentation. These issues were promptly resolved, resulting in clearer instructions and a more complete artifact. Following these refinements, we success- fully reproduced the reported performance trends and results. Our evaluation confirms that the H-Rocks artifact is reproducible on the specified hardware and software environment. However, H- rocks requires a strict adherence to the required hardware system configuration for consistent results
items.abstract_zh: 该再现性报告评估了 H-Rocks 附带的工件，H-Rocks 是一种异构键值存储，可使用 GPU 和持久内存加速持久 RocksDB 操作。我们在作者的原始硬件配置上重现了论文的关键实验结果，包括英特尔傲腾持久内存和 NVIDIA Titan RTX GPU。在最初的复制阶段，我们在工件文档中遇到了一些小的歧义和缺失的组件。这些问题得到了及时解决，从而产生了更清晰的指令和更完整的工件。经过这些改进，我们成功地重现了报告的性能趋势和结果。我们的评估证实 H-Rocks 工件在指定的硬件和软件环境上是可重现的。然而，H-rocks 需要严格遵守所需的硬件系统配置才能获得一致的结果

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'SBSC: A fast Self-tuned Bipartite proximity graph-based Spectral Clustering'.
items.abstract: This reproducibility report evaluates the SBSC algorithm for fast self-tuned bipartite proximity graph-based spectral clustering using the artifacts released by the original authors. The provided source code, datasets, and documentation enabled successful execution of the complete experimental pipeline. By following the supplied instructions, the main experimental results reported in the paper were reproduced successfully. Overall, the core claims of the work were validated under the described experimental setup.
items.abstract_zh: 该再现性报告使用原作者发布的工件评估了 SBSC 算法，用于快速自调整的基于二分邻近图的谱聚类。提供的源代码、数据集和文档使完整的实验流程得以成功执行。按照提供的说明，成功重现了论文中报告的主要实验结果。总的来说，这项工作的核心主张在所描述的实验设置下得到了验证。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Auto-Test: Learning Semantic-Domain Constraints for Unsupervised Error Detection in Tables'.
items.abstract: The main experiments in the paper ''Auto-Test: Learning Semantic-Domain Constraints for Unsupervised Error Detection in Tables'' can be reproduced successfully. The authors provided a repository with clear instructions on reproducing each experiment, the code used to run the program, and the link to the datasets used in the experiment.
items.abstract_zh: 论文“自动测试：学习表中无监督错误检测的语义域约束”中的主要实验可以成功重现。作者提供了一个存储库，其中包含有关重现每个实验的清晰说明、用于运行程序的代码以及实验中使用的数据集的链接。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'On the Feasibility and Benefits of Extensive Evaluation'.
items.abstract: We accurately reproduced the core findings of the original paper, encompassing its reported explanation quality and extensive evaluation results of existing methods. This was facilitated by the authors' release of sample datasets, well-documented scripts, and plotting tools, which allowed us to recreate the figures with near-exact precision.
items.abstract_zh: 我们准确地再现了原始论文的核心发现，包括其报告的解释质量和现有方法的广泛评估结果。作者发布的示例数据集、记录良好的脚本和绘图工具促进了这一点，使我们能够以近乎精确的精度重新创建图形。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'PLM4NDV: Minimizing Data Access for Number of Distinct Values Estimation with Pre-trained Language Models'.
items.abstract: We successfully reproduced the key performance results that presented in Tables~3 and~4 of the original paper. The authors provided a GitHub repository. https://github.com/bytedance/plm4ndv that includes the implementation, detailed build and execution instructions, and links to the datasets used in the experiments. We appreciate the authors' assistance with environment setup and debugging.
items.abstract_zh: 我们成功地重现了原始论文表~3 和~4 中的关键性能结果。作者提供了一个 GitHub 存储库。 https://github.com/bytedance/plm4ndv，其中包括实现、详细的构建和执行说明以及实验中使用的数据集的链接。我们感谢作者在环境设置和调试方面提供的帮助。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Constant Optimization Driven Database System Testing'.
items.abstract: The authors proposed a new automated methodology for finding bugs in databases and provided scripts to run their bug-finding method against production databases. They also provided a list of bugs found through their approach which are currently filed in the respective database's bug tracking system. We were able to successfully run the scripts and verify the bug artifacts provided by the authors.
items.abstract_zh: 作者提出了一种新的自动化方法来查找数据库中的错误，并提供了脚本来针对生产数据库运行他们的错误查找方法。他们还提供了通过他们的方法发现的错误列表，这些错误目前已归档在各自数据库的错误跟踪系统中。我们能够成功运行脚本并验证作者提供的错误工件。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Live Patching for Distributed In-Memory Key-Value Stores'.
items.abstract: This paper proposes a new solution to handle the service downtime and performance degradation issues posed by traditional rolling updates when maintaining distributed in-memory key-value stores (IMKVS). The authors propose a live patching system for Redis Cluster which proactively manages software updates by modifying process memory directly without restarting nodes, while leveraging the cluster-internal gossip protocol for automatic patch distribution. The authors present a set of experiments comparing the efficacy of live patching (LP) to state-of-the-art rolling updates (CP) on a range of cluster sizes and memory states up to 200~GiB. We find that on the hardware specified (including Intel Xeon and AMD EPYC configurations) we were able to reproduce the key results and plots shown in the paper, illustrating the robust nature of these experiments and confirming that live patching achieves constant-time updates regardless of data size. Furthermore, the authors provided well-written shell scripts and easy-to-follow instructions within a QEMU virtual machine environment to reproduce this material.
items.abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种新的解决方案，用于处理维护分布式内存键值存储 (IMKVS) 时传统滚动更新带来的服务停机和性能下降问题。作者提出了一种用于 Redis 集群的实时补丁系统，该系统通过直接修改进程内存而无需重新启动节点来主动管理软件更新，同时利用集群内部八卦协议进行自动补丁分发。作者提出了一组实验，比较了实时修补 (LP) 与最先进的滚动更新 (CP) 在一系列集群大小和高达 200~GiB 的内存状态上的功效。我们发现，在指定的硬件（包括 Intel Xeon 和 AMD EPYC 配置）上，我们能够重现论文中显示的关键结果和图表，说明这些实验的稳健性，并确认实时修补可以实现恒定时间更新，无论数据大小如何。此外，作者在 QEMU 虚拟机环境中提供了编写良好的 shell 脚本和易于遵循的指令来重现此材料。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'CRDV: Conflict-free Replicated Data Views'.
items.abstract: This report presents the reproducibility review of the paper ''CRDV: Conflict-free Replicated Data Views''. The paper's artifacts were available and well-documented. The authors provided Docker deployment with all scripts and plotting routines, which made reproducibility straightforward. We successfully reproduced the core claims of the paper and documented any deviations in this report.
items.abstract_zh: 本报告介绍了论文“CRDV：无冲突复制数据视图”的可重复性审查。这篇论文的成果是可用的并且有详细的记录。作者为 Docker 部署提供了所有脚本和绘图例程，这使得可重复性变得简单。我们成功地重现了该论文的核心主张，并在本报告中记录了任何偏差。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'HotStuff-1: Linear Consensus with One-Phase Speculation'.
items.abstract: We reproduce the experimental results reported in the original paper. The authors provided their original implementation and scripts that (i) execute all experiments from the evaluation section and collect the relevant metrics, and (ii) generate the plots reported in the paper. Our evaluation confirms that the main experimental findings of the paper are reproducible.
items.abstract_zh: 我们重现了原始论文中报告的实验结果。作者提供了他们的原始实现和脚本，（i）执行评估部分的所有实验并收集相关指标，以及（ii）生成论文中报告的图。我们的评估证实该论文的主要实验结果是可重复的。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'A Structured Study of Multivariate Time-Series Distance Measures'.
items.abstract: This reproducibility report provides AE details for the paper ''A Structured Study of Multivariate Time-Series Distance Measures'', which is accepted at the SIGMOD 2025 conference. In all, the code provided by the authors is well-arranged, and the main results are reproduced as expected.
items.abstract_zh: 该再现性报告提供了论文“多元时间序列距离测量的结构化研究”的 AE 详细信息，该论文已在 SIGMOD 2025 会议上被接受。总而言之，作者提供的代码排列良好，主要结果按照预期重现。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Zombie Hashing: Reanimating Tombstones in Graveyard'.
items.abstract: The paper introduces Zombie hashing, a novel scheme that deamortizes tombstone redistribution across hash table operations. The authors present a set of experiments to evaluate the proposed hash table approach on space-efficiency, throughput, and latency distribution. Our reproducibility evaluation confirms that the paper's key findings can be reliably reproduced and are supported by the reproduced results in this report.
items.abstract_zh: 本文介绍了僵尸哈希，这是一种新颖的方案，可以跨哈希表操作对墓碑重新分配进行去分摊。作者提出了一组实验来评估所提出的哈希表方法的空间效率、吞吐量和延迟分布。我们的再现性评估证实，该论文的主要发现可以可靠地再现，并得到本报告再现结果的支持。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'BCviz: A Linear-Space Index for Mining and Visualizing Cohesive Bipartite Subgraphs'.
items.abstract: This report presents the successful reproducibility assessment of the SIGMOD 2025 paper ''BCviz: A Linear-Space Index for Mining and Visualizing Cohesive Bipartite Subgraphs''. The paper introduces BCviz, a linear-space, data-driven index designed to reveal the structure of cohesive subgraphs and accelerate maximum biclique search. The evaluation successfully reproduces key results, including index visualization, construction time, search performance, and scalability analysis. The runtime results obtained on the reviewer's testbed matched (and in some cases exceeded) those reported in the paper. Suggestions for readability and code decomposition are provided.
items.abstract_zh: 本报告介绍了 SIGMOD 2025 论文“BCviz：用于挖掘和可视化内聚二分子图的线性空间索引”的成功再现性评估。本文介绍了 BCviz，这是一种线性空间、数据驱动的索引，旨在揭示内聚子图的结构并加速最大 Biclique 搜索。评估成功地重现了关键结果，包括索引可视化、构建时间、搜索性能和可扩展性分析。在审稿人的测试平台上获得的运行时结果与论文中报告的结果相匹配（在某些情况下甚至超过了）。提供了可读性和代码分解的建议。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'T3: Accurate and Fast Performance Prediction for Relational Database Systems With Compiled Decision Trees'.
items.abstract: This report evaluates the availability and reproducibility of the paper entitled, ''T3: Accurate and Fast Performance Prediction for Relational Database Systems With Compiled Decision Trees.'' The report outlines the submitted artifact and details the reproduction process. As the reproduced results were consistent with those of the original paper, the artifact is deemed eligible for both the Availability and Reproducibility badges.
items.abstract_zh: 本报告评估了题为“T3：使用编译决策树对关系数据库系统进行准确且快速的性能预测”的论文的可用性和可重复性。该报告概述了提交的工件并详细介绍了重复过程。由于再现的结果与原始论文的结果一致，因此该工件被视为有资格获得可用性和再现性徽章。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Dangers of List Processing in Querying Property Graphs'.
items.abstract: The core thesis of this paper could easily be reproduced in the original environment. The authors provided an almost fully automated set of scripts that automatically execute the experiments. The experimental data was automatically collected and plotted. The resulting figures have some minor deviations from the originals, but the overall trends remain the same, and hence they can be considered a good fit.
items.abstract_zh: 本文的核心论文可以很容易地在原始环境中复制。作者提供了一组几乎完全自动化的脚本来自动执行实验。自动收集并绘制实验数据。所得数据与原始数据有一些微小偏差，但总体趋势保持不变，因此可以认为它们是很好的拟合。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Clementi: Efficient Load Balancing and Communication Overlap for Multi-FPGA Graph Processing'.
items.abstract: This reproducibility study evaluates the Clementi multi-FPGA graph processing framework using the authors' preconfigured cluster environment. By following the provided reproducibility guide and execution scripts, I was able to verify the correctness of the system's core components and confirm that the produced outputs were consistent with the expected results. Due to the specialized hardware requirements, only functional correctness was reproduced. Overall, the key claims of the paper were validated within the constraints of the available environment.
items.abstract_zh: 这项可重复性研究使用作者预先配置的集群环境评估 Clementi 多 FPGA 图形处理框架。通过遵循提供的重现性指南和执行脚本，我能够验证系统核心组件的正确性，并确认生成的输出与预期结果一致。由于特殊的硬件要求，仅再现了功能正确性。总体而言，本文的关键主张在可用环境的限制内得到了验证。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Parallel k-Core Decomposition: Theory and Practice'.
items.abstract: This report evaluates the reproducibility of the paper ''Parallel k -Core Decomposition: Theory and Practice'' by Liu et al. The authors provide a public GitHub repository and an artifact README describing a single script that runs all k -core algorithms and baselines and aggregates results into one CSV. The paper's core performance claims were reproduced, with minor variations on a few datasets.
items.abstract_zh: 本报告评估了 Liu 等人的论文“并行 k 核心分解：理论与实践”的可重复性。作者提供了一个公共 GitHub 存储库和一个工件自述文件，描述了运行所有 k 核算法和基线并将结果聚合到一个 CSV 中的单个脚本。该论文的核心性能主张被复制，但在一些数据集上略有变化。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'An Elephant Under the Microscope: Analyzing the Interaction of Optimizer Components in PostgreSQL'.
items.abstract: This document reports our experience in reproducing the experiments from the paper ''An Elephant Under the Microscope: Analyzing the Interaction of Optimizer Components in PostgreSQL''[1], published at SIGMOD 2025. The paper investigates the intricate interactions among key components of classic SQL query optimizers: the candidate plan enumerator, the table statistics, and the plan cost estimator. This investigation is based on empirical benchmarks conducted on a real-world database system, PostgreSQL. Although optimizer-centric benchmarks are often difficult to reproduce due to long execution times, high sensitivity to the execution environment, and the sometimes unpredictable behavior of production-grade optimizers, we found that the authors of [1] did an excellent job at designing reproducible experiments. While this report does not cover the full set of experiments presented in [1], we believe that the results obtained here are sufficient to validate the paper's core thesis.
items.abstract_zh: 本文档报告了我们在 SIGMOD 2025 上发表的论文“显微镜下的大象：分析 PostgreSQL 中优化器组件的交互”[1] 中重现实验的经验。该论文研究了经典 SQL 查询优化器的关键组件之间复杂的交互：候选计划枚举器、表统计信息和计划成本估计器。这项调查基于在现实世界的数据库系统 PostgreSQL 上进行的实证基准。尽管由于执行时间长、对执行环境高度敏感以及生产级优化器有时不可预测的行为，以优化器为中心的基准测试通常很难重现，但我们发现[1]的作者在设计可重现的实验方面做得非常出色。虽然本报告没有涵盖[1]中提出的全套实验，但我们相信这里获得的结果足以验证论文的核心论文。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Fast and Scalable Data Transfer Across Data Systems'.
items.abstract: The submission provides detailed instructions for setting up the required system and running the experiments using the supplied scripts. While the full experimental evaluation is time-consuming due to the large number of experiments, the documentation enables a systematic execution of the workflows and the regeneration of figures that reproduce the performance trends reported in the paper "Fast and Scalable Data Transfer Across Data Systems".
items.abstract_zh: 提交内容提供了设置所需系统并使用提供的脚本运行实验的详细说明。虽然由于大量实验，完整的实验评估非常耗时，但文档可以系统地执行工作流程和重新生成图形，从而重现“跨数据系统的快速且可扩展的数据传输”论文中报告的性能趋势。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'PilotDB: Database-Agnostic Online Approximate Query Processing with A Priori Error Guarantees'.
items.abstract: This is a reproducibility report for the paper ''PilotDB: Database-Agnostic Online Approximate Query Processing with A Priori Error Guarantees'' published in SIGMOD 2025. The paper proposes PilotDB, an online approximate query processing (AQP) system that provides a priori error guarantees without requiring database system modification or incurring maintenance overhead. The main evaluation results of the paper can be successfully reproduced. Our findings confirm that PilotDB achieves substantial query performance improvements compared to conventional database systems.
items.abstract_zh: 这是 SIGMOD 2025 上发表的论文“PilotDB：具有先验错误保证的与数据库无关的在线近似查询处理”的可重复性报告。该论文提出了 PilotDB，一种在线近似查询处理（AQP）系统，可以提供先验错误保证，无需数据库系统修改或产生维护开销。论文的主要评价结果可以成功复现。我们的研究结果证实，与传统数据库系统相比，PilotDB 实现了显着的查询性能改进。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Disco: A Compact Index for LSM-trees'.
items.abstract: This report presents a reproducibility study of Disco, a compact multi-run index designed to improve read performance in LSM-tree-based key-value stores. Disco introduces compact key representations to enable both point and range queries with at most one I/O to underlying runs, achieving efficiency close to B+-trees. We reproduce the main experimental results reported in the original paper, including micro-benchmark evaluations and end-to-end performance comparisons against RocksDB and REMIX. Our reproduced results match closely the trends and performance gains claimed by the authors, demonstrating the robustness of the Disco design. The provided implementation and experimental scripts are well-documented, which significantly facilitates reproducibility.
items.abstract_zh: 本报告介绍了 Disco 的可重复性研究，Disco 是一种紧凑的多运行索引，旨在提高基于 LSM 树的键值存储中的读取性能。 Disco 引入了紧凑的键表示，以支持点查询和范围查询，底层运行最多具有 1 个 I/O，实现接近 B+ 树的效率。我们重现了原始论文中报告的主要实验结果，包括微基准评估以及与 RocksDB 和 REMIX 的端到端性能比较。我们重现的结果与作者声称的趋势和性能增益非常匹配，证明了 Disco 设计的稳健性。提供的实现和实验脚本都有详细记录，这极大地促进了可重复性。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'SuSe: Summary Selection for Regular Expression Subsequence Aggregation over Streams'.
items.abstract: The core claims of the SuSe paper are reproducible. The authors provide an accessible implementation of the system along with clear instructions for building and executing the experiments. The repository includes all components necessary to evaluate the StateSummary structure and the summary selection strategy. The datasets referenced in the paper are also publicly available. Our reproduction confirms the reported effectiveness of SuSe in maintaining accurate aggregates under subsequence matching while using significantly fewer resources than baseline approaches.
items.abstract_zh: SuSe 论文的核心主张是可重复的。作者提供了系统的可访问实现以及构建和执行实验的明确说明。该存储库包括评估 StateSummary 结构和摘要选择策略所需的所有组件。论文中引用的数据集也是公开的。我们的复制证实了 SuSe 在子序列匹配下保持准确聚合的有效性，同时使用比基线方法少得多的资源。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Approximating Opaque Top-k Queries'.
items.abstract: This report documents the reproducibility review of the paper ''Approximating Opaque Top-k Queries'' by Jiwon Chang and Fatemeh Nargesian. We successfully reproduced the experimental results for the synthetic and tabular datasets (Figures 4, 5, and 6), which support the core claims regarding the efficiency and accuracy of the proposed bandit algorithm. The authors provided a well-structured repository and cooperated to resolve minor script issues during the review process.
items.abstract_zh: 本报告记录了 Jiwon Chang 和 Fatemeh Nargesian 的论文“近似不透明 Top-k 查询”的可重复性审查。我们成功地重现了合成数据集和表格数据集的实验结果（图 4、5 和 6），这些结果支持了有关所提出的 bandit 算法的效率和准确性的核心主张。作者提供了一个结构良好的存储库，并在审查过程中合作解决了较小的脚本问题。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'RWalks: RandomWalks as Attribute Diffusers for Filtered Vector Search'.
items.abstract: The reproducibility evaluation successfully replicated key functionalities of RWalks: Random Walks as Attribute Diffusers for Filtered Vector Search. The authors provided documentation of the software and hardware requirements, along with well-structured scripts and datasets hosted in an organized GitHub repository. This reproducibility report compares the original results with the reproduced findings, both of which show similar trends, thereby supporting the authors' claims.
items.abstract_zh: 再现性评估成功复制了 RWalks 的关键功能：随机游走作为过滤矢量搜索的属性扩散器。作者提供了软件和硬件要求的文档，以及托管在有组织的 GitHub 存储库中的结构良好的脚本和数据集。这份再现性报告将原始结果与再现的结果进行了比较，两者都显示出相似的趋势，从而支持了作者的主张。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Deep Overlapping Community Search via Subspace Embedding'.
items.abstract: The paper introduces and evaluates Simplified Multi-hop Attention Networks. The experiments included in the paper are reproducible. Our reproduced figures showcase similar trends and relationships between the alternatives compared and support the major findings of the evaluation. We noted variations in the performance and quality of training and predictive measurements of some experiments, which can be attributed to hardware differences.
items.abstract_zh: 本文介绍并评估了简化多跳注意力网络。论文中包含的实验是可重复的。我们复制的数据展示了比较的替代方案之间的相似趋势和关系，并支持了评估的主要发现。我们注意到一些实验的训练和预测测量的性能和质量存在差异，这可以归因于硬件差异。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'SPARTAN: Data-Adaptive Symbolic Time-Series Approximation'.
items.abstract: We were able to reproduce the findings presented in the original paper. Our experimental results and key performance findings align with those presented by the authors. The setup of the environment was minimal and easy to follow. The authors provided a detailed description of the data acquisition, along with instructions for the experimental setup, running the scripts, and generating the plots presented in the paper.
items.abstract_zh: 我们能够重现原始论文中提出的发现。我们的实验结果和关键性能发现与作者提出的结果一致。环境的设置非常简单且易于遵循。作者提供了数据采集的详细描述，以及实验设置、运行脚本和生成论文中的绘图的说明。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Self-Enhancing Video Data Management System for Compositional Events with Large Language Models'.
items.abstract: This report presents a reproducibility evaluation of the VOCAL-UDF paper [1] from SIGMOD 2025, which introduces a video data management system that answers complex compositional queries over videos. Using the author-provided software artifact and cached outputs for LLM-based components, we successfully reproduced all main figures and tables supporting the paper's core claims.
items.abstract_zh: 本报告对 SIGMOD 2025 的 VOCAL-UDF 论文 [1] 进行了可重复性评估，该论文引入了一种视频数据管理系统，可以回答视频中复杂的构图查询。使用作者提供的软件工件和基于 LLM 的组件的缓存输出，我们成功地重现了支持论文核心主张的所有主要数字和表格。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Mnemosyne: Dynamic Workload-Aware BF Tuning via Accurate Statistics in LSM trees'.
items.abstract: This report presents a reproducibility evaluation of the Mnemosyne paper [1] from SIGMOD 2025, which proposes a framework to allocate Bloom filter memory at a per-file granularity in evolving LSM trees. The artifact enables near one-command reproduction of all the paper's experiments and figures. We successfully reproduced all key empirical findings of the paper using a different hardware platform from the original study.
items.abstract_zh: 本报告对 SIGMOD 2025 的 Mnemosyne 论文 [1] 进行了可重复性评估，该论文提出了一个框架，用于在不断发展的 LSM 树中以每个文件的粒度分配布隆过滤器内存。该工件几乎可以通过一个命令再现论文中的所有实验和数据。我们使用与原始研究不同的硬件平台成功地重现了论文的所有关键实证结果。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Alsatian: Optimizing Model Search for Deep Transfer Learning'.
items.abstract: This is a reproduction evaluation of ''Alsatian: Optimizing Model Search for Deep Transfer Learning'', which observed the overlapping of models during model searching and optimizes it with a novel solution that utilizes caches and excellent searching algorithms. The authors provided detailed descriptions for setting up the required environment through Docker containers, as well as scripts to perform experiments for each figure in paper. Thanks to the authors' help, we were able to perform the reproduction evaluation using the same environment as described in the paper. We succeeded in reproducing the results that can serve as a certificate of the result authenticity.
items.abstract_zh: 这是对“Alsatian：优化深度迁移学习的模型搜索”的再现评估，它观察了模型搜索过程中模型的重叠，并通过利用缓存和优秀搜索算法的新颖解决方案对其进行了优化。作者在论文中提供了通过 Docker 容器设置所需环境的详细描述，以及对每个图进行实验的脚本。感谢作者的帮助，我们能够使用论文中描述的相同环境进行再现评估。我们成功地复制了结果，可以作为结果真实性的证明。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'InTime: Towards Performance Predictability In Byzantine Fault Tolerant Proof-of-Stake Consensus'.
items.abstract: We present the availability and reproducibility report of the ACM SIGMOD 2025 paper titled ''InTime: Towards Performance Predictability In Byzantine Fault Tolerant Proof-of-Stake Consensus''. Following the instructions provided the authors, we evaluated the artifacts hosted on Github. The reviewers confirmed that the codebase is functional and evaluatable. Reproducibility was straightforward as the authors provided automated scripts that simplified the execution of experiments. Experiments on single machine were reproduced while experiments requiring a large cluster of machines are constrained by limited resource capacity and instructions. Hence, the paper's core claims were partially reproduced.
items.abstract_zh: 我们提出了 ACM SIGMOD 2025 论文的可用性和可重复性报告，题为“InTime：在拜占庭容错权益证明共识中实现性能可预测性”。按照作者提供的说明，我们评估了 Github 上托管的工件。审阅者确认代码库功能齐全且可评估。重现性很简单，因为作者提供了简化实验执行的自动化脚本。在单台机器上进行的实验是重复的，而需要大量机器的实验则受到有限的资源容量和指令的限制。因此，该论文的核心主张被部分转载。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'Are Database System Researchers Making Correct Assumptions about Transaction Workloads?'.
items.abstract: This reproducibility report evaluates the artifact associated with the SIGMOD 2025 paper ''Are Database System Researchers Making Correct Assumptions about Transaction Workloads?''. We successfully reproduced the core data analysis pipeline using the authors' provided VS Code extension (splinter), ORM-specific analyzers, and Jupyter notebooks. The semi-automated tool accurately categorized ORM operations across multiple open-source applications, generating JSON results that match those in the authors' data.zip archive. Visualizations produced from the provided notebook closely replicate the figures in the original paper. Our findings confirm that the experimental methodology is well-documented, executable, and yields consistent results across environments. We conclude that the paper meets SIGMOD's reproducibility standards.
items.abstract_zh: 这份可重复性报告评估了与 SIGMOD 2025 论文“数据库系统研究人员对事务工作负载做出正确假设吗？”相关的工件。我们使用作者提供的 VS Code 扩展（splinter）、特定于 ORM 的分析器和 Jupyter Notebook 成功地重现了核心数据分析管道。该半自动化工具准确地对多个开源应用程序中的 ORM 操作进行了分类，生成与作者的 data.zip 存档中的结果相匹配的 JSON 结果。从所提供的笔记本中生成的可视化结果与原始论文中的数字非常接近。我们的研究结果证实，实验方法有详细记录、可执行，并且在不同环境中产生一致的结果。我们的结论是这篇论文符合 SIGMOD 的可重复性标准。

items.title: Reproducibility Report for ACM SIGMOD 2025 Paper: 'A Lovász-Simonovits Theorem for Hypergraphs with Application to Local Clustering'.
items.abstract: In this report, we provide an overview of the reproducibility of the ACM SIGMOD 2025 paper ''\papertitle''. The authors provided a project on GitHub and Google Colab where all the environments, code, and scripts were set up. It is easy to regenerate the results. The output aligns with the authors' presentation in the paper.
items.abstract_zh: 在本报告中，我们概述了 ACM SIGMOD 2025 论文“\papertitle”的可重复性。作者在 GitHub 和 Google Colab 上提供了一个项目，其中设置了所有环境、代码和脚本。重新生成结果很容易。输出与作者在论文中的介绍一致。

items.title: Reproducibility Reports of the 2025 International Conference on Management of Data, SIGMOD ARI Reports 2025, Berlin, Germany, June 22-27, 2025
items.abstract:
This is the second SIGMOD ARI Companion with Reproducibility Reports for SIGMOD papers that are fully reproduced following the ACM SIGMOD Availability and Reproducibility Initiative (ARI) process as outlined at https://reproducibility.sigmod.org/. The ACM SIGMOD 2025 ARI requested from the authors of all papers presented in ACM SIGMOD 2025 to submit their artifacts for evaluation. The evaluation process may have a few different outcomes. All papers for which the artifacts are made available received the ''Artifacts Available'' badge. Further, when the artifacts are found to be exercisable and well-documented, the corresponding papers received the ''Artifacts Evaluated'' badge. Finally, when the artifacts led to a full reproducibility of the presented results, the papers received the ''Results Reproduced'' badge. More details on the ACM badging and the requirements of each badge can be found on the official ACM website: https://www.acm.org/publications/policies/artifact-review-and-badging-current.

From a total of 250 paper presented in SIGMOD 2025, we received 107 artifact submissions, 57 of targeting the ''Artifacts Available'' and ''Artifacts Evaluated'' and 50 targeting the ''Results Reproduced'' badge as well. We highlight that the overall ARI submission rate has increased to 43% in 2025 from 33% in 2024 (and 25% in 2023), showing a very encouraging shift in culture towards sharing artifacts and ensuring ease of adoption and cross-pollination.

After a thorough reproducibility review, 35 papers were found fully reproducible and the reviewers compiled reproducibility reports for each of those papers. The reproducibility reports were compiled by the reviewers, where the authors could optionally contribute. This process has led to new synergies in the community while also improving the final version of the artifacts in terms of presentation, ease of deployment, or even minor errors on occasion.

Last but not least, we highlight that not finding a paper fully reproduced is not a negative result, rather, it is a ''not yet reproduced'' result and the authors may decide to submit their artifacts for reproducibility review in a subsequent round.
items.abstract_zh:
这是第二个带有 SIGMOD 论文再现性报告的 SIGMOD ARI 配套文件，按照 https://reproducibility.sigmod.org/ 概述的 ACM SIGMOD 可用性和再现性计划 (ARI) 流程进行了完全再现。 ACM SIGMOD 2025 ARI 要求 ACM SIGMOD 2025 中发表的所有论文的作者提交他们的工件以供评估。评估过程可能会产生一些不同的结果。所有提供文物的论文都获得了“文物可用”徽章。此外，当发现这些工件可以使用并且有详细记录时，相应的论文就会获得“工件评估”徽章。最后，当这些工件能够完全再现所呈现的结果时，论文将获得“结果再现”徽章。有关 ACM 徽章以及每个徽章的要求的更多详细信息，请访问 ACM 官方网站：https://www.acm.org/publications/policies/artifact-review-and-badging-current。

从 SIGMOD 2025 上提交的总共 250 篇论文中，我们收到了 107 份工件提交，其中 57 份针对“可用工件”和“评估工件”，还有 50 份针对“结果再现”徽章。我们强调，ARI 的总体提交率已从 2024 年的 33%（2023 年的 25%）增加到 2025 年的 43%，这表明文化向共享工件和确保易于采用和异花授粉的方向发生了非常令人鼓舞的转变。

经过彻底的再现性审查后，发现 35 篇论文完全可再现，审稿人为每篇论文编制了再现性报告。重现性报告由审稿人编制，作者可以选择贡献。这一过程在社区中带来了新的协同效应，同时还改进了工件的最终版本，包括在演示、易于部署甚至偶尔出现的小错误方面。

最后但并非最不重要的一点是，我们强调，没有找到一篇完全被复制的论文并不是一个负面结果，而是一个“尚未复制”的结果，作者可能会决定在下一轮中提交他们的作品进行可复制性审查。

items.title: Authenticating Multi-Chain Queries: Verifiable Virtual Filesystem Is All You Need.
items.abstract: This demonstration showcases a verifiable multi-chain querying system. The proliferation of blockchain-driven applications has created a demand for on-chain data analysis across multiple blockchains. However, existing solutions face challenges in seamlessly integrating with existing blockchains, maintaining compatible with various database engines, and ensuring the integrity of query results. In response, we develop a blockchain indexing system that utilizes a novel verifiable virtual filesystem (V 2 FS) for query authentication. Our demonstration focuses on highlighting the key features of our system, including blockchain data indexing, verifiable multi-chain query processing, and the usability and performance of our solution. Additionally, we provide an interactive visualization module to enhance attendees' understanding and insights.
items.abstract_zh: 该演示展示了一个可验证的多链查询系统。区块链驱动的应用程序的激增产生了对跨多个区块链的链上数据分析的需求。然而，现有的解决方案在与现有区块链无缝集成、保持与各种数据库引擎的兼容以及确保查询结果的完整性方面面临挑战。为此，我们开发了一种区块链索引系统，该系统利用新颖的可验证虚拟文件系统（V 2 FS）进行查询身份验证。我们的演示重点突出我们系统的关键功能，包括区块链数据索引、可验证的多链查询处理以及我们解决方案的可用性和性能。此外，我们还提供交互式可视化模块来增强与会者的理解和见解。

items.title: Where Does Academic Database Research Go From Here?
items.abstract: An open forum to discuss and debate the future of database research in the context of industry, other research communities, and AI.
items.abstract_zh: 一个开放的论坛，讨论和辩论工业、其他研究社区和人工智能背景下数据库研究的未来。

items.title: Reproducible Prototyping of Query Optimizer Components.
items.abstract: Query optimization was, is, and will for the foreseeable future be a very active research field with novel concepts being introduced each year. Over the last decade, two key trends have emerged in this domain: First, many researchers apply machine learning methods to replace components of the traditional optimizer architecture with learned models. Second, PostgreSQL has risen to the standard database system -- either as a benchmarking baseline, or as a target platform for implementation. However, most novel concepts are integrated directly into specific versions of PostgreSQL. As a result, the research community has produced a diverse ''zoo'' of optimization concepts that are difficult to compare. Implementations are often incompatible, rely on different PostgreSQL versions, lack clear a documentation of changes in the base system, or depend on custom tooling. To overcome this situation, this tutorial makes the following contributions: (i) we summarize important characteristics of the PostgreSQL optimizer architecture, emphasizing opportunities for extension, (ii) we survey recent research that improves the traditional optimizer architecture with a special focus on implementation and evaluation, and (iii) in a hands-on part, we showcase how proposed concepts can be expressed in a novel optimization framework to enable a common-ground comparison and rapid prototyping. Finally, we hope to raise the participants' awareness for the importance of reproducible and openly accessible prototypes, as well as for the importance of fair and transparent benchmarks.
items.abstract_zh: 查询优化过去、现在和在可预见的未来都将是一个非常活跃的研究领域，每年都会推出新的概念。在过去的十年中，该领域出现了两个主要趋势：首先，许多研究人员应用机器学习方法，用学习模型取代传统优化器架构的组件。其次，PostgreSQL 已经上升为标准数据库系统——要么作为基准测试基线，要么作为实施的目标平台。然而，大多数新颖的概念都直接集成到 PostgreSQL 的特定版本中。结果，研究界产生了难以比较的多样化优化概念“动物园”。实现通常不兼容，依赖于不同的 PostgreSQL 版本，缺乏基本系统中更改的清晰文档，或者依赖于自定义工具。为了克服这种情况，本教程做出了以下贡献：（i）我们总结了 PostgreSQL 优化器架构的重要特征，强调扩展的机会，（ii）我们调查了改进传统优化器架构的最新研究，特别关注实现和评估，以及（iii）在实践部分，我们展示了如何在新颖的优化框架中表达所提出的概念，以实现共同点比较和快速原型设计。最后，我们希望提高参与者对可复制和公开访问原型的重要性以及公平和透明基准的重要性的认识。

items.title: Learned Indexes From the One-dimensional to the Multi-dimensional Spaces: Challenges, Techniques, and Opportunities.
items.abstract: Recently, the class of learned index structures has emerged as one form of database indexes that make use of Machine Learning (ML) techniques. The learned indexes designed for the one-dimensional space have demonstrated improvements in both the query processing time and the index size. Observing the advantages of one-dimensional learned indexes, various learned indexes have been proposed for the multi-dimensional space. This class of learned indexes is termed ''Learned Multi-dimensional Indexes.'' This tutorial on learned indexes is designed based on our long survey article on the subject [4]. In this tutorial, we use a taxonomy to categorize over 100 learned one- and multi-dimensional indexes with more focus on the class of learned multi-dimensional indexes. The goal of this tutorial is to explain the fundamental techniques behind state-of-the-art learned one- and multi-dimensional indexes with emphasis on the latter, and identify the ongoing challenges and future opportunities for research in this domain.
items.abstract_zh: 最近，学习索引结构类已经作为利用机器学习 (ML) 技术的数据库索引的一种形式出现。为一维空间设计的学习索引在查询处理时间和索引大小方面都得到了改进。观察一维学习索引的优点，针对多维空间提出了各种学习索引。此类学习索引称为“学习多维索引”。本关于学习索引的教程是根据我们关于该主题的长篇调查文章设计的 [4]。在本教程中，我们使用分类法对 100 多个学习的一维和多维索引进行分类，更关注学习的多维索引的类别。本教程的目标是解释最先进的学习一维和多维索引背后的基本技术，重点是后者，并确定该领域研究中持续的挑战和未来的机遇。

items.title: LpBound in Action: Cardinality Estimation with One-Sided Guarantees.
items.abstract: We demonstrate LpBound , a cardinality estimator that computes guaranteed upper bounds on the output size of a given query. Among the wealth of traditional, learned, and pessimistic estimators, LpBound 's uniqueness lies in its use of two key ingredients: (1) data statistics based on ℓ p -norms of degree sequences of the join columns, and (2) a linear program formulation of the cardinality estimation problem, whose constraints are the Shannon inequalities and new information inequalities derived from data statistics. LpBound comes with a visual interface accessible in the browser. The users can interact with the interface by choosing a query from the JOB, STATS, and Subgraph Matching benchmarks and the range of ℓ p -norms available to LpBound . Within a few milliseconds, LpBound computes an upper bound on the query output size. This bound is explained by a closed-form formula using the available ℓ p -norms. The users can also inspect the estimation errors of LpBound and a variety of other estimators.
items.abstract_zh: 我们演示了 LpBound，一个基数估计器，用于计算给定查询的输出大小的保证上限。在众多传统的、学习的和悲观的估计器中，LpBound 的独特之处在于它使用了两个关键成分：（1）基于连接列的度序列的 ℓ p 范数的数据统计，以及（2）基数估计问题的线性程序公式，其约束是香农不等式和从数据统计导出的新信息不等式。 LpBound 带有可在浏览器中访问的可视化界面。用户可以通过从 JOB、STATS 和子图匹配基准以及 LpBound 可用的 ℓ p 范数范围中选择查询来与界面进行交互。在几毫秒内，LpBound 计算查询输出大小的上限。该界限由使用可用 ℓ p 范数的封闭式公式来解释。用户还可以检查LpBound和各种其他估计器的估计误差。

items.title: TEQ: An Open and Developer-friendly Testbed for Edge-based Query Processing Algorithms.
items.abstract: Edge computing is ideally suited for querying populations of fast data streams. However, developing and evaluating edge-based query processing algorithms is challenging due to the decentralized setting and the lack of effective tools. For example, current simulators fail to execute real queries. To rectify these shortcomings, we propose TeQ, a <u>t</u>estbed for <u>e</u>dge-based <u>q</u>uery processing algorithms, designed expressly to be open, enabling extensible simulation and algorithm deployment, and to be developer-friendly, simplifying otherwise cumbersome simulation, complex algorithm management, and tedious evaluation. TeQ encompasses novel techniques to achieve these capabilities, including (1) a streamlined framework for implementing decentralized algorithms as modular, Docker-based executables; (2) data and query playback controls to enable reproducible and consistent evaluations; and (3) mechanisms for transforming runtime metrics into system-wide metrics desired by algorithm developers.
items.abstract_zh: 边缘计算非常适合查询快速数据流群体。然而，由于分散的设置和缺乏有效的工具，开发和评估基于边缘的查询处理算法具有挑战性。例如，当前的模拟器无法执行真实的查询。为了纠正这些缺点，我们提出了 TeQ，这是一个基于边缘查询处理算法的测试平台，其明确设计为开放的，支持可扩展的仿真和算法部署，并且对开发人员友好，简化了繁琐的仿真、复杂的算法管理和繁琐的评估。 TeQ 包含实现这些功能的新技术，包括 (1) 一个简化的框架，用于将去中心化算法实现为模块化、基于 Docker 的可执行文件； (2) 数据和查询回放控制，以实现可重复且一致的评估； (3) 将运行时指标转换为算法开发人员所需的系统范围指标的机制。

items.title: Flux: Unifying Heterogeneous Infrastructure for Alibaba AnalyticDB.
items.abstract: The growing complexity and variety of workloads in cloud data warehouses encompass both traditional analytical workloads and emerging AI-related workloads. And this diversity has highlighted the limitations of traditional CPU-based systems in processing heterogeneous workloads. Hardware acceleration, including Field-Programmable Gate Arrays (FPGAs) and Graphics Processing Units (GPUs), improves computational efficiency. However, the high total cost of ownership (TCO) hinders widespread adoption of the acceleration hardware in cloud data warehouses. Addressing the challenge, we introduce Flux, a unified cluster management system on heterogeneous infrastructure for Alibaba AnalyticDB. Our work begins by defining an acceleration priority score that evaluates the cost saving and acceleration ratios for different workloads. By employing this metric, we design a priority-based workload manager and a unified resource scheduler that optimizes the performance and TCOs for both the users and cloud service providers. In the end, we present empirical evaluations conducted on real-world AnalyticDB instances, demonstrating an average 4.39x acceleration, a 21.9% reduction in user TCO, and a 47% decrease in cloud service providers' TCO.
items.abstract_zh: 云数据仓库中工作负载的复杂性和多样性不断增加，既包括传统的分析工作负载，也包括新兴的人工智能相关工作负载。而这种多样性凸显了传统的基于CPU的系统在处理异构工作负载方面的局限性。硬件加速，包括现场可编程门阵列 (FPGA) 和图形处理单元 (GPU)，可提高计算效率。然而，高昂的总体拥有成本（TCO）阻碍了加速硬件在云数据仓库中的广泛采用。为了应对这一挑战，我们推出了 Flux，这是阿里巴巴 AnalyticDB 异构基础设施上的统一集群管理系统。我们的工作首先定义加速优先级分数，评估不同工作负载的成本节省和加速比。通过采用这个指标，我们设计了一个基于优先级的工作负载管理器和一个统一的资源调度程序，可以优化用户和云服务提供商的性能和 TCO。最后，我们对真实的 AnalyticDB 实例进行了实证评估，结果显示平均加速为 4.39 倍，用户 TCO 降低 21.9%，云服务提供商 TCO 降低 47%。

items.title: Demonstrating PDSP-Bench: A Benchmarking System for Parallel and Distributed Stream Processing.
items.abstract: Parallel stream processing plays a crucial role in efficiently handling massive workloads for modern data-driven applications. However, understanding the performance implications of operator parallelism in distributed environments remains a challenging task. Existing benchmarking systems primarily analyze Stream Processing Systems (SPS) using sequential operator pipelines in homogeneous, centralized environments, limiting their applicability to real-world distributed scenarios. In this demo paper, we demonstrate PDSPBench, a benchmarking system designed to systematically evaluate and understand the performance of parallel stream processing in distributed and heterogeneous environments. PDSP-B ench enables the evaluation of how SPS, such as Apache Flink and Apache Storm, leverage operator parallelism and available resources to process diverse workloads. Our benchmark includes parallel query structures derived from 15 real-world applications and 9 synthetic queries, allowing users to explore the impact of parallelism on performance by varying query parameters, data stream characteristics, and parallelism enumeration strategies, including different configurations and resource allocations in cloud-cluster environments. Our demo allows users to experience performance benchmarking of SPS using PDSP-B ench by providing interactive visualizations and performance insights of parallelism on query execution in heterogeneous resource environments.
items.abstract_zh: 并行流处理在有效处理现代数据驱动应用程序的大量工作负载方面发挥着至关重要的作用。然而，理解分布式环境中运算符并行性的性能影响仍然是一项具有挑战性的任务。现有的基准测试系统主要在同质、集中式环境中使用顺序运算符管道来分析流处理系统（SPS），限制了它们对现实世界分布式场景的适用性。在本演示论文中，我们演示了 PDSPBench，这是一个基准测试系统，旨在系统地评估和了解分布式异构环境中并行流处理的性能。 PDSP-B ench 能够评估 SPS（例如 Apache Flink 和 Apache Storm）如何利用运算符并行性和可用资源来处理不同的工作负载。我们的基准测试包括源自 15 个实际应用程序和 9 个综合查询的并行查询结构，允许用户通过改变查询参数、数据流特征和并行性枚举策略（包括云集群环境中的不同配置和资源分配）来探索并行性对性能的影响。我们的演示通过提供异构资源环境中查询执行并行性的交互式可视化和性能洞察，让用户能够体验使用 PDSP-B ench 的 SPS 性能基准测试。

items.title: Privacy and Security in Distributed Data Markets.
items.abstract: Data markets play a pivotal role in modern industries by facilitating the exchange of data for predictive modeling, targeted marketing, and research. However, as data becomes a valuable commodity, privacy and security concerns have grown, particularly regarding the personal information of individuals. This tutorial explores privacy and security issues when integrating different data sources in data market platforms. As motivation for the importance of enforcing privacy requirements, we discuss attacks on data markets focusing on membership inference and reconstruction attacks. We also discuss security vulnerabilities in decentralized data marketplaces, including adversarial manipulations by buyers or sellers. We provide an overview of privacy and security mechanisms designed to mitigate these risks. In order to enforce the least amount of trust for buyers and sellers, we focus on distributed protocols. Finally, we conclude with opportunities for future research on understanding and mitigating privacy and security concerns in distributed data markets.
items.abstract_zh: 数据市场通过促进预测建模、有针对性的营销和研究的数据交换，在现代工业中发挥着关键作用。然而，随着数据成为一种有价值的商品，隐私和安全问题日益增多，尤其是个人信息。本教程探讨在数据市场平台中集成不同数据源时的隐私和安全问题。作为执行隐私要求重要性的动机，我们讨论了对数据市场的攻击，重点是成员推理和重建攻击。我们还讨论了去中心化数据市场中的安全漏洞，包括买家或卖家的对抗性操纵。我们概述了旨在减轻这些风险的隐私和安全机制。为了增强买家和卖家的最低限度的信任，我们专注于分布式协议。最后，我们总结了未来研究理解和减轻分布式数据市场中的隐私和安全问题的机会。

items.title: ScaleLLM: A Technique for Scalable LLM-augmented Data Systems.
items.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) offer powerful semantic insights for data analytics, but row-by-row LLM calls quickly become prohibitively expensive in large datasets. We introduce ScaleLLM, a novel system that substantially reduces both latency and cost on text classification tasks. ScaleLLM couples LLM-generated labels on a small subset of data with a lightweight machine learning model for large-scale inference. This approach provides significant speed-ups-up to 37×-while maintaining accuracy close to that of a full LLM baseline, converging within 1% of its accuracy in several tasks. ScaleLLM also provides cost-accuracy trade-off projections, giving users fine-grained control over performance trade-offs. Our demonstration illustrates ScaleLLM's reusable embedding views, efficient inference architecture, and potential for integration with query optimization frameworks in LLM-augmented database systems.
items.abstract_zh: 大型语言模型 (LLM) 为数据分析提供了强大的语义洞察，但在大型数据集中，逐行 LLM 调用很快就会变得极其昂贵。我们推出了 ScaleLLM，这是一种新颖的系统，可以大大减少文本分类任务的延迟和成本。 ScaleLLM 将 LLM 在一小部分数据上生成的标签与轻量级机器学习模型结合起来，以进行大规模推理。这种方法可显着提升高达 37 倍的速度，同时保持接近完整 LLM 基线的精度，在多项任务中收敛到其精度的 1% 以内。 ScaleLLM 还提供成本准确性权衡预测，使用户能够对性能权衡进行细粒度控制。我们的演示展示了 ScaleLLM 的可重用嵌入视图、高效的推理架构以及与 LLM 增强数据库系统中的查询优化框架集成的潜力。

items.title: Catching up with Disorder: Dynamic Graphs with Out-of-Order Updates.
items.abstract:
Several real-time applications rely on dynamic graphs to model and store data arriving from multiple streams, potentially distributed. Providing both high ingestion rate and efficient analytics with transactional guarantees over such dynamic graphs is challenging, even more so when updates may be received out-of-order at the database. We propose to demonstrate HAL [2], a novel in-memory dynamic graph database system, which addresses these challenges. HAL outperforms comparable systems by a factor of up to 73× in terms of update processing throughput and up to 357× for analytics, while being the first to support out-of-order updates.

Demo video: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=S-Qi6Oj68oU
items.abstract_zh:
一些实时应用程序依赖动态图来建模和存储来自多个可能分布的流的数据。在此类动态图上提供高摄取率和高效分析以及事务保证是具有挑战性的，当数据库中可能无序接收更新时更是如此。我们建议展示 HAL [2]，一种新颖的内存动态图数据库系统，它可以解决这些挑战。 HAL 在更新处理吞吐量方面比同类系统高出 73 倍，在分析方面比同类系统高出 357 倍，同时是第一个支持乱序更新的系统。

演示视频：https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=S-Qi6Oj68oU

items.title: Rel: A Programming Language for Relational Data.
items.abstract: Rel is a new relational language whose key design goal is to allow both database querying and programming in the large without relying on the currently dominant paradigm in which a query sublanguage is embedded in a host programming language. With the new approach we can model the semantics of entire enterprise applications relationally, which helps significantly reduce architecture complexity and avoid the well-known impedance mismatch problem. This paradigm shift is enabled by 50 years of database research, making it possible to revisit the sublanguage/host language paradigm, starting from the fundamental principles. We present the main features of Rel: those that give it the power to express traditional query language operations, and those that are designed to grow the language and allow programming in the large.
items.abstract_zh: Rel 是一种新的关系语言，其主要设计目标是允许大规模数据库查询和编程，而不依赖于当前占主导地位的范式（其中查询子语言嵌入到主机编程语言中）。通过新方法，我们可以对整个企业应用程序的语义进行关联建模，这有助于显着降低架构复杂性并避免众所周知的阻抗不匹配问题。这种范式转变是通过 50 年的数据库研究实现的，使得从基本原则开始重新审视子语言/主语言范式成为可能。我们介绍 Rel 的主要功能：那些赋予它表达传统查询语言操作的能力的功能，以及那些旨在扩展语言并允许大规模编程的功能。

items.title: Eighth Joint Workshop on Graph Data Management Experiences & Systems (GRADES) and Network Data Analytics (NDA).
items.abstract: GRADES-NDA 2025 marks the eighth joint meeting of the GRADES and NDA workshops, which have been independently co-located with the SIGMOD/PODS conference since 2013 and 2016, respectively. The workshop provides a dedicated forum for advancing the state of the art in graph data management and network analytics, fostering cross-disciplinary collaboration on recent advances in handling complex, interconnected data at scale. GRADES-NDA brings together researchers and practitioners from diverse backgrounds to explore foundational models, scalable systems, and emerging applications of graph-structured data. The 2025 edition highlights topics such as graph query languages, processing platforms, dynamic and streaming graphs, and graph-based machine learning, with growing attention to intersections with generative AI and privacy-preserving computation.
items.abstract_zh: GRADES-NDA 2025 是 GRADES 和 NDA 研讨会的第八次联席会议，这两个研讨会自 2013 年和 2016 年以来分别与 SIGMOD/PODS 会议独立举办。该研讨会提供了一个专门的论坛，用于推进图形数据管理和网络分析的最新技术，促进跨学科合作，以解决大规模处理复杂、互连数据的最新进展。 GRADES-NDA 汇集了来自不同背景的研究人员和从业者，共同探索图结构数据的基础模型、可扩展系统和新兴应用。 2025 年版本重点介绍了图查询语言、处理平台、动态和流图以及基于图的机器学习等主题，并且越来越关注与生成式 AI 和隐私保护计算的交叉点。

items.title: CausalExplain: Causal Explanations of Black-box Models with Training Data Subsets.
items.abstract: AI models are becoming increasingly opaque, prompting significant efforts to explain their predictions. However, data-based explanations-i.e., explaining predictions using training data points-remain largely underexplored. Such explanations are essential for debugging training data and gaining deeper insights into model behavior. We introduce a demo of CausalExplain, a model-agnostic system designed to trace the predictions of popular machine learning (ML) models back to their corresponding training data points. For any given prediction, CausalExplain provides explanations based on predicates from the training data that caused the outcome. These explanations are derived from the causal DAG of the training data, with CausalExplain presenting the user with the top- k data-centric explanations for a given query row. In this demo, participants will explore how causal DAGs support the generation of such explanations across various datasets and underlying ML models.
items.abstract_zh: 人工智能模型变得越来越不透明，促使人们付出巨大努力来解释他们的预测。然而，基于数据的解释（即使用训练数据点解释预测）在很大程度上仍未得到充分探索。这些解释对于调试训练数据和深入了解模型行为至关重要。我们介绍了 CausalExplain 的演示，这是一个与模型无关的系统，旨在将流行机器学习 (ML) 模型的预测追溯到其相应的训练数据点。对于任何给定的预测，CausalExplain 根据导致结果的训练数据中的谓词提供解释。这些解释源自训练数据的因果 DAG，CausalExplain 向用户提供给定查询行的前 k 个以数据为中心的解释。在此演示中，参与者将探索因果 DAG 如何支持跨各种数据集和底层 ML 模型生成此类解释。

items.title: ProvenanceWeek2025.
items.abstract: ProvenanceWeek2025 built on a successful history of provenance workshops bringing together researchers from a wide range of computer science fields including workflows, semantic web, databases,high performance computing, distributed systems, operating systems, programming languages, and software engineering, as well as researchers from other fields, such as biology and physics that have urgent provenance needs.
items.abstract_zh: ProvenanceWeek2025 建立在起源研讨会的成功历史之上，汇集了来自广泛计算机科学领域的研究人员，包括工作流、语义网络、数据库、高性能计算、分布式系统、操作系统、编程语言和软件工程，以及来自其他领域的研究人员，例如有紧急起源需求的生物学和物理学。

items.title: ACE-in-Action: A Smart DBMS Bufferpool for SSDs.
items.abstract: Solid-State Drives (SSDs) have two key properties: (i) read/write asymmetry, where writes are slower than reads, and (ii) access concurrency, allowing multiple I/O operations in parallel to maximize the bandwidth. However, many applications treat reads and writes equally and do not fully utilize the device concurrency, a behavior observed in traditional database bufferpool managers. To address this, we propose an A symmetry & C oncurrency-Awar e bufferpool manager ( ACE ) that batches writes based on the device's write concurrency and amortizes the high asymmetric write cost by issuing them in parallel. ACE notably improves the application performance (e.g., PostgreSQL) and is easy to integrate since it can work as a wrapper around any existing page replacement policy. In this demonstration, we present a web simulation of the ACE bufferpool manager integrated with three popular page replacement policies (LRU, CFLRU and LRU-WSR). The conference participants can configure the simulation, view the real-time bufferpool animation and statistics, as well as run various experiments to compare the performance of different page replacement policies and their ACE counterparts. The demonstration is available at https://disc-projects.bu.edu/ACE/research.html.
items.abstract_zh: 固态硬盘 (SSD) 具有两个关键属性：(i) 读/写不对称，其中写入速度比读取慢；(ii) 访问并发性，允许并行进行多个 I/O 操作以最大化带宽。然而，许多应用程序平等地对待读取和写入，并且没有充分利用设备并发性，这是传统数据库缓冲池管理器中观察到的行为。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种对称性和流通性感知的缓冲池管理器（ACE），它根据设备的写入并发性对写入进行批量写入，并通过并行发出它们来分摊高非对称写入成本。 ACE 显着提高了应用程序性能（例如 PostgreSQL），并且易于集成，因为它可以作为任何现有页面替换策略的包装器。在此演示中，我们展示了 ACE 缓冲池管理器的 Web 模拟，该管理器与三种流行的页面替换策略（LRU、CFLRU 和 LRU-WSR）集成。会议参与者可以配置模拟、查看实时缓冲池动画和统计数据，以及运行各种实验来比较不同页面替换策略及其 ACE 策略的性能。该演示可在 https://disc-projects.bu.edu/ACE/research.html 上获取。

items.title: MiniClean: A Single-Machine System for Cleaning Big Graphs.
items.abstract: We demonstrate MiniClean, a single-machine system for cleaning large-scale graphs. MiniClean adopts a rule-based approach that unifies logic reasoning and machine learning, and supports rule discovery, error detection and error correction. To cope with repeated graph pattern matching and large intermediate results, MiniClean proposes (1) a pipelined workflow that coordinates CPU, GPU and I/O operations, (2) memory footprint reduction by bundled processing and data compression, and (3) a multi-mode parallel model that combines SIMD, pipelined, and independent parallelism to maximize CPU--GPU synergy. We demonstrate how MiniClean cleans real-life billion-scale graphs and outperforms a SOTA multi-machine system with a 32-node cluster by at least 8.09X.
items.abstract_zh: 我们演示了 MiniClean，一个用于清理大规模图形的单机系统。 MiniClean采用基于规则的方法，将逻辑推理和机器学习相结合，支持规则发现、错误检测和纠错。为了应对重复的图形模式匹配和大量中间结果，MiniClean 提出了 (1) 协调 CPU、GPU 和 I/O 操作的流水线工作流程，(2) 通过捆绑处理和数据压缩减少内存占用，以及 (3) 结合 SIMD、流水线和独立并行性的多模式并行模型，以最大限度地提高 CPU-GPU 协同作用。我们演示了 MiniClean 如何清理现实生活中数十亿级的图形，并且性能比具有 32 节点集群的 SOTA 多机系统至少高出 8.09 倍。

items.title: How DuckDB is USING KEY to Unlock Recursive Query Performance.
items.abstract: SQL's recursive common table expressions (CTEs) can express complex computations over tabular data. Their accumulative semantics---which collects all intermediate results in a union table---may incur substantial space and runtime overhead, however. This paper takes the recently proposed USING KEY variant of recursive CTEs as a starting point and demonstrates it as a production-ready feature in D uck DB. This CTE variant admits queries to selectively ''overwrite'' prior intermediate results, which ultimately leads to substantially smaller union tables and runtime savings. We present the changes we made to D uck DB to support this new CTE form and showcase the performance of the USING KEY variant using the LDBC graph instances. The demonstration features a fully functional implementation of USING KEY in a D uck DB instance, pre-loaded with LDBC graphs, and a large set of queries that demonstrate the benefits. The demonstration will be interactive, allowing attendees to play with sample SQL queries and data.
items.abstract_zh: SQL 的递归公用表表达式 (CTE) 可以表达对表格数据的复杂计算。然而，它们的累积语义（将所有中间结果收集在联合表中）可能会产生大量的空间和运行时开销。本文以最近提出的递归 CTE 的 USING KEY 变体为起点，并将其演示为 Duck DB 中的生产就绪功能。此 CTE 变体允许查询有选择地“覆盖”先前的中间结果，这最终会导致联合表变得更小并节省运行时间。我们介绍了对 Duck DB 所做的更改，以支持这种新的 CTE 形式，并展示使用 LDBC 图实例的 USING KEY 变体的性能。该演示在 Duck DB 实例中实现了 USING KEY 的全功能实现，预加载了 LDBC 图，以及展示其优势的大量查询。该演示将是交互式的，允许与会者使用示例 SQL 查询和数据。

items.title: Apache Wayang in Action: Enabling Data Systems Integration via a Unified Data Analytics Framework.
items.abstract: Apache Wayang is an open-source framework, which provides a systematic and efficient solution for unifying data analytics over disparate data sources and via integrating multiple heterogeneous data systems. It achieves that by decoupling applications from the underlying systems. In addition, it provides an optimizer so that users do not have to specify the platforms on which their pipeline should run but the optimizer can determine the best way given a cost metric. In this demonstration, we showcase how the flexible architecture of Wayang enables seamless integration with multiple heterogeneous data systems and how the query optimizer can lead to better performance.
items.abstract_zh: Apache Wayang 是一个开源框架，它提供了系统且高效的解决方案，用于通过集成多个异构数据系统来统一不同数据源的数据分析。它通过将应用程序与底层系统解耦来实现这一点。此外，它还提供了一个优化器，以便用户不必指定其管道应运行的平台，但优化器可以根据给定的成本指标确定最佳方式。在本次演示中，我们展示了 Wayang 的灵活架构如何实现与多个异构数据系统的无缝集成，以及查询优化器如何带来更好的性能。

items.title: Finding What You're Looking For: A Distribution-Aware Dataset Search Engine in Action.
items.abstract: The growing volume of academic, commercial, and governmental datasets distributed across countless independent repositories calls for dataset search engines that can answer queries only using publicly shared metadata instead of relying on raw data access. However, the keyword search interfaces of existing metadata-based search engines fail to capture complex user needs, such as distributional requirements, thereby limiting their effectiveness. In this demonstration, we present the first end-to-end system for distribution-aware dataset search over decentralized data repositories. Our prototype combines existing search techniques with recently proposed percentile predicates to provide more powerful query capabilities. Based on our novel Dataset Query Language and a distribution-aware index, the system enables efficient, flexible search without access to raw data. To demonstrate its utility, we curated over 150,000 profiles of tabular datasets from Kaggle and enriched them with statistical information, enabling attendees to explore distribution-aware search and the trade-offs involved in system configuration.
items.abstract_zh: 分布在无数独立存储库中的学术、商业和政府数据集数量不断增长，需要数据集搜索引擎能够仅使用公开共享的元数据而不是依赖于原始数据访问来回答查询。然而，现有的基于元数据的搜索引擎的关键词搜索界面无法捕获复杂的用户需求，例如分布需求，从而限制了其有效性。在本次演示中，我们展示了第一个端到端系统，用于在分散的数据存储库上进行分布式数据集搜索。我们的原型将现有的搜索技术与最近提出的百分位谓词相结合，以提供更强大的查询功能。基于我们新颖的数据集查询语言和分布感知索引，该系统无需访问原始数据即可实现高效、灵活的搜索。为了展示其实用性，我们整理了超过 150,000 个来自 Kaggle 的表格数据集配置文件，并用统计信息丰富了它们，使与会者能够探索分布感知搜索以及系统配置中涉及的权衡。

items.title: PY-SHARQ: A Holistic Python Library for Explaining Association Rules on Relational Data.
items.abstract: Association rules are an important technique for gaining insights over large relational datasets consisting of tuples of elements (i.e., attribute-value pairs). However, it is difficult to explain the relative importance of data elements with respect to the rules in which they appear. This demo showcases SHARQ (ShApley Rules Quantification), a Shapley-based measure for quantifying an element's contribution to a set of association rules. SHARQ is implemented in a convenient Python library, PY-SHARQ, which supports various explainability scenarios for association rules-including importance analysis of individual elements, rules, and attributes.
items.abstract_zh: 关联规则是深入了解由元素元组（即属性值对）组成的大型关系数据集的重要技术。然而，很难解释数据元素相对于它们出现的规则的相对重要性。该演示展示了 SHARQ（ShApley 规则量化），这是一种基于 Shapley 的度量，用于量化元素对一组关联规则的贡献。 SHARQ 在方便的 Python 库 PY-SHARQ 中实现，它支持关联规则的各种可解释性场景，包括单个元素、规则和属性的重要性分析。

items.title: Fifty Years of Transaction Processing Research.
items.abstract: In this short paper, I recount some early history of transaction research, explain why transaction research continues to this day, and speculate about its future.
items.abstract_zh: 在这篇简短的论文中，我回顾了交易研究的一些早期历史，解释了为什么交易研究持续至今，并推测了它的未来。

items.title: Panel on AI for Future Databases: A New Beginning or a Boulevard of Broken Dreams?
items.abstract:
AI has opened new directions in database research, from learned components replacing traditional internals to large language mod- els(LLMs), enabling a new generation of database systems that allow querying data beyond tables. Yet, adoption in commercial databases has been incremental rather than a fundamental rethinking of mod- ern data system stacks. In this panel, we bring together experts from academia and industry to discuss the tension between potential and reality in how AI shapes real-world database products.

We will explore questions such as: What should an AI-ready database stack look like: incremental evolution or radical departure? What prevents AI from replacing traditional components like query optimizers, cost models, and indexes? What does it take for LLM- based innovations to move beyond impressive demos? Can we use LLMs for more than Text-to-SQL and LLM-UDFs? By tackling these questions, this panel will challenge assumptions in research, examine the role of AI in future databases, and ask the following: Is AI the key to overcoming core limitations and will thus enable a new generation of database systems, or maybe AI is just another boulevard of broken (database) dreams?
items.abstract_zh:
人工智能开辟了数据库研究的新方向，从取代传统内部结构的学习组件到大型语言模型（LLM），使新一代数据库系统能够查询表格之外的数据。然而，商业数据库的采用是渐进式的，而不是对现代数据系统堆栈的根本性重新思考。在这个小组中，我们聚集了学术界和工业界的专家，讨论人工智能如何塑造现实世界的数据库产品中潜力与现实之间的紧张关系。

我们将探讨以下问题：人工智能就绪的数据库堆栈应该是什么样子：增量进化还是彻底背离？是什么阻止人工智能取代查询优化器、成本模型和索引等传统组件？基于法学硕士的创新如何才能超越令人印象深刻的演示？我们是否可以将 LLM 用于文本到 SQL 和 LLM-UDF 之外的其他用途？通过解决这些问题，该小组将挑战研究中的假设，审视人工智能在未来数据库中的作用，并提出以下问题：人工智能是克服核心限制的关键，从而实现新一代数据库系统，还是人工智能只是破碎的（数据库）梦想的另一条大道？

items.title: 21st International Workshop on Data Management on New Hardware (DaMoN).
items.abstract: New hardware, such as multi-core CPUs, GPUs, FPGAs, new mem- ory and storage technologies, and low-power devices, bring new challenges and opportunities in optimizing database systems per- formance. Consequently, exploiting the characteristics of modern hardware has become an important topic of database systems re- search. In the last two decades, the DaMoN Workshop has estab- lished itself as the primary database venue to present ideas on how to exploit new hardware for data management, in particular, how to improve the performance or scalability of databases, how new hardware unlocks new database application scenarios, and how data management could benefit from future hardware.
items.abstract_zh: 新硬件，如多核CPU、GPU、FPGA、新内存和存储技术以及低功耗设备，为优化数据库系统性能带来了新的挑战和机遇。因此，利用现代硬件的特性已成为数据库系统研究的一个重要课题。在过去的二十年中，DaMoN Workshop 已成为主要的数据库场所，提出如何利用新硬件进行数据管理的想法，特别是如何提高数据库的性能或可扩展性、新硬件如何解锁新的数据库应用场景以及数据管理如何从未来的硬件中受益。

items.title: Eighth International Workshop on Exploiting Artificial Intelligence Techniques for Data Management (aiDM).
items.abstract:
Recent advances in AI techniques, along with the development of enabling hardware and infrastructure, have facilitated the integration of AI across a wide range of domains and tasks. Specifically, AI has been employed to process various types of data, including numerical, textual, and image data, and has been adopted in large-scale distributed systems. From a data management perspective, this requires the use of cutting-edge AI solutions for data management tasks and systems.

aiDM is a full-day workshop that provides a platform for innovative interdisciplinary research on the interaction between AI and data management, and the development of new AI technologies for data-related tasks. This year, aiDM'25 will focus on the transparent application of AI techniques (e.g., utilizing Generative AI frameworks) for data management in enterprise-level workloads.
items.abstract_zh:
人工智能技术的最新进展，以及支持硬件和基础设施的发展，促进了人工智能在广泛领域和任务中的集成。具体来说，人工智能已被用来处理各种类型的数据，包括数字、文本和图像数据，并已被应用于大规模分布式系统。从数据管理的角度来看，这需要使用尖端的人工智能解决方案来执行数据管理任务和系统。

aiDM 是一个全天的研讨会，为人工智能和数据管理之间的交互以及为数据相关任务开发新的人工智能技术的创新跨学科研究提供了一个平台。今年，aiDM'25 将重点关注人工智能技术（例如利用生成式人工智能框架）在企业级工作负载中进行数据管理的透明应用。

items.title: CORE+: A Complex Event Recognition Engine in C++.
items.abstract: Complex Event Recognition (CER) refers to the activity of analyzing streams of continuously arriving event data, to recognize collections of events that satisfy user-defined patterns. CER is known to be computationally challenging, since it requires maintaining a set of partial matches, and this set quickly grows super-linearly in the number of processed events. In this paper we demonstrate CORE+, a mature COmplex event Recognition Engine that circumvents the super-linear partial match problem. CORE+ is based on a research prototype named CORE; we demonstrate the new features and capabilities that make CORE+ a release version of CORE.
items.abstract_zh: 复杂事件识别 (CER) 是指分析连续到达的事件数据流以识别满足用户定义模式的事件集合的活动。众所周知，CER 在计算上具有挑战性，因为它需要维护一组部分匹配，并且该组在处理事件的数量上快速超线性增长。在本文中，我们演示了 CORE+，这是一个成熟的复杂事件识别引擎，可以规避超线性部分匹配问题。 CORE+基于名为CORE的研究原型；我们展示了使 CORE+ 成为 CORE 发行版本的新特性和功能。

items.title: DANTE: Hybrid AI System for Context-Aware Interpretable Feature Engineering.
items.abstract: Machine learning has become essential in data management, enabling accuracy and efficiency in handling complex datasets. For structured data, however, feature engineering is required to enhance predictive performance but remains a time-consuming, domain-specific task. Moreover, as ML systems are increasingly adopted, ensuring interpretability, particularly for domain experts, has become important. To address these challenges, we proposed KRAFT , an automated feature engineering (AutoFE) approach that combines a neural generator for feature transformation with a knowledge-based discriminator to ensure interpretability through symbolic reasoning. We have also empirically shown that KRAFT outperforms SOTA approaches in both accuracy and interpretability. In this demonstration, we introduce DANTE, an interactive tool based on KRAFT to facilitate feature generation and model evaluation on structured data. Its no-code interface empowers users to generate meaningful, interpretable features for real-world applications. A demonstration video of DANTE is available at https://DANTE.com.
items.abstract_zh: 机器学习在数据管理中已变得至关重要，可以提高处理复杂数据集的准确性和效率。然而，对于结构化数据，需要特征工程来增强预测性能，但这仍然是一项耗时的、特定于领域的任务。此外，随着机器学习系统越来越多地被采用，确保可解释性（特别是对于领域专家而言）变得非常重要。为了应对这些挑战，我们提出了 KRAFT，这是一种自动化特征工程 (AutoFE) 方法，它将用于特征转换的神经生成器与基于知识的鉴别器相结合，以确保通过符号推理的可解释性。我们还凭经验证明，KRAFT 在准确性和可解释性方面均优于 SOTA 方法。在本次演示中，我们介绍了 DANTE，这是一种基于 KRAFT 的交互式工具，可促进结构化数据的特征生成和模型评估。其无代码界面使用户能够为现实应用程序生成有意义的、可解释的功能。 DANTE 的演示视频可在 https://DANTE.com 上获取。

items.title: Blink Twice - Automatic Workload Pinning and Regression Detection for Versionless Apache Spark using Retries.
items.abstract: For many users of Apache Spark, managing Spark version upgrades is a significant interruption that typically involves a time-intensive code migration. This is mainly because in Spark, there is no clear separation between the application code and the engine code, making it hard to manage them independently (dependency clashes, use of internal APIs). In Databricks' Serverless Spark offering, we introduced Versionless Spark where we leverage Spark Connect to fully decouple the client application from the Spark engine which allows us to seamlessly upgrade Spark engine versions. In this paper, we show how our infrastructure built around Spark Connect automatically upgrades and remediates failures in automated Spark workloads without any interruption. Using Versionless Spark, Databricks users' Spark workloads run indefinitely, and always on the latest version based on a fully managed experience while retaining nearly all of the programmability of Apache Spark.
items.abstract_zh: 对于 Apache Spark 的许多用户来说，管理 Spark 版本升级是一个重大中断，通常涉及耗时的代码迁移。这主要是因为在 Spark 中，应用程序代码和引擎代码之间没有明确的分离，导致很难独立管理它们（依赖冲突、使用内部 API）。在 Databricks 的 Serverless Spark 产品中，我们引入了 Versionless Spark，其中我们利用 Spark Connect 将客户端应用程序与 Spark 引擎完全解耦，这使我们能够无缝升级 Spark 引擎版本。在本文中，我们展示了围绕 Spark Connect 构建的基础设施如何自动升级并修复自动化 Spark 工作负载中的故障，而不会造成任何中断。使用 Versionless Spark，Databricks 用户的 Spark 工作负载可以无限期运行，并且始终基于完全托管体验的最新版本，同时保留 Apache Spark 的几乎所有可编程性。

items.title: Query Decorrelation in the Fabric Data Warehouse.
items.abstract: SQL subqueries allow practitioners to embed logic almost anywhere within the context of a more general query and are used both by developers and automatic query generators to express complex SQL logic. Correlated subqueries refer to columns defined by their outer queries and can be evaluated by tuple-at-a-time execution strategies. These evaluation schemes are generally slow, except when specialized index strategies are available. Query decorrelation is a technique that removes correlation from subqueries and typically results in faster set-oriented execution. Query decorrelation has been an active area of research and has received recent attention due to some distributed engines not directly supporting execution of correlated subqueries. This work extends previous approaches and describes our experience in building a new decorrelation framework for the Microsoft Fabric Data Warehouse. We explain our new decorrelation approach, how we integrate it in the query optimizer, and report an experimental evaluation of our techniques on a complex benchmark over TPC-DS data.
items.abstract_zh: SQL 子查询允许从业者将逻辑嵌入到更通用查询上下文中的几乎任何位置，并且开发人员和自动查询生成器都可以使用 SQL 子查询来表达复杂的 SQL 逻辑。相关子查询是指由其外部查询定义的列，并且可以通过一次元组执行策略进行评估。这些评估方案通常很慢，除非有专门的索引策略可用。查询去相关是一种从子查询中消除相关性的技术，通常会导致更快的面向集合的执行。查询去相关一直是一个活跃的研究领域，并且由于一些分布式引擎不直接支持相关子查询的执行而最近受到关注。这项工作扩展了以前的方法，并描述了我们为 Microsoft Fabric 数据仓库构建新的去相关框架的经验。我们解释了新的去相关方法、如何将其集成到查询优化器中，并报告了在 TPC-DS 数据的复杂基准上对我们的技术进行的实验评估。

items.title: LLM-DPM - Workshop on Large Language Models for Data Process Management.
items.abstract: The LLM-DPM Workshop investigates the transformative impact of Large Language Models (LLMs) and Explainable AI (XAI) on Data and Process Management. With organizations facing growing dependence on intricate data-driven workflows, there is an urgent demand for systems that prioritize not only efficiency but also transparency, reliability, and equity. This workshop serves as a dedicated platform to explore the convergence of LLMs, process mining, and database technologies, tackling critical issues such as enhancing data integrity, refining query understanding, forecasting process outcomes, and optimizing system performance. Featuring technical discussions, keynote speeches, and collaborative panels, LLM-DPM seeks to bridge the gap between academic research and industry applications, inspiring innovative approaches and advancing the creation of accountable, AI-powered solutions for process management challenges.
items.abstract_zh: LLM-DPM 研讨会研究大型语言模型 (LLM) 和可解释人工智能 (XAI) 对数据和流程管理的变革性影响。随着组织越来越依赖复杂的数据驱动工作流程，迫切需要不仅优先考虑效率，而且优先考虑透明度、可靠性和公平性的系统。该研讨会作为一个专用平台，探索法学硕士、流程挖掘和数据库技术的融合，解决关键问题，例如增强数据完整性、完善查询理解、预测流程结果和优化系统性能。 LLM-DPM 以技术讨论、主题演讲和协作小组为特色，旨在弥合学术研究和行业应用之间的差距，激发创新方法，并推动创建负责任的、人工智能驱动的解决方案，以应对流程管理挑战。

items.title: Locator: Local Stability for Rankings.
items.abstract: Rankings play a crucial role in decision-making. However, if minor changes to items significantly alter their rankings, the quality of the decisions being made can be compromised. The stability of ranking is a measure used to assess how modifications to the ranking algorithm or data affect results. We introduce a novel measure we refer to as local stability, indicating the effect of minor changes to the values of an item in the ranking on its rank. Our proposed definition takes into account the presence of multiple items with similar qualities in the ranking, called dense regions, permitting minor modifications to swap the positions of items within the region. We present Locator, a system utilizing local stability to provide ranking stakeholders--producers, participants, and consumers--with insights into the ranking. Ranking producers can use Locator to adjust their ranking functions, enhancing item stability. They can then share the local stability information with ranking stakeholders to provide a meaningful understanding of how uncertainty in an item's attributes affects its rank without exposing the ranking methodology.
items.abstract_zh: 排名在决策中起着至关重要的作用。然而，如果项目的微小变化会显着改变其排名，则决策的质量可能会受到影响。排名的稳定性是用于评估排名算法或数据的修改如何影响结果的衡量标准。我们引入了一种称为局部稳定性的新颖度量，表明排名中某个项目的值的微小变化对其排名的影响。我们提出的定义考虑到排名中存在多个具有相似质量的项目，称为密集区域，允许进行较小的修改以交换区域内项目的位置。我们推出了 Locator，这是一个利用本地稳定性为排名利益相关者（生产者、参与者和消费者）提供排名见解的系统。排名制作者可以使用Locator来调整排名功能，增强项目稳定性。然后，他们可以与排名利益相关者共享本地稳定性信息，以有意义地了解项目属性的不确定性如何影响其排名，而无需公开排名方法。

items.title: Prompt Editor: A Taxonomy-driven System for Guided LLM Prompt Development in Enterprise Settings.
items.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly integrated into enterprise platforms, requiring business users to write prompts despite the lack of prompt engineering expertise. Although these users possess domain knowledge, they struggle to translate their intentions into effective prompts and often overlook established practices such as including few-shot examples. We present Prompt Editor, a human-in-the-loop system that assists users in writing high-quality prompts by leveraging an organization's existing corpus of prompts to both initialize and refine user-created prompts. At its core, Prompt Editor leverages a taxonomy of prompt segment types learned from an organization's existing collection of prompts. This supports the segmentation of prompts and feedback generation process. We demonstrate Prompt Editor with a hypothetical LLM-based enterprise platform that extracts structured data from unstructured text. Attendees can interact with the demo to experience first-hand how Prompt Editor supports prompt writing.
items.abstract_zh: 大型语言模型 (LLM) 越来越多地集成到企业平台中，要求业务用户在缺乏即时工程专业知识的情况下编写提示。尽管这些用户拥有领域知识，但他们很难将自己的意图转化为有效的提示，并且经常忽视既定的做法，例如包含少量示例。我们推出了提示编辑器，这是一个人机交互系统，可利用组织现有的提示语料库来初始化和完善用户创建的提示，从而帮助用户编写高质量的提示。提示编辑器的核心是利用从组织现有提示集合中学习到的提示段类型分类。这支持提示和反馈生成过程的分段。我们使用假设的基于 LLM 的企业平台演示 Prompt Editor，该平台可从非结构化文本中提取结构化数据。与会者可以与演示互动，亲身体验提示编辑器如何支持提示写作。

items.title: A Fast Line Density Visualization Plugin for Geographic Information Systems.
items.abstract: Line Density Visualization (LDV) has been widely used in different domains, e.g., transportation science, urban planning, and criminology. Therefore, various geographic information systems, e.g., ArcGIS and QGIS, can also support this tool. However, all these GIS platforms mainly adopt the naïve algorithm for generating LDV, which cannot be scalable to high resolution sizes and large-scale line-segment datasets. To tackle this issue, we have developed a new QGIS plugin, called Fast Line Density Analysis, for efficiently supporting two types of accurate approximation, namely (1) generating LDV with an ε-relative error guarantee (εLDV) and (2) generating LDV with multiple thresholds (τLDV). In this demonstration, we have prepared three large-scale datasets (with up to 15.7 million line segments) for participants to compare our plugin with QGIS and ArcGIS in terms of accuracy and efficiency. In particular, participants can also tune different parameters in our plugin for understanding how they can affect the visualization quality and efficiency. The demonstration video is available in the YouTube and Bilibili links, which are https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EYl3Wieb-2I and https://www.bilibili.com/video/BV1aJwaeMEHr, respectively.
items.abstract_zh: 线密度可视化（LDV）已广泛应用于不同领域，例如交通科学、城市规划和犯罪学。因此，各种地理信息系统，例如ArcGIS和QGIS，也可以支持该工具。然而，所有这些GIS平台主要采用朴素的LDV生成算法，无法扩展到高分辨率尺寸和大规模线段数据集。为了解决这个问题，我们开发了一个新的 QGIS 插件，称为快速线密度分析，用于有效支持两种类型的精确近似，即（1）生成具有 ε 相​​对误差保证的 LDV（εLDV）和（2）生成具有多个阈值的 LDV（τLDV）。在本次演示中，我们准备了三个大型数据集（多达1570万条线段）供参与者将我们的插件与QGIS和ArcGIS在准确性和效率方面进行比较。特别是，参与者还可以调整我们插件中的不同参数，以了解它们如何影响可视化质量和效率。演示视频可在 YouTube 和 Bilibili 链接中获取，分别为 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EYl3Wieb-2I 和 https://www.bilibili.com/video/BV1aJwaeMEHr。

items.title: Unity Catalog: Open and Universal Governance for the Lakehouse and Beyond.
items.abstract: Enterprises are increasingly adopting the Lakehouse architecture to manage their data assets due to its flexibility, low cost, and high performance. While the catalog plays a central role in this architecture, it remains underexplored, and current Lakehouse catalogs exhibit key limitations, including inconsistent governance, narrow interoperability, and lack of support for data discovery. Additionally, there is growing demand to govern a broader range of assets beyond tabular data, such as unstructured data and AI models, which existing catalogs are not equipped to handle. To address these challenges, we introduce Unity Catalog (UC), an open and universal Lakehouse catalog developed at Databricks that supports a wide variety of assets and workloads, provides consistent governance, and integrates efficiently with external systems, all with strong performance guarantees. We describe the primary design challenges and how UC's architecture meets them, and share insights from usage across thousands of customer deployments that validate its design choices. UC's core APIs and both server and client implementations have been available as open source since June 2024.
items.abstract_zh: 由于其灵活性、低成本和高性能，越来越多的企业采用 Lakehouse 架构来管理其数据资产。虽然目录在该架构中发挥着核心作用，但它仍然未被充分开发，并且当前的 Lakehouse 目录表现出关键的局限性，包括不一致的治理、狭窄的互操作性以及缺乏对数据发现的支持。此外，人们越来越需要管理表格数据之外的更广泛的资产，例如非结构化数据和人工智能模型，而现有目录无法处理这些数据。为了应对这些挑战，我们引入了 Unity Catalog (UC)，这是一个由 Databricks 开发的开放式通用 Lakehouse 目录，支持各种资产和工作负载，提供一致的治理，并与外部系统高效集成，所有这些都具有强大的性能保证。我们描述了主要的设计挑战以及 UC 的架构如何应对这些挑战，并分享了来自数千个客户部署的使用情况的见解，以验证其设计选择。自 2024 年 6 月起，UC 的核心 API 以及服务器和客户端实现均已开源。

items.title: Ninth Workshop on Human-In-the-Loop Data Analytics (HILDA).
items.abstract: HILDA brings together researchers and practitioners to exchange ideas and results on human-data interaction. It explores how data management and analysis can be made more effective when taking into account the people who design and build these processes as well as those who are impacted by their results. Following the past few years, we plan to continue to focus on this year's workshop on early-stage research that is promising and exciting, which includes pairing each accepted paper with a mentor.
