The theme for this edition of the workshop is HILDA and Large Language Models. However, the workshop is not limited to this theme and other topics are also of interest. In this summary, we describe the workshop, its main focus areas and our review and mentorship plan.
items.abstract_zh: HILDA 将研究人员和从业者聚集在一起，交流有关人类数据交互的想法和结果。它探讨了在考虑设计和构建这些流程的人员以及受其结果影响的人员的情况下，如何使数据管理和分析更加有效。继过去几年之后，我们计划继续关注今年的早期研究研讨会，这些研究前景光明且令人兴奋，其中包括将每篇被接受的论文与导师配对。本期研讨会的主题是 HILDA 和大型语言模型。不过，研讨会并不局限于这个主题，其他话题也很有趣。在本摘要中，我们描述了研讨会、其主要重点领域以及我们的审查和指导计划。

items.title: PASCAL: A Theory-Informed Visual Interface for Property Graph Schema Visualization.
items.abstract: Property graph schemas are essential for organizing property graph data, serving both prescriptive and descriptive roles. This has led to the recent development of property graph schema languages such as PG-Schema. While understanding of these languages requires familiarity with complex syntax, this poses usability challenges, particularly for domain experts who are not programmers. Current visual abstractions, such as the labeled schema graph (LSG), simplify representation but suffers from visual clutter and limited feature support. In this demonstration, we present a novel theory-informed visual interface called PASCAL for multi-modal visualization of property graph schemas to address these limitations.It realizes a novel visual abstraction called labeled iconized composite schema (LICS), whose design is informed by theories and principles from HCI, cognitive psychology, and visualization. Under the hood, it leverages the novel Map-Paint paradigm for creating and maintaining the visual components of LICS.
items.abstract_zh: 属性图模式对于组织属性图数据至关重要，具有规定性和描述性的作用。这导致了属性图模式语言（例如 PG-Schema）的最新发展。虽然理解这些语言需要熟悉复杂的语法，但这带来了可用性挑战，特别是对于非程序员的领域专家而言。当前的视觉抽象，例如标记模式图（LSG），简化了表示，但存在视觉混乱和功能支持有限的问题。在本次演示中，我们提出了一种新颖的基于理论的可视化界面，称为 PASCAL，用于属性图模式的多模式可视化，以解决这些限制。它实现了一种称为标记图标化复合模式 (LICS) 的新颖视觉抽象，其设计受到 HCI、认知心理学和可视化的理论和原理的启发。在底层，它利用新颖的地图绘制范例来创建和维护 LICS 的视觉组件。

items.title: RTS+: Reliable Text to SQL.
items.abstract: The reliable translation of natural language queries to SQL is piv- otal for empowering non-technical users to interact with databases. Current state-of-the-art systems fail to holistically address ambi- guities or schema-linking challenges and may generate erroneous SQL leading to faulty query generation. We present RTS+ (Reliable Text-to-SQL Plus), a novel interactive system that builds on the con- cepts of Reliable Text-to-SQL (RTS) [3 ]. RTS+ focuses on improving user experience by incorporating adaptive abstention mechanisms and interactive feedback loops during query generation. Through this demo, we illustrate RTS+'s capabilities using a rich graphical interface that guides users through schema-linking decisions, query generation, verification, and refinement. Users can explore RTS+ on real-world datasets with detailed analytics for understanding abstention-triggered interventions and their outcomes. Using this system, users can achieve perfect scores at popular benchmarks correctly answering a few questions, if required.
items.abstract_zh: 将自然语言查询可靠地转换为 SQL 对于非技术用户与数据库交互至关重要。当前最先进的系统无法全面解决歧义或模式链接挑战，并且可能会生成错误的 SQL，从而导致错误的查询生成。我们提出了 RTS+（Reliable Text-to-SQL Plus），这是一种基于 Reliable Text-to-SQL (RTS) [3] 概念构建的新型交互系统。 RTS+ 专注于通过在查询生成过程中结合自适应弃权机制和交互式反馈循环来改善用户体验。通过此演示，我们使用丰富的图形界面演示了 RTS+ 的功能，该界面指导用户完成模式链接决策、查询生成、验证和细化。用户可以在现实数据集上探索 RTS+，并进行详细分析，以了解弃权触发的干预措施及其结果。使用该系统，用户可以根据需要正确回答一些问题，在流行的基准测试中获得满分。

items.title: Oceanus: Enable SLO-Aware Vertical Autoscaling for Cloud-Native Streaming Services in Tencent.
items.abstract:
Stream services have gained immense popularity for facilitating real-time data processing, powering numerous applications within Tencent. Most of these streaming jobs are deployed at scale on the cloud to achieve higher availability and stability. Due to the dynamic nature of input data rates, cloud-native streaming services within Tencent experience both periodical and distributional workload imbalances. Vertical scaling mechanism provided on the cloud platform aids in more fine-grained automatic resource adjustments for the workload imbalances. However, current vertical autoscaling solutions on cloud platform face challenges in achieving timely and accurate scaling predictions, managing service interruptions caused by resource scaling, and efficiently handling various autoscaling abnormalities. These issues can hinder the solutions from meeting the service level objectives (SLO) of streaming jobs in Tencent.

In this paper, we introduce Oceanus, an end-to-end solution designed to facilitate precise and automated resource adjustment through vertical autoscaling for cloud-native streaming services, while ensuring that their SLO are consistently met. Oceanus incorporates proactive ML-enhanced forecasting technique that learns from historical load patterns to accurately determine optimal scaling times, cross-layer in-situ resource adjustment mechanism that enables resource adjustments without interrupting stream processing and robust resolution workflow to ensure rapid recovery from unexpected issues after resource adjustment. We evaluate Oceanus in our production environment, and the experiment results have further validated our system design.
items.abstract_zh:
流服务因促进实时数据处理而广受欢迎，为腾讯内部的众多应用程序提供支持。这些流作业大部分都大规模部署在云端，以实现更高的可用性和稳定性。由于输入数据速率的动态特性，腾讯内部的云原生流媒体服务会遇到周期性和分布式工作负载不平衡的情况。云平台提供的垂直伸缩机制，有助于针对工作负载不平衡情况进行更细粒度的自动资源调整。然而，当前云平台上的垂直自动伸缩解决方案在实现及时准确的伸缩预测、管理资源伸缩引起的服务中断以及高效处理各种自动伸缩异常方面面临挑战。这些问题可能会阻碍解决方案满足腾讯流媒体作业的服务水平目标（SLO）。

在本文中，我们介绍了 Oceanus，这是一种端到端解决方案，旨在通过云原生流媒体服务的垂直自动扩展来促进精确和自动化的资源调整，同时确保始终满足其 SLO。 Oceanus 采用了主动式机器学习增强预测技术，可以从历史负载模式中学习，准确确定最佳扩展时间；跨层原位资源调整机制，可以在不中断流处理的情况下进行资源调整；以及强大的解决工作流程，以确保在资源调整后从意外问题中快速恢复。我们在生产环境中对Oceanus进行了评估，实验结果进一步验证了我们的系统设计。

items.title: CloudJump II: Optimizing Cloud Databases for Shared Storage.
items.abstract: The CloudJump framework aims to shed light on how compute-storage disaggregation can effectively enhance cloud databases' scalability, resilience, and efficiency by only relying on standard cloud storage services. To explore the evolving landscape of cloud-native databases, this study navigates through the design and implementation intricacies of the shared-storage architecture in the CloudJump framework. By focusing on the shared storage model integral to cloud database systems with a leader-follower pattern, we address the challenges of ensuring data consistency across multiple compute nodes-a cornerstone for achieving scalability in cloud environments. At its core, this study proposes Multi-Version Data (MVD), a technique designed to improve node consistency in shared storage environments. MVD enables fine-grained version management and precise reconstruction of data pages by compute nodes, facilitating streamlined recovery and robust data persistence without requiring a custom storage layer. This design simplifies operations, reduces costs, and presents a pragmatic response to the limitations of existing cloud storage approaches. The deployment of MVD in PolarDB demonstrates its practical benefits, including enhanced node decoupling and accelerated failover. The performance and reliability of the enhanced system, CloudJump II, are substantiated through comprehensive experimental evaluation and production usage.
items.abstract_zh: CloudJump 框架旨在阐明计算存储分解如何仅依靠标准云存储服务来有效增强云数据库的可扩展性、弹性和效率。为了探索云原生数据库不断发展的前景，本研究探讨了 CloudJump 框架中共享存储架构的设计和实现的复杂性。通过专注于具有领导者-跟随者模式的云数据库系统集成的共享存储模型，我们解决了确保跨多个计算节点的数据一致性的挑战，这是在云环境中实现可扩展性的基石。本研究的核心提出了多版本数据（MVD），这是一种旨在提高共享存储环境中节点一致性的技术。 MVD 支持计算节点的细粒度版本管理和数据页的精确重建，从而促进简化的恢复和强大的数据持久性，而无需自定义存储层。这种设计简化了操作，降低了成本，并对现有云存储方法的局限性做出了务实的回应。在PolarDB中部署MVD展示了它的实际好处，包括增强的节点解耦和加速故障转移。增强型系统 CloudJump II 的性能和可靠性通过全面的实验评估和生产使用得到证实。

items.title: CausaLens: A System for Summarizing Causal DAGs.
items.abstract: Causal inference aids researchers in discovering causal relationships, leading to scientific insights. Pearl's causal model uses causal DAGs to estimate causal effects, so DAG correctness is essential for reliable causal conclusions. However, for high-dimensional data, the causal DAGs are often complex beyond human verifiability. Graph summarization is a logical next step, but current methods for general-purpose graph summarization are inadequate for causal DAG summarization, as they are not designed to preserve causal information. In this demonstration, we present a system called CausaLens that summarizes a given causal DAG and balances graph simplification for better understanding and retention of essential causal information for reliable inference directly on the summary DAG. We illustrate that causal inference on the summary DAG is more robust to misspecification in the initial causal DAG compared to performing inference directly on the initial causal DAG, thereby enhancing the robustness of causal inference. We will demonstrate the utility of CausaLens for generating useful summary causal DAGs by interacting with the SIGMOD'25 participants, who will act as data analysts aiming to perform causal analysis on high dimensional datasets.
items.abstract_zh: 因果推理帮助研究人员发现因果关系，从而获得科学见解。 Pearl 的因果模型使用因果 DAG 来估计因果效应，因此 DAG 的正确性对于可靠的因果结论至关重要。然而，对于高维数据，因果 DAG 通常非常复杂，超出了人类的可验证性。图摘要是合乎逻辑的下一步，但当前的通用图摘要方法不足以用于因果 DAG 摘要，因为它们并非旨在保留因果信息。在此演示中，我们提出了一个名为 CausaLens 的系统，该系统总结给定的因果 DAG 并平衡图简化，以便更好地理解和保留基本因果信息，以便直接在摘要 DAG 上进行可靠的推理。我们说明，与直接在初始因果 DAG 上执行推理相比，摘要 DAG 上的因果推理对于初始因果 DAG 中的错误指定更加稳健，从而增强了因果推理的稳健性。我们将通过与 SIGMOD'25 参与者交互来演示 CausaLens 在生成有用的摘要因果 DAG 方面的实用性，这些参与者将充当数据分析师，旨在对高维数据集执行因果分析。

items.title: Scheduling Data Processing Pipelines for Incremental Training on MLP-based Recommendation Models.
items.abstract: Multi-layer Perceptron (MLP)-based models have been widely exploited by modern recommendation applications. In practice, industrial recommendation scenarios frequently launch continuous incremental training jobs with only one epoch to capture real-time user features. This kind of job is shorter than full training and has a larger proportion of feature processing time. To fully utilize fragmentation resources, our model engineering team at Tencent explores resource-constrained CPU clusters to perform such incremental training workloads. To improve the efficiency of such workloads, we notice scheduling optimizations by overlapping feature processing and model training at the level of data processing pipelines. In particular, we propose an intra-pipeline scheduling strategy, which prefetches feature processing operators dynamically to fill the idle time of CPUs during the communication of embedding lookup. Furthermore, we propose an inter-pipeline scheduling strategy, which balances the resource demands of different pipelines. It prioritizes the execution of critical pipelines and overlaps the communication in critical pipelines with the execution of non-critical pipelines. Based on the two scheduling strategies, we implement a novel incremental recommendation training framework called RECS on top of TensorFlow. In our experimental studies, RECS achieves a speedup of 1.36x over existing solutions on industrial workloads.
items.abstract_zh: 基于多层感知器（MLP）的模型已被现代推荐应用广泛利用。在实践中，工业推荐场景经常启动持续增量训练作业，仅用一个 epoch 来捕获实时用户特征。这种工作比完整训练的时间更短，并且特征处理时间比例更大。为了充分利用碎片资源，腾讯模型工程团队探索了资源受限的CPU集群来执行此类增量训练工作负载。为了提高此类工作负载的效率，我们注意到通过在数据处理管道级别重叠特征处理和模型训练来进行调度优化。特别是，我们提出了一种管道内调度策略，该策略动态预取特征处理运算符以填充嵌入查找通信期间CPU的空闲时间。此外，我们提出了一种管道间调度策略，可以平衡不同管道的资源需求。它优先考虑关键管道的执行，并将关键管道中的通信与非关键管道的执行重叠。基于这两种调度策略，我们在 TensorFlow 之上实现了一种新颖的增量推荐训练框架，称为 RECS。在我们的实验研究中，RECS 在工业工作负载方面比现有解决方案实现了 1.36 倍的加速。

items.title: AlayaDB: The Data Foundation for Efficient and Effective Long-context LLM Inference.
items.abstract: AlayaDB is a cutting-edge vector database system natively architected for efficient and effective long-context inference for Large Language Models (LLMs) at AlayaDB AI. Specifically, it decouples the KV cache and attention computation from the LLM inference systems, and encapsulates them into a novel vector database system. For the Model as a Service providers (MaaS), AlayaDB consumes fewer hardware resources and offers higher generation quality for various workloads with different kinds of Service Level Objectives (SLOs), when compared with the existing alternative solutions (e.g., KV cache disaggregation, retrieval-based sparse attention). The crux of AlayaDB is that it abstracts the attention computation and cache management for LLM inference into a query processing procedure, and optimizes the performance via a native query optimizer. In this work, we demonstrate the effectiveness of AlayaDB via (i) two use cases from our industry partners, and (ii) extensive experimental results on LLM inference benchmarks.
items.abstract_zh: AlayaDB 是一个尖端的矢量数据库系统，其原生架构旨在为 AlayaDB AI 的大型语言模型 (LLM) 进行高效且有效的长上下文推理。具体来说，它将 KV 缓存和注意力计算与 LLM 推理系统解耦，并将它们封装到一个新颖的向量数据库系统中。对于模型即服务提供商（MaaS）来说，与现有的替代解决方案（例如，KV缓存分解、基于检索的稀疏注意力）相比，AlayaDB消耗更少的硬件资源，并为具有不同服务级别目标（SLO）的各种工作负载提供更高的生成质量。 AlayaDB的关键在于它将LLM推理的注意力计算和缓存管理抽象为查询处理过程，并通过原生查询优化器优化性能。在这项工作中，我们通过（i）我们行业合作伙伴的两个用例，以及（ii）LLM推理基准的广泛实验结果来证明AlayaDB的有效性。

items.title: Mobility Stream Processing on NebulaStream and MEOS.
items.abstract:
The increasing use of Internet-of-Things (IoT) sensors in moving objects has resulted in vast amounts of spatiotemporal streaming data. To analyze this data in situ, real-time spatiotemporal processing is needed. However, current stream processing systems designed for IoT environments often lack spatiotemporal processing capabilities, and existing spatiotemporal libraries primarily focus on analyzing historical data. This gap makes performing real-time spatiotemporal analytics challenging.

In this demonstration, we present NebulaMEOS, which combines MEOS (Mobility Engine Open Source), a spatiotemporal processing library, with NebulaStream, a scalable data management system for IoT applications. By integrating MEOS into NebulaStream, NebulaMEOS utilizes spatiotemporal functionalities to process and analyze streaming data in real-time. We demonstrate NebulaMEOS by querying data streamed from edge devices on trains by the Société Nationale des Chemins de fer Belges (SNCB). Visitors can experience demonstrations of geofencing and geospatial complex event processing, visualizing real-time train operations and environmental impacts.
items.abstract_zh:
物联网 (IoT) 传感器在移动物体中的使用越来越多，产生了大量的时空流数据。为了原位分析这些数据，需要实时时空处理。然而，当前针对物联网环境设计的流处理系统往往缺乏时空处理能力，而现有的时空库主要侧重于分析历史数据。这种差距使得执行实时时空分析变得具有挑战性。

在本次演示中，我们展示了 NebulaMEOS，它将时空处理库 MEOS（开源移动引擎）与适用于物联网应用的可扩展数据管理系统 NebulaStream 相结合。通过将MEOS集成到NebulaStream中，NebulaMEOS利用时空功能来实时处理和分析流数据。我们通过查询比利时国家铁路公司 (SNCB) 从火车边缘设备传输的数据来演示 NebulaMEOS。参观者可以体验地理围栏和地理空间复杂事件处理的演示，直观地看到实时列车运行和环境影响。

items.title: Sentence to Model: Cost-Effective Data Collection LLM Agent.
items.abstract: We introduce Sentence-to-Model , an automated system that converts natural language queries into tabular datasets and predictive models. The system utilizes LLM agents for planning and active learning to prioritize data collection under budget constraints, such as API costs and rate limits. By integrating resources, it generates datasets and trains machine learning models with minimal human intervention. Sentence-to-Model streamlines data collection and enables the refinement of user needs and easy exploration of the data landscape. Our approach highlights the potential of combining LLMs with algorithmic techniques for efficient data science workflows.
items.abstract_zh: 我们引入了 Sentence-to-Model ，这是一个将自然语言查询转换为表格数据集和预测模型的自动化系统。该系统利用 LLM 代理进行规划和主动学习，以在预算限制（例如 API 成本和速率限制）下确定数据收集的优先级。通过整合资源，它可以在最少的人工干预下生成数据集并训练机器学习模型。句子到模型简化了数据收集，并能够细化用户需求并轻松探索数据景观。我们的方法强调了将法学硕士与算法技术相结合以实现高效数据科学工作流程的潜力。

items.title: MaLT: A Framework for Managing Large Transactions in OceanBase.
items.abstract:
Large transactions challenge the designs of the modern relational database systems, as they necessitate the management of uncommitted changes. OceanBase, a distributed relational database system, has implemented an LSM-tree based storage engine to achieve high read and write performance. However, the support for large transactions of existing LSM-tree based systems remains a problem under the append-only principle of LSM-tree, making them suffer from memory constraints and low efficiency.

In this paper, we present MaLT, a framework designed to efficiently Ma nage L arge T ransactions within OceanBase system. We introduce Transaction Context Table (TCT) and Transaction Data Table (TDT) to manage transaction states in the LSM-tree based storage engine. Based on them, we further devise an efficient recovery mechanism to provide high availability of the databases after unexpected system failures. Unlike existing LSM-tree based RDBMSs that abstract LSM-trees as key-value stores, MaLT directly implements transactions into the LSM-tree and leverages its unique features. The backfill (i.e., in-row version number update upon commit) and undo operations for transactions are seamlessly integrated into the compaction stage of the LSM-tree. This enables efficient commit and abort, and in the meantime helps avoid the instant latency of recovering the system from uncommitted large transactions. Moreover, MaLT also embeds transaction information directly within the LSM-tree, facilitating various optimizations to improve both read and write performance. Finally, the experimental results demonstrate the effectiveness and the scalability of our approach implemented in OceanBase.
items.abstract_zh:
大型事务对现代关系数据库系统的设计提出了挑战，因为它们需要管理未提交的更改。 OceanBase是一个分布式关系数据库系统，实现了基于LSM-tree的存储引擎，以实现较高的读写性能。然而，在LSM-tree的append-only原则下，现有的基于LSM-tree的系统对大事务的支持仍然是一个问题，这使得它们受到内存限制和低效率的困扰。

在本文中，我们提出了 MaLT，一个旨在高效管理 OceanBase 系统内的大额交易的框架。我们引入事务上下文表（TCT）和事务数据表（TDT）来管理基于LSM树的存储引擎中的事务状态。在此基础上，我们进一步设计了一种有效的恢复机制，以在意外系统故障后提供数据库的高可用性。与现有的基于 LSM 树的 RDBMS 将 LSM 树抽象为键值存储不同，MaLT 直接将事务实现到 LSM 树中并利用其独特的功能。事务的回填（即提交时行内版本号更新）和撤消操作无缝集成到 LSM 树的压缩阶段。这可以实现高效的提交和中止，同时有助于避免从未提交的大型事务中恢复系统的即时延迟。此外，MaLT还将事务信息直接嵌入LSM树中，促进各种优化以提高读写性能。最后，实验结果证明了我们在OceanBase中实现的方法的有效性和可扩展性。

items.title: Demonstrating CatDB: LLM-based Generation of Data-centric ML Pipelines.
items.abstract: AutoML systems automate finding Machine Learning (ML) pipelines but struggle to scale with large datasets due to time-consuming data analysis and complex hyper-parameter search spaces. LLMs (Large Language Models) offer flexibility and scalability for code generation with strong generalization across coding tasks. However, generating data-centric ML pipeline scripts is more challenging, as it requires complex reasoning to align the needs of a dataset with coding tasks, such as data cleaning or feature transformation. Thus, LLMs struggle to generate effective and efficient ML pipelines. This demo paper presents CatDB, which overcomes these challenges by dynamically generating dataset-specific instructions to guide LLMs in generating effective pipelines. CatDB profiles datasets to extract metadata, including refined data catalog information and statistics, and then uses this metadata to break down pipeline generation into instructions of tasks such as data cleaning, transformation, and model training, tailored to specifics of the dataset at hand. This process enables CatDB to leverage LLM coding capabilities more effectively. Our evaluation shows CatDB outperforms existing LLM-based and AutoML systems with up to orders of magnitude faster runtime on large datasets. The audience will experience CatDB's capabilities with commercial and open-source LLMs, using a variety of real datasets, as shown in our demo video and Colab notebook.
items.abstract_zh: AutoML 系统可以自动查找机器学习 (ML) 管道，但由于耗时的数据分析和复杂的超参数搜索空间，难以扩展大型数据集。 LLM（大型语言模型）为代码生成提供灵活性和可扩展性，并具有跨编码任务的强大泛化能力。然而，生成以数据为中心的 ML 管道脚本更具挑战性，因为它需要复杂的推理来使数据集的需求与编码任务（例如数据清理或特征转换）保持一致。因此，法学硕士很难生成有效且高效的机器学习管道。本演示论文介绍了 CatDB，它通过动态生成特定于数据集的指令来指导法学硕士生成有效的流程，从而克服了这些挑战。 CatDB 对数据集进行分析以提取元数据，包括精炼的数据目录信息和统计信息，然后使用此元数据将管道生成分解为数据清理、转换和模型训练等任务的指令，并根据当前数据集的具体情况进行定制。此过程使 CatDB 能够更有效地利用 LLM 编码功能。我们的评估表明，CatDB 的性能优于现有的基于 LLM 和 AutoML 系统，在大型数据集上运行速度提高了几个数量级。观众将使用各种真实数据集体验 CatDB 与商业和开源 LLM 的功能，如我们的演示视频和 Colab 笔记本中所示。

items.title: First Workshop Connecting Academia and Industry on Modern Integrated Database and AI Systems (MIDAS).
items.abstract: The MIDAS workshop is designed to foster meaningful collaboration between researchers and industry practitioners by identifying and addressing complex challenges in the field of Generative AI (GenAI) and Data. These challenges often require a longer-term research perspective while simultaneously needing to remain grounded in real-world constraints and operational scenarios.
items.abstract_zh: MIDAS 研讨会旨在通过识别和解决生成人工智能 (GenAI) 和数据领域的复杂挑战，促进研究人员和行业从业者之间有意义的合作。这些挑战通常需要长期的研究视角，同时需要立足于现实世界的限制和操作场景。

items.title: UDFBench: A Tool for Benchmarking UDF Queries on SQL Engines.
items.abstract: User-defined functions (UDFs) extend SQL with functional capabilities. However, integrating UDFs efficiently into a data engine is a challenge, mainly due to the mismatch of the UDF execution and SQL processing environments. Several techniques have been proposed to deal with this challenge spanning a variety of logical and physical optimizations, and architecture designs. These however are not evident to the casual user, who would benefit from knowing the pros and cons of the available solutions. Towards this direction, we present UDFBench , a benchmarking toolkit for running seamlessly UDF queries (queries with UDFs) on a variety of SQL engines and presenting to the user execution results accompanied with highlights of the main reasons driving the query performance. UDFBench comprises a set of queries and UDFs of varying complexity based on a real-world application and data. Our current implementation has been deployed on four popular database engines with different characteristics. Our pluggable design enables extending UDFBench to additional systems and workloads.
items.abstract_zh: 用户定义函数 (UDF) 通过函数功能扩展 SQL。然而，将 UDF 有效地集成到数据引擎中是一个挑战，这主要是由于 UDF 执行和 SQL 处理环境的不匹配。已经提出了几种技术来应对这一挑战，涵盖各种逻辑和物理优化以及架构设计。然而，这些对于临时用户来说并不明显，他们会从了解可用解决方案的优缺点中受益。朝着这个方向，我们推出了 UDFBench，这是一个基准测试工具包，用于在各种 SQL 引擎上无缝运行 UDF 查询（使用 UDF 的查询），并向用户呈现执行结果，并重点介绍驱动查询性能的主要原因。 UDFBench 包含一组基于实际应用程序和数据的不同复杂度的查询和 UDF。我们当前的实现已部署在四种具有不同特征的流行数据库引擎上。我们的可插拔设计可以将 UDFBench 扩展到其他系统和工作负载。

items.title: GES: High-Performance Graph Processing Engine and Service in Huawei.
items.abstract: This paper presents the Graph Engine Service (GES), an advanced graph database management system characterized by its composable architecture and highly factorized query executor. This design enables GES to manage a substantial volume of concurrent queries with remarkable efficiency. GES currently holds the top position in the authoritative LDBC-SNB-DECLARATIVE benchmark rankings, showcasing its superior performance - exceeding the throughput of the second-place system by over three orders of magnitude, which provides its customers with exceptional cost-effectiveness and extraordinary user experiences.
items.abstract_zh: 本文提出了图形引擎服务（GES），这是一种先进的图形数据库管理系统，其特点是可组合架构和高度分解的查询执行器。这种设计使 GES 能够以卓越的效率管理大量并发查询。目前，GES在权威的LDBC-SNB-DECLARATIVE基准排名中位居榜首，展示了其卓越的性能——超出第二名系统吞吐量三个数量级以上，为客户提供了卓越的成本效益和非凡的用户体验。

items.title: SwellDB: Dynamic Query-Driven Table Generation with Large Language Models.
items.abstract: We present SwellDB, a data system that enables analytical query processing over tables generated dynamically with Large Language Models (LLMs). Typically, traditional database systems function under the closed-world assumption, allowing query execution over already existing tables. In contrast, SwellDB dynamically generates the required tables based on input SQL queries, and user-defined table definitions. Powered by an LLM, SwellDB integrates external data sources, including diverse datasets, database systems, and search engines to extract and blend relevant information into a structured table. Given an input query and schema, SwellDB intelligently selects data sources, synthesizes structured tables adhering to the specified schema, and makes them queriable through its execution engine. During the demonstration, we will showcase how SwellDB addresses data generation and integration challenges across domains such as web search and bioinformatics. Attendees can define custom schemas and execute SQL queries, gaining hands-on experience with SwellDB's table generation capabilities. Video URL: https://youtu.be/X1VOCVYb3Sc
items.abstract_zh: 我们推出了 SwellDB，这是一个数据系统，可以对使用大型语言模型 (LLM) 动态生成的表进行分析查询处理。通常，传统数据库系统在封闭世界假设下运行，允许对现有表执行查询。相比之下，SwellDB 根据输入 SQL 查询和用户定义的表定义动态生成所需的表。在法学硕士的支持下，SwellDB 集成了外部数据源，包括不同的数据集、数据库系统和搜索引擎，以提取相关信息并将其混合到结构化表中。给定输入查询和模式，SwellDB 智能地选择数据源，合成符合指定模式的结构化表，并使其可通过其执行引擎进行查询。在演示过程中，我们将展示 SwellDB 如何解决跨领域（例如网络搜索和生物信息学）的数据生成和集成挑战。与会者可以定义自定义模式并执行 SQL 查询，获得 SwellDB 的表生成功能的实践经验。视频网址：https://youtu.be/X1VOCVYb3Sc

items.title: Demonstration of DPClustX: Differentially Private Explanations for Clusters.
items.abstract: We present DPClustX, a framework designed to generate Differential Privacy (DP)-compliant histogram-based explanations for black-box clustering results. Interpreting clustering results is challenging even without privacy constraints, and the challenge is amplified under DP, as analysts receive noisy query responses. By addressing these challenges, DPClustX enables the selection of high-quality explaining attributes and the generation of informative histograms that balance privacy and utility. Compatible with any DP-clustering algorithm, our framework provides explanations that uncover significant patterns even under strict privacy constraints. This demonstration highlights DPClustX's capabilities using real datasets, illustrating its practical utility in sensitive data analysis and its potential for enhancing the interpretability of clustering methods.
items.abstract_zh: 我们提出了 DPClustX，这是一个旨在为黑盒聚类结果生成符合差分隐私 (DP) 的基于直方图的解释的框架。即使没有隐私限制，解释聚类结果也充满挑战，而在 DP 下，随着分析师收到嘈杂的查询响应，这一挑战会被放大。通过解决这些挑战，DPClustX 能够选择高质量的解释属性并生成平衡隐私和实用性的信息直方图。我们的框架与任何 DP 聚类算法兼容，提供的解释即使在严格的隐私限制下也能发现重要的模式。该演示强调了 DPClustX 使用真实数据集的功能，说明了其在敏感数据分析中的实用性及其增强聚类方法可解释性的潜力。

items.title: Ninth Workshop on Data Management for End-to-End Machine Learning (DEEM).
items.abstract: The DEEM'25 workshop (Data Management for End-to-End Machine Learning) is held on Friday, June 27th, in conjunction with SIGMOD/PODS 2025. DEEM brings together researchers and practitioners at the intersection of applied machine learning, data management, and systems research, with the goal of discussing the arising data management issues in ML application scenarios. The workshop solicits regular research papers (8 pages) describing preliminary and ongoing research results, including industrial experience reports of end-to-end ML deployments, related to DEEM topics. In addition, DEEM 2025 has a category for short papers (4 pages) as a forum for sharing interesting use cases, problems, datasets, benchmarks, visionary ideas, system designs, preliminary results, and descriptions of system components and tools related to end-to-end ML pipelines. This year, the workshop received 18 high-quality submissions on diverse topics relevant to DEEM.
items.abstract_zh: DEEM'25 研讨会（端到端机器学习的数据管理）于 6 月 27 日星期五与 SIGMOD/PODS 2025 同期举行。DEEM 汇集了应用机器学习、数据管理和系统研究交叉领域的研究人员和从业者，旨在讨论 ML 应用场景中出现的数据管理问题。该研讨会征集定期研究论文（8 页），描述初步和正在进行的研究成果，包括与 DEEM 主题相关的端到端 ML 部署的行业经验报告。此外，DEEM 2025 还有一个短论文类别（4 页）作为论坛，用于分享有趣的用例、问题、数据集、基准、远见卓识、系统设计、初步结果以及与端到端 ML 管道相关的系统组件和工具的描述。今年，研讨会收到了 18 份高质量的提交材料，涉及与 DEEM 相关的不同主题。

items.title: AutoComp: Automated Data Compaction for Log-Structured Tables in Data Lakes.
items.abstract: The proliferation of small files in data lakes poses significant challenges, including degraded query performance, increased storage costs, and scalability bottlenecks in distributed storage systems. Log-structured table formats (LSTs) such as Delta Lake, Apache Iceberg, and Apache Hudi exacerbate this issue due to their append-only write patterns and metadata-intensive operations. While compaction--the process of consolidating small files into fewer, larger files--is a common solution, existing automation mechanisms often lack the flexibility and scalability to adapt to diverse workloads and system requirements while balancing the trade-offs between compaction benefits and costs. In this paper, we present AutoComp, a scalable framework for automatic data compaction tailored to the needs of modern data lakes. Drawing on deployment experience at LinkedIn, we analyze the operational impact of small file proliferation, establish key requirements for effective automatic compaction, and demonstrate how AutoComp addresses these challenges. Our evaluation, conducted using synthetic benchmarks and production environments via integration with OpenHouse--a control plane for catalog management, schema governance, and data services--shows significant improvements in file count reduction and query performance. We believe AutoComp's built-in extensibility provides a robust foundation for evolving compaction systems, facilitating future integration of refined multi-objective optimization approaches, workload-aware compaction strategies, and expanded support for broader data layout optimizations.
items.abstract_zh: 数据湖中小文件的激增带来了重大挑战，包括查询性能下降、存储成本增加以及分布式存储系统的可扩展性瓶颈。 Delta Lake、Apache Iceberg 和 Apache Hudi 等日志结构表格式 (LST) 由于其仅追加写入模式和元数据密集型操作而加剧了此问题。虽然压缩（将小文件合并为更少、更大的文件的过程）是一种常见的解决方案，但现有的自动化机制通常缺乏灵活性和可扩展性，无法适应不同的工作负载和系统要求，同时平衡压缩收益和成本之间的权衡。在本文中，我们提出了 AutoComp，这是一个可扩展的框架，用于根据现代数据湖的需求定制自动数据压缩。借鉴 LinkedIn 的部署经验，我们分析了小文件扩散对运营的影响，建立了有效自动压缩的关键要求，并演示了 AutoComp 如何应对这些挑战。我们的评估是通过与 OpenHouse（用于目录管理、模式治理和数据服务的控制平面）集成，使用综合基准和生产环境进行的，结果显示文件数量减少和查询性能有了显着改进。我们相信，AutoComp 的内置可扩展性为不断发展的压缩系统提供了坚实的基础，有助于未来集成精细的多目标优化方法、工作负载感知压缩策略以及对更广泛的数据布局优化的扩展支持。

items.title: Databricks Lakeguard: Supporting Fine-grained Access Control and Multi-user Capabilities for Apache Spark Workloads.
items.abstract: Today, upgrading Apache Spark versions typically involves significant effort, with unclear investment requirements, including trial and error. This is mainly because in Spark there is no clear separation between the application code and the engine code. Apart from changes in the public API, any Spark internal changes may affect user workloads as users may rely on Spark internals: bug fixes in the Spark engine, changes to internal APIs, library upgrades, or language upgrades may affect customer workloads. As a result, Spark users are often reluctant to upgrade. The downside is that performance improvements, bug fixes, and new features take significantly longer to adopt, preventing customers from quickly benefiting from these improvements. In addition, it increases engineering complexity to manage a large number of different Spark versions. For Databricks serverless jobs and notebooks, we fundamentally transformed and simplified the user experience when using Spark. We shifted user focus from managing Spark runtime versions to managing the stable API that they integrate with - we created client-versioned workloads with a versionless Spark server. Decoupling the client from the Spark engine using Spark Connect has enabled Databricks to automatically upgrade the Spark server, providing users faster access to the latest features while minimizing disruptions from both intentional and unintentional breaking changes, all without compromising workload compatibility and with zero code changes needed from the user. This approach also offers significant benefits to Databricks by streamlining the release process, consolidating usage onto fewer server versions, and reducing engineering overhead from needing to backport changes. In this demonstration, we will first briefly introduce the architectural foundation of versionless Spark, leveraging Databricks' multi-user Spark compute and Spark Connect, followed by describing in more detail how we manage seamless upgrades for our customers, and finally talk about what the user experience is.
items.abstract_zh: 如今，升级 Apache Spark 版本通常需要付出巨大的努力，且投资要求不明确，包括反复试验。这主要是因为在 Spark 中，应用程序代码和引擎代码之间没有明确的分离。除了公共 API 的更改之外，任何 Spark 内部更改都可能会影响用户工作负载，因为用户可能依赖 Spark 内部结构：Spark 引擎中的错误修复、内部 API 的更改、库升级或语言升级可能会影响客户工作负载。因此，Spark 用户往往不愿意升级。缺点是性能改进、错误修复和新功能的采用需要更长的时间，从而阻止客户快速从这些改进中受益。此外，管理大量不同的 Spark 版本也增加了工程复杂性。对于 Databricks 无服务器作业和笔记本，我们从根本上改变并简化了使用 Spark 时的用户体验。我们将用户关注点从管理 Spark 运行时版本转移到管理与其集成的稳定 API - 我们使用无版本 Spark 服务器创建了客户端版本化工作负载。使用 Spark Connect 将客户端与 Spark 引擎解耦，使 Databricks 能够自动升级 Spark 服务器，使用户能够更快地访问最新功能，同时最大限度地减少有意和无意的重大更改造成的中断，所有这些都不会影响工作负载兼容性，并且用户所需的代码更改为零。这种方法还通过简化发布流程、将使用整合到更少的服务器版本以及减少因需要向后移植更改而产生的工程开销，为 Databricks 带来显着的好处。在本次演示中，我们将首先简要介绍无版本 Spark 的架构基础，利用 Databricks 的多用户 Spark 计算和 Spark Connect，然后更详细地描述我们如何为客户管理无缝升级，最后讨论用户体验是什么。

items.title: JSON Relational Duality: A Revolutionary Combination of Document, Object, and Relational Models.
items.abstract:
JSON Relational Duality is a novel solution to the object-relational impedance mismatch problem. By decoupling the data access model from the data storage model, application data can be read and written in terms of application-friendly hierarchical JSON documents using Duality Views , even when the underlying data is stored in the form of normalized relational tables (that can include JSON columns for schema flexibility). When data is stored natively as JSON documents, shared data is duplicated across many documents. With duality views, different JSON documents can efficiently share the same underlying data without any data duplication, or the risk of data divergence. Duality views also use a novel value-based optimistic concurrency control protocol that enables stateless APIs such as REST to be used across multiple reads and writes with full transactional consistency. JSON Relational Duality is a transformative breakthrough for application development that preserves the best of both worlds: Developer-friendly JSON-oriented access to application objects, combined with the power (efficiency, integrity, and composability) of the relational model.

This paper describes the concepts of JSON Relational Duality and various aspects of its implementation in Oracle Database Release 23ai. It also poses some interesting questions for further research.
items.abstract_zh:
JSON Relational Duality 是解决对象关系阻抗不匹配问题的新颖解决方案。通过将数据访问模型与数据存储模型解耦，即使底层数据以规范化关系表的形式存储（可以包括 JSON 列以实现架构灵活性），也可以使用 Duality Views 以应用程序友好的分层 JSON 文档的形式读取和写入应用程序数据。当数据本地存储为 JSON 文档时，共享数据会在许多文档中重复。通过对偶视图，不同的 JSON 文档可以有效地共享相同的底层数据，而不会出现任何数据重复或数据发散的风险。对偶视图还使用一种新颖的基于值的乐观并发控制协议，该协议使无状态 API（例如 REST）能够在多个读取和写入中使用，并具有完全事务一致性。 JSON 关系二元性是应用程序开发的革命性突破，它保留了两全其美的优点：开发人员友好的、面向 JSON 的应用程序对象访问，与关系模型的强大功能（效率、完整性和可组合性）相结合。

本文介绍了 JSON 关系对偶性的概念及其在 Oracle 数据库版本 23ai 中实现的各个方面。它还提出了一些需要进一步研究的有趣问题。

items.title: ShapX Engine: A Demonstration of Shapley Value Approximations.
items.abstract: Interpreting decisions made by machine learning models helps build trust in their predictions, ultimately facilitating their practical application. Shapley values have emerged as a popular and theoretically robust method for interpreting models by quantifying the contribution of each feature toward individual predictions. The inherent complexity associated with the computation of Shapley values as an NP-hard problem has driven the development of numerous approximation techniques, leading to a plethora of options in literature. This abundance of choices has created a substantial gap in determining the most appropriate approach for practical applications. To address this gap, we propose ShapX, a web engine that comprehensively evaluates 17 approximation methods across diverse regression and classification tasks. ShapX facilitates an interactive exploration of the strengths and limitations of various Shapley value approximations by guiding users through the suitable selections of replacement and tractable estimation strategies. Ultimately, our study reveals that strategies competent at capturing all the feature interactions leading to accurate estimations of Shapley values. ShapX also allows users to effortlessly upload their own dataset along with the corresponding machine learning model, enabling them to obtain detailed individualized explanations. A detailed walkthrough video of the demonstration is available online. Video link: https://youtu.be/5uPocjPUAA8.
items.abstract_zh: 解释机器学习模型做出的决策有助于建立对其预测的信任，最终促进其实际应用。沙普利值已成为一种流行且理论上可靠的方法，用于通过量化每个特征对个体预测的贡献来解释模型。作为 NP 难题，与 Shapley 值计算相关的固有复杂性推动了众多近似技术的发展，导致文献中出现了大量的选择。如此丰富的选择在确定最适合实际应用的方法方面造成了巨大的差距。为了解决这一差距，我们提出了 ShapX，这是一个 Web 引擎，可以综合评估跨不同回归和分类任务的 17 种近似方法。 ShapX 通过引导用户选择合适的替换和易于处理的估计策略，促进对各种 Shapley 值近似的优点和局限性的交互式探索。最终，我们的研究表明，能够捕获所有特征交互的策略可以准确估计 Shapley 值。 ShapX还允许用户轻松上传自己的数据集以及相应的机器学习模型，使他们能够获得详细的个性化解释。在线提供了演示的详细演练视频。视频链接：https://youtu.be/5uPocjPUAA8。

items.title: OmniTune: A Universal Framework for Query Refinement via LLMs.
items.abstract: Numerous studies have proposed solutions for SQL query refinement, where the goal is to make minimal adjustments to an input query to satisfy a given set of constraints. While effective, these approaches typically address specific query types and constraints, whereas, in practice, users may need to refine a diverse range of queries based on their requirements. To address this, we present OmniTune, a universal framework for query refinement. OmniTune features a Refinement Problem Wizard for defining refinement tasks in natural language and a flexible Refinement Engine, which employs an LLM-based multi-agent architecture to support any query refinement problem. We demonstrate OmniTune across various query refinement scenarios using real-world datasets.
items.abstract_zh: 许多研究提出了 SQL 查询细化的解决方案，其目标是对输入查询进行最小的调整以满足一组给定的约束。虽然有效，但这些方法通常解决特定的查询类型和约束，而在实践中，用户可能需要根据自己的要求细化各种查询。为了解决这个问题，我们推出了 OmniTune，一个用于查询细化的通用框架。 OmniTune 具有用于以自然语言定义细化任务的细化问题向导和灵活的细化引擎，该引擎采用基于 LLM 的多代理架构来支持任何查询细化问题。我们使用真实数据集在各种查询细化场景中演示 OmniTune。

items.title: anagodb: Offering Massive Parallelism for Database Engine.
items.abstract: Parallelism is a primary factor in exploiting the potential capability of modern computer systems having a number of processing cores and storage chips/spindles. The database community has exerted much effort to achieve good parallelism in query processing to shorten latency and/or improve throughput. One approach is out-of-order database execution (OoODE), which dynamically and recursively decomposes the query work into tasks; it divides a parent task into child tasks if the parent task is dividable, and eventually, it divides the query work into a massive number of tasks. Hence, this mechanism can exploit the parallelism of the underlying computer infrastructure, specifically leveraging the potential computational capacity and/or the potential IO bandwidth. In particular, OoODE offers significant speedups for selective analytical queries. This paper presents anagodb , a research prototype database engine that we have developed for exploring the implementation of OoODE. anagodb has the capability of processing a wide variety of analytical queries according to the OoODE mechanism. Our demonstration will present how anagodb runs analytical queries on a cloud server. The visual client tool reports the query progress and the hardware performance metrics at runtime, clarifying that anagodb exploits the potential hardware power effectively.
items.abstract_zh: 并行性是利用具有多个处理核心和存储芯片/主轴的现代计算机系统的潜在能力的主要因素。数据库社区付出了很大的努力来在查询处理中实现良好的并行性，以缩短延迟和/或提高吞吐量。一种方法是乱序数据库执行（OoODE），它将查询工作动态地、递归地分解为任务；如果父任务可分割，则将父任务分割为子任务，最终将查询工作分割为大量任务。因此，该机制可以利用底层计算机基础设施的并行性，特别是利用潜在的计算能力和/或潜在的 IO 带宽。特别是，OoODE 为选择性分析查询提供了显着的加速。本文介绍了 anagodb，这是我们为探索 OoODE 的实现而开发的研究原型数据库引擎。 anagodb 能够根据 OoODE 机制处理各种分析查询。我们的演示将展示 anagodb 如何在云服务器上运行分析查询。可视化客户端工具报告运行时的查询进度和硬件性能指标，阐明anagodb有效地利用了潜在的硬件能力。

items.title: ChARLES: Change-Aware Recovery of Latent Evolution Semantics in Relational Data.
items.abstract:
Data-driven decision-making is at the core of many modern applications, and understanding the data is critical in supporting trust in these decisions. However, data is dynamic and evolving, just like the real-world entities it represents. Thus, an important component of understanding data is analyzing and drawing insights from the changes it undergoes. Existing methods for exploring data change list differences exhaustively, which are not interpretable by humans, and fail to provide salient insights into change trends.

We demonstrate ChARLES, a system that derives semantic summaries of changes between two snapshots of an evolving database, in an effective, concise, and interpretable way. Our key observation is that while datasets often evolve through point and other small-batch updates, rich data features can reveal latent semantics that can intuitively summarize the changes. Under the hood, ChARLES compares database versions, infers feasible transformations by fitting multiple regression lines over different data partitions to derive change summaries, and ranks them. ChARLES allows users to customize the ranking to obtain their preferred explanation by navigating the accuracy-interpretability tradeoff, and offers a proof of concept for reasoning about data evolution over real-world datasets.
items.abstract_zh:
数据驱动的决策是许多现代应用程序的核心，理解数据对于支持对这些决策的信任至关重要。然而，数据是动态的、不断发展的，就像它所代表的现实世界实体一样。因此，理解数据的一个重要组成部分是分析数据所经历的变化并从中汲取见解。现有的探索数据变化的方法详尽地列出了人类无法解释的差异，并且无法提供对变化趋势的显着洞察。

我们演示了 ChARLES，这是一个以有效、简洁和可解释的方式导出不断发展的数据库的两个快照之间变化的语义摘要的系统。我们的主要观察是，虽然数据集通常通过点更新和其他小批量更新而演变，但丰富的数据特征可以揭示潜在的语义，从而可以直观地总结变化。 Under the hood, ChARLES compares database versions, infers feasible transformations by fitting multiple regression lines over different data partitions to derive change summaries, and ranks them. ChARLES 允许用户通过精确度与可解释性权衡来定制排名，以获得他们喜欢的解释，并为推理现实世界数据集上的数据演化提供概念证明。

items.title: Mosaic: An Architecture for Linking Databases and Scalable Interactive Visualizations.
items.abstract: Real-time interaction and visualization over large data volumes requires careful coordination of data queries and visual updates. Mosaic is an architecture for optimizing scalable and interoperable visualizations backed by a database, providing a platform for developing and deploying optimizations that span both visualization clients and backing databases. Mosaic applications consist of data-consuming clients that publish data needs as declarative queries, parameterized by shared filtering selections. These queries are managed and automatically optimized by a coordinator that proxies access to a scalable data store. For example, by analyzing selection predicates and client queries, the coordinator automatically constructs materialized views to perform selection updates over pre-aggregated data at interactive rates. Given only a high-level specification, Mosaic automatically enables orders-of-magnitude performance improvements over standard update queries.
items.abstract_zh: 大数据量的实时交互和可视化需要仔细协调数据查询和可视化更新。 Mosaic 是一种用于优化由数据库支持的可扩展和可互操作可视化的架构，提供了一个用于开发和部署跨可视化客户端和支持数据库的优化的平台。 Mosaic 应用程序由数据消耗客户端组成，这些客户端将数据需求发布为声明性查询，并通过共享过滤选择进行参数化。这些查询由代理对可扩展数据存储的访问的协调器进行管理和自动优化。例如，通过分析选择谓词和客户端查询，协调器自动构建物化视图，以交互速率对预聚合数据执行选择更新。只需给出高级规范，Mosaic 就会自动实现比标准更新查询数量级的性能改进。

items.title: Scalable Execution of Application Logic within Everest BusinessStore.
items.abstract:
Relational databases with SQL as the querying language are still the gold standard for developing business applications. Complex logic is often expressed using application code which involves multiple database queries resulting in higher latencies and less optimization potential for the database. Moreover, while modern web development is increasingly relying on JSON-based interfaces, database queries return identically structured rows further exacerbating the long-standing impedance mismatch.

In this paper, we introduce Everest BusinessStore which aims to simplify application development by providing a JSON-based interface to relational databases as well as a calculation language that allows to express complex business logic. The main challenge is the efficient mapping of application logic into highly efficient SQL code, which is demanding due to the high complexity of business applications and thus the calculations. Based on the real-world applications developed at Everest, we demonstrate that our approach offers speedups of up to two orders of magnitude compared to a straightforward mapping of application logic to SQL.
items.abstract_zh:
以 SQL 作为查询语言的关系数据库仍然是开发业务应用程序的黄金标准。复杂的逻辑通常使用涉及多个数据库查询的应用程序代码来表达，从而导致更高的延迟和更少的数据库优化潜力。此外，虽然现代 Web 开发越来越依赖基于 JSON 的接口，但数据库查询返回相同结构的行，进一步加剧了长期存在的阻抗不匹配。

在本文中，我们介绍了 Everest BusinessStore，它旨在通过提供基于 JSON 的关系数据库接口以及允许表达复杂业务逻辑的计算语言来简化应用程序开发。主要挑战是将应用程序逻辑有效映射为高效的 SQL 代码，由于业务应用程序和计算的复杂性很高，因此要求很高。基于在 Everest 开发的实际应用程序，我们证明了与应用程序逻辑到 SQL 的直接映射相比，我们的方法可提供高达两个数量级的加速。

items.title: OLTP Engines on Modern Storage Architectures.
items.abstract:
Online transaction processing (OLTP) engines are crucial components of database systems, facing significant challenges due to the rapid growth of data on the Internet. Memory-oriented OLTP engines struggle with the cost and capacity limitations of hosting large data volumes, while disk-oriented engines suffer performance degradation when their working set size exceeds available memory. Limitations in DRAM density and disk latency hinder scalability and service quality, complicating large-scale data management and transaction execution.

In recent years, advancements in storage architecture, such as persistent memory, NVMe SSDs, and CXL, help alleviate these memory and I/O pressures by bridging the performance gap between DRAM and traditional block storage devices or efficiently expanding memory pools. These technologies are used to enhance and accelerate OLTP engines, with emerging storage hardware and protocols offering improved scalability and remote access.

Despite these innovations, new challenges arise, requiring advanced data management strategies in dynamic environments. This tutorial provides an overview of modern OLTP engines leveraging cutting-edge storage solutions, exploring storage hierarchies, protocols, and programming models that offer insights for researchers and industry professionals. Additionally, it highlights the challenges and opportunities presented by emerging storage architectures for OLTP engines.
items.abstract_zh:
在线事务处理（OLTP）引擎是数据库系统的重要组成部分，由于互联网上数据的快速增长而面临着巨大的挑战。面向内存的 OLTP 引擎面临托管大数据量的成本和容量限制，而面向磁盘的引擎在工作集大小超过可用内存时会遭受性能下降。 DRAM 密度和磁盘延迟的限制阻碍了可扩展性和服务质量，使大规模数据管理和事务执行变得复杂。

近年来，持久内存、NVMe SSD 和 CXL 等存储架构的进步，通过缩小 DRAM 和传统块存储设备之间的性能差距或有效扩展内存池，有助于缓解这些内存和 I/O 压力。这些技术用于增强和加速 OLTP 引擎，新兴的存储硬件和协议可提供改进的可扩展性和远程访问。

尽管有这些创新，但还是出现了新的挑战，需要在动态环境中采用先进的数据管理策略。本教程概述了利用尖端存储解决方案的现代 OLTP 引擎，探索存储层次结构、协议和编程模型，为研究人员和行业专业人士提供见解。此外，它还强调了新兴存储架构为 OLTP 引擎带来的挑战和机遇。

items.title: Zorro: Quantifying Uncertainty in Models & Predictions Arising from Dirty Data.
items.abstract: Machine learning models are increasingly employed in high-stakes decision making in domains such as personalized medicine, policing,and many others. As data quality issues are prevalent and recovering a ground truth clean dataset is often impossible or prohibitively expense, heuristic cleaning techniques are employed in practice to clean training data. The net result are models whose predictions can fundamentally not be trusted as we do not know how much the model's predictions differ from a model trained on the unknown ground truth clean data. We present Zorro, a principled framework for modeling the uncertainty in model parameters and predictions arising from the multiplicity of datasets that could feasibly be the ground truth clean version of a dirty training / test dataset. Under the hood, Zorro employs a novel framework for training and prediction with linear models over uncertain data. Given training and test datasets that are subject to data quality issues, we compute a sound over-approximation of all possible models, the set of models generated by training a model on each possible clean version of the dataset, and then over-approximate all possible predictions based on these models. Using Zorro, we can certify the robustness of models, i.e., to what degree are the model parameters impacted by data quality issues, and of individual and aggregated predictions. The demonstration video is available at https://drive.google.com/file/d/1gxkvRY3pLM0ATco2qvBnM1EcR8-U9F5h/view?usp=sharing.
items.abstract_zh: 机器学习模型越来越多地应用于个性化医疗、警务等领域的高风险决策。由于数据质量问题普遍存在，并且恢复真实的干净数据集通常是不可能的或费用高昂，因此在实践中采用启发式清理技术来清理训练数据。最终结果是模型的预测从根本上不可信，因为我们不知道模型的预测与在未知的真实干净数据上训练的模型有多大差异。我们提出了 Zorro，一个原则框架，用于对模型参数的不确定性和由多个数据集产生的预测进行建模，这些数据集可能是脏训练/测试数据集的地面真实干净版本。在幕后，佐罗采用了一种新颖的框架，通过不确定数据的线性模型进行训练和预测。给定受数据质量问题影响的训练和测试数据集，我们计算所有可能模型的合理过度近似，即通过在数据集的每个可能的干净版本上训练模型而生成的模型集，然后基于这些模型过度近似所有可能的预测。使用 Zorro，我们可以证明模型的稳健性，即模型参数受数据质量问题以及个体和聚合预测影响的程度。演示视频位于 https://drive.google.com/file/d/1gxkvRY3pLM0ATco2qvBnM1EcR8-U9F5h/view?usp=sharing。

items.title: A Query-Aware Enormous Database Generator For System Performance Evaluation.
items.abstract: In production, simulating the real application without exposing the privacy data is essential for database benchmarking or performance debugging. A rich body of query-aware database generators (QAG) are proposed for this purpose. The complex data dependencies hidden behind queries make previous work suffer from critical deficiencies in supporting complex operators with high simulation accuracy. To fill the gap between the existing QAGs and the urgent demands, we implement a data generator M irage with the attractive characteristics of reproducing applications based on the queries even with complex operators and having a theoretical zero error. Specifically,M irage leverages Query Rewriting and Set Transforming Rules to decouple the implicit dependencies from queries, which greatly simplify the generation problem; it presents a uniform representation of various join types and formulates key population as a Constraint Programming (CP) problem, which can be well solved by an off-the-shelf CP Solver . In this demonstration, users can explore the core features of M irage in generating synthetic databases, which has the widest support to operators and the best simulation fidelity compared to the related work.
items.abstract_zh: 在生产中，在不暴露隐私数据的情况下模拟真实应用程序对于数据库基准测试或性能调试至关重要。为此目的，提出了丰富的查询感知数据库生成器（QAG）。隐藏在查询背后的复杂数据依赖关系使得之前的工作在支持高模拟精度的复杂算子方面存在严重缺陷。为了填补现有 QAG 和迫切需求之间的差距，我们实现了一种数据生成器 Mirage，它具有基于查询重现应用程序的有吸引力的特性，即使使用复杂的运算符，并且理论上为零错误。具体来说，M irage 利用查询重写和设置转换规则来解耦查询中的隐式依赖关系，从而大大简化了生成问题；它提供了各种连接类型的统一表示，并将关键群体制定为约束规划（CP）问题，可以通过现成的 CP 求解器很好地解决。在本次演示中，用户可以探索 Mirage 在生成合成数据库方面的核心功能，与相关工作相比，它对操作员的支持最广泛，模拟保真度也最好。

items.title: Unified Lineage System: Tracking Data Provenance at Scale.
items.abstract: As businesses increasingly rely on large-scale data analysis to build products and features, understanding how data flows through heterogeneous storage assets has become a critical challenge. To address these challenges, we present ULS, an end-to-end lineage aggregator designed to track data flows at scale. Our system features a general data model representing data flows between different asset types, a lineage data model that enables navigation across granularities, and techniques to stitch incomplete lineage traces. We also describe methods for processing the lineage graph to customize precision and recall. We implemented ULS at Meta, where its lineage graph contains billions of nodes and edges and supports over one hundred use cases, including routine operations, capacity management and privacy.
items.abstract_zh: 随着企业越来越依赖大规模数据分析来构建产品和功能，了解数据如何流经异构存储资产已成为一项关键挑战。为了应对这些挑战，我们推出了 ULS，这是一种端到端的谱系聚合器，旨在大规模跟踪数据流。我们的系统具有表示不同资产类型之间数据流的通用数据模型、支持跨粒度导航的沿袭数据模型以及缝合不完整沿袭痕迹的技术。我们还描述了处理谱系图以定制精度和召回率的方法。我们在 Meta 实施了 ULS，其谱系图包含数十亿个节点和边，并支持一百多个用例，包括日常操作、容量管理和隐私。

items.title: Demonstrating CEDAR: A System for Cost-Efficient Data-Driven Claim Verification.
items.abstract: We present CEDAR, a system for cost-efficient, data-driven claim verification using large language models (LLMs). Given a collection of text documents containing claims that can be verified from relational data, CEDAR maps claims to SQL queries for automated verification. The system employs multiple verification approaches, from cost-effective zero-shot LLM invocations to more accurate but expensive iterative, agent-based methods. CEDAR dynamically selects and combines these methods, optimizing for accuracy and cost through cost-based optimization and parameter tuning. Visitors can interact with CEDAR to observe its behavior across various scenarios and data sets. They can explore how the system prioritizes verification approaches, starting with inexpensive methods and escalating to more advanced ones as needed. Users can experiment with CEDAR by modifying claims, data sets, and tuning parameters, observing how these changes influence verification accuracy and cost. Additionally, visitors can upload their own data sets to experience CEDAR's capabilities in real-world claim verification tasks. The demonstration highlights CEDAR's adaptability and its ability to balance cost and accuracy in customizable claim verification workflows.
items.abstract_zh: 我们推出了 CEDAR，这是一种使用大型语言模型 (LLM) 进行经济高效、数据驱动的索赔验证的系统。给定包含可以从关系数据验证的声明的文本文档集合，CEDAR 将声明映射到 SQL 查询以进行自动验证。该系统采用多种验证方法，从经济高效的零样本 LLM 调用到更准确但昂贵的基于代理的迭代方法。 CEDAR 动态选择和组合这些方法，通过基于成本的优化和参数调整来优化准确性和成本。访问者可以与 CEDAR 交互，观察其在各种场景和数据集中的行为。他们可以探索系统如何优先考虑验证方法，从廉价的方法开始，根据需要升级到更先进的方法。用户可以通过修改声明、数据集和调整参数来试验 CEDAR，观察这些变化如何影响验证准确性和成本。此外，访问者还可以上传自己的数据集，以体验 CEDAR 在现实世界索赔验证任务中的功能。该演示强调了 CEDAR 的适应性及其在可定制索赔验证工作流程中平衡成本和准确性的能力。

items.title: VQLens: A Demonstration of Vector Query Execution Analysis.
items.abstract: With the increasing demand for high-speed similarity search over massive datasets, vector databases have become essential in modern data-intensive applications, particularly in LLMs and other AI-driven workloads. The growing complexity and scale of vector database operations demand more comprehensive evaluation methodologies. However, existing evaluation methodologies primarily rely on high-level information retrieval metrics (e.g., recall, accuracy) and system performance indicators (e.g., query latency, throughput), which fail to provide fine-grained insights. This limitation hinders the diagnosis of unexpected query results and the optimization of system performance. To bridge this gap, we present VQLens , an interactive visual analytics system for in-depth exploration of query execution traces and large-scale vector distributions. Specifically, VQLens (1) computes the overall data distribution and overlays query traces to reveal retrieval patterns, (2) visualizes query execution trace using multiple representations to enhance interpretability, and (3) enables interactive multi-view exploration, supporting both global pattern discovery and case-specific analysis. VQLens is open-source at https://github.com/DBGroup-SUSTech/VQLens.
items.abstract_zh: 随着对海量数据集的高速相似性搜索的需求不断增加，矢量数据库在现代数据密集型应用程序中变得至关重要，特别是在法学硕士和其他人工智能驱动的工作负载中。矢量数据库操作的复杂性和规模不断增长，需要更全面的评估方法。然而，现有的评估方法主要依赖于高级信息检索指标（例如召回率、准确性）和系统性能指标（例如查询延迟、吞吐量），无法提供细粒度的洞察。这种限制阻碍了对意外查询结果的诊断和系统性能的优化。为了弥补这一差距，我们推出了 VQLens，这是一种交互式可视化分析系统，用于深入探索查询执行轨迹和大规模向量分布。具体来说，VQLens (1) 计算整体数据分布并覆盖查询跟踪以揭示检索模式，(2) 使用多种表示形式可视化查询执行跟踪以增强可解释性，(3) 实现交互式多视图探索，支持全局模式发现和特定于案例的分析。 VQLens 已开源，网址为 https://github.com/DBGroup-SUSTech/VQLens。

items.title: LingoDB-CT: Understanding LingoDB's Inner Workings.
items.abstract:
While compiling query engines can be highly efficient, especially for complex queries, they also have the reputation of being too difficult to understand, debug, and profile. However, this is not necessarily the case if supported by the right architecture and tools.

With this demonstration, we want to show this for our own compiling query engine LingoDB. For this purpose, we built an instrumentation and visualization framework called LingoDB-CT, which aggregates data from multiple angles and visualizes it to reveal the bigger picture. Through two interactive demonstration scenarios, using publicly hosted web applications, we show that LingoDB is indeed easy to understand and profile.
items.abstract_zh:
虽然编译查询引擎可以非常高效，尤其是对于复杂的查询，但它们也因难以理解、调试和分析而闻名。然而，如果有正确的架构和工具支持，情况就不一定如此。

通过这个演示，我们希望为我们自己的编译查询引擎 LingoDB 展示这一点。为此，我们构建了一个名为 LingoDB-CT 的仪器和可视化框架，它从多个角度聚合数据并将其可视化以揭示更大的图景。通过使用公共托管的 Web 应用程序的两个交互式演示场景，我们表明 LingoDB 确实易于理解和分析。

items.title: ABase: the Multi-Tenant NoSQL Serverless Database for Diverse and Dynamic Workloads in Large-scale Cloud Environments.
items.abstract: Multi-tenant architectures enhance the elasticity and resource utilization of NoSQL databases by allowing multiple tenants to co-locate and share resources. However, in large-scale cloud environments, the diverse and dynamic nature of workloads poses significant challenges for multi-tenant NoSQL databases. Based on our practical observations, we have identified three crucial challenges: (1) the impact of caching on performance isolation, as cache hits alter request execution and resource consumption, leading to inaccurate traffic control; (2) the dynamic changes in traffic, with changes in tenant traffic trends causing throttling or resource wastage, and changes in access distribution causing hot key pressure or cache hit ratio drops; and (3) the imbalanced layout of data nodes due to tenants' diverse resource requirements, leading to low resource utilization. To address these challenges, we introduce ABase, a multi-tenant NoSQL serverless database developed at ByteDance. ABase introduces a two-layer caching mechanism with a cache-aware isolation mechanism to ensure accurate resource consumption estimates. Furthermore, ABase employs a predictive autoscaling policy to dynamically adjust resources in response to tenant traffic changes and a multi-resource rescheduling algorithm to balance resource utilization across data nodes. With these innovations, ABase has successfully served ByteDance's large-scale cloud environment, supporting a total workload that has achieved a peak QPS of over 13 billion and total storage exceeding 1 EB.
items.abstract_zh: 多租户架构允许多个租户共置和共享资源，从而增强了 NoSQL 数据库的弹性和资源利用率。然而，在大规模云环境中，工作负载的多样性和动态性给多租户 NoSQL 数据库带来了重大挑战。根据我们的实际观察，我们发现了三个关键挑战：（1）缓存对性能隔离的影响，因为缓存命中会改变请求执行和资源消耗，导致流量控制不准确； （2）流量的动态变化，租户流量趋势的变化导致节流或资源浪费，访问分布的变化导致热键压力或缓存命中率下降； (3)租户资源需求多样化导致数据节点布局不平衡，导致资源利用率低。为了应对这些挑战，我们推出了 ABase，这是字节跳动开发的多租户 NoSQL 无服务器数据库。 ABase引入了两层缓存机制和缓存感知隔离机制，以确保准确的资源消耗估算。此外，ABase还采用预测性自动扩展策略来动态调整资源以响应租户流量变化，并采用多资源重新调度算法来平衡数据节点之间的资源利用率。通过这些创新，ABase成功服务于字节跳动大规模云环境，支撑总工作负载峰值QPS超过130亿，总存储超过1EB。

items.title: Navigating Data Errors in Machine Learning Pipelines: Identify, Debug, and Learn.
items.abstract: Addressing data errors-such as missing, incorrect, noisy, biased, or out-of-distribution values-is essential to building reliable machine learning (ML) systems. Traditional methods often focus on refining the training process to minimize error symptoms or repairing data errors indiscriminately, without addressing their root causes. These isolated approaches ignore how errors originate and propagate through the interconnected stages of ML pipelines-data preprocessing, model training, and prediction-resulting in superficial fixes and suboptimal solutions. Consequently, they miss the opportunity to understand how data errors impact downstream tasks and to implement targeted, effective interventions. In recent years, the research community has made significant progress in developing holistic approaches to identify the most harmful data errors, prioritize impactful repairs, and reason about their effects when errors cannot be fully resolved. This tutorial surveys prominent work in this area and introduces practical tools designed to address data quality issues across the ML lifecycle. By combining theoretical insights with hands-on demonstrations, attendees will gain actionable strategies to diagnose, repair, and manage data errors, enhancing the reliability, fairness, and transparency of ML systems in real-world applications.
items.abstract_zh: 解决数据错误（例如缺失、不正确、有噪声、有偏差或超出分布值）对于构建可靠的机器学习 (ML) 系统至关重要。传统方法通常侧重于改进训练过程，以最大程度地减少错误症状或不加区别地修复数据错误，而不解决其根本原因。这些孤立的方法忽略了错误是如何在机器学习管道的互连阶段（数据预处理、模型训练和预测）中产生和传播的，从而导致肤浅的修复和次优的解决方案。因此，他们错过了了解数据错误如何影响下游任务以及实施有针对性的有效干预措施的机会。近年来，研究界在开发整体方法方面取得了重大进展，以识别最有害的数据错误，优先考虑有影响的修复，并在错误无法完全解决时推理其影响。本教程调查了该领域的杰出工作，并介绍了旨在解决整个机器学习生命周期中的数据质量问题的实用工具。通过将理论见解与实践演示相结合，与会者将获得诊断、修复和管理数据错误的可行策略，从而增强机器学习系统在实际应用中的可靠性、公平性和透明度。

items.title: Enterprise Application-Database Co-Innovation for Hybrid Transactional/Analytical Processing: A Virtual Data Model and Its Query Optimization Needs.
items.abstract:
The advent of hybrid transactional/analytical processing (HTAP) systems has reshaped enterprise data management, breaking the wall between transaction processing and analytics. This paper explores the co-innovation between enterprise applications and databases to advance HTAP architecture and drive its scalable adoption, as exemplified by SAP S/4HANA powered by SAP HANA. At the core of this innovation lies the Virtual Data Model (VDM), which simplifies the formulation of analytical queries directly over transactional data, making HTAP databases more accessible to application developers.

This paper delves into the complexities of the VDM, highlighting key query optimization challenges such as expansive join views, augmentation joins, and augmentation self-joins. Our findings underscore the pivotal role of robust query optimizers in achieving efficient HTAP query execution. By providing actionable insights into the design and optimization of database systems, this paper may serve as motivation for further research into advancing HTAP database technology.
items.abstract_zh:
混合事务/分析处理 (HTAP) 系统的出现重塑了企业数据管理，打破了事务处理和分析之间的壁垒。本文探讨了企业应用程序和数据库之间的联合创新，以推进 HTAP 架构并推动其可扩展的采用，例如由 SAP HANA 提供支持的 SAP S/4HANA。这项创新的核心在于虚拟数据模型 (VDM)，它简化了直接对事务数据进行分析查询的制定，使应用程序开发人员更容易访问 HTAP 数据库。

本文深入研究了 VDM 的复杂性，重点介绍了关键的查询优化挑战，例如扩展连接视图、增强连接和增强自连接。我们的研究结果强调了强大的查询优化器在实现高效 HTAP 查询执行方面的关键作用。通过提供对数据库系统的设计和优化的可行见解，本文可以作为进一步研究推进 HTAP 数据库技术的动力。

items.title: Introducing RAW Hollow: An In-Memory, Co-Located, Compressed Object Store with Opt-In Strong Consistency.
items.abstract:
In recent years, significant advances have been made to NoSQL databases, enabling them to operate at extreme scale - Cassandra has been benchmarked at over a million writes/second. However our experience at Netflix has shown that applications often deal with small-medium datasets that change less frequently. For these scenarios, while SQL databases remain the popular persistence option, we've found that it can be challenging for applications to achieve consistent performance with low latency and high availability. Supplementing the primary store with caching improves read performance, but at the cost of dealing with cache synchronization complexities. Even fully in-memory databases carry the overhead of network calls for every request.

This paper introduces RAW Hollow, a first of its kind object store that is local and in-memory with opt-in strong consistency. With RAW Hollow, the entire dataset is disseminated across the application cluster and resides in the memory of each application process. By leveraging compression, despite being in-memory, RAW Hollow allows scaling datasets up to a 100 million records per entity. Reads are eventually consistent by default, and completely local to the application, ensuring extremely low latencies and high availability. RAW Hollow allows applications to query millions of records in milliseconds. Writes are reflected throughout the cluster with single digit millisecond latencies. Users can select strong consistency at the individual request level allowing them to balance high availability with strong consistency. RAW Hollow simplifies building stateful applications that are highly available and easy to scale.

For this demo, we will load the entire public IMDb dataset into RAW Hollow and allow users to experience first hand the in-memory performance of RAW Hollow by querying, modifying and cloning the data. We will also showcase its resiliency to distributed failures.
items.abstract_zh:
近年来，NoSQL 数据库取得了重大进展，使其能够以极大规模运行 - Cassandra 的基准测试速度超过每秒 100 万次写入。然而，我们在 Netflix 的经验表明，应用程序通常处理变化不太频繁的中小型数据集。对于这些场景，虽然 SQL 数据库仍然是流行的持久性选项，但我们发现应用程序要在低延迟和高可用性的情况下实现一致的性能可能具有挑战性。通过缓存补充主存储可以提高读取性能，但代价是处理缓存同步的复杂性。即使完全内存数据库也会承担每个请求的网络调用开销。

本文介绍了 RAW Hollow，这是同类中第一个对象存储，它位于本地且位于内存中，具有选择加入的强一致性。使用 RAW Hollow，整个数据集可以跨应用程序集群传播，并驻留在每个应用程序进程的内存中。尽管在内存中，但通过利用压缩，RAW Hollow 允许将数据集扩展至每个实体 1 亿条记录。默认情况下，读取最终是一致的，并且完全在应用程序本地，确保极低的延迟和高可用性。 RAW Hollow 允许应用程序在几毫秒内查询数百万条记录。写入会以个位数毫秒的延迟反映在整个集群中。用户可以在单个请求级别选择强一致性，从而使他们能够平衡高可用性与强一致性。 RAW Hollow 简化了构建高可用性且易于扩展的有状态应用程序。

在本次演示中，我们将把整个公开的 IMDb 数据集加载到 RAW Hollow 中，并让用户通过查询、修改和克隆数据来亲身体验 RAW Hollow 的内存性能。我们还将展示其对分布式故障的弹性。

items.title: Autotuning Systems: Techniques, Challenges, and Opportunities.
items.abstract: The rapid growth of cloud computing and systems has introduced significant complexity in managing and optimizing configurations to meet diverse workload demands across a wide array of hardware. Autotuning systems, leveraging advancements in machine learning and optimization, offer an effective solution to these challenges. By automating configuration tuning, these systems can dynamically adapt to workload changes, optimize performance in real time, and reduce the burden on system administrators. This tutorial provides both theoretical foundations and practical demonstrations of systems autotuning software, with a focus on offline and online optimization methodologies. We present a comprehensive review of state-of-the-art autotuning systems and discuss how they address key challenges, such as handling large configuration spaces, mitigating noise in real-world environments, and ensuring safe and efficient exploration during tuning. To conclude, we offer a hands-on session where participants can experiment with an open-source system and gain experience with real-world tuning scenarios.
items.abstract_zh: 云计算和系统的快速增长给管理和优化配置带来了极大的复杂性，以满足各种硬件上的不同工作负载需求。自动调整系统利用机器学习和优化方面的进步，为这些挑战提供了有效的解决方案。通过自动化配置调整，这些系统可以动态适应工作负载变化，实时优化性能，并减轻系统管理员的负担。本教程提供系统自动调整软件的理论基础和实践演示，重点关注离线和在线优化方法。我们对最先进的自动调谐系统进行了全面的回顾，并讨论了它们如何应对关键挑战，例如处理大型配置空间、减轻现实环境中的噪声以及确保调谐过程中安全高效的探索。最后，我们提供了一个实践课程，参与者可以尝试开源系统并获得实际调整场景的经验。

items.title: SeerCuts: Explainable Attribute Discretization.
items.abstract: This demonstration showcases SeerCuts - a tool that suggests useful and semantically meaningful discretization strategies (partitions) for numerical attributes. SeerCuts is a generic, interactive framework where users specify attributes to discretize and their utility measure for a downstream task of choice. It uses GPT-4o to assess the semantic meaningfulness of candidate partitions and employs an efficient search strategy to explore the vast space of discretization options. With hierarchical clustering to group related partitions and a multi-armed bandit policy to identify useful partitions with only a few samples, SeerCuts quickly finds meaningful and useful partitions. In the demo, we will provide an overview of SeerCuts and allow the audience to explore various datasets and tasks, including data visualization and comprehensive modeling. The users will be able to evaluate how SeerCuts identifies meaningful discretization strategies and compare the tradeoff between different discretization options.
items.abstract_zh: 该演示展示了 SeerCuts - 一种为数值属性建议有用且语义上有意义的离散化策略（分区）的工具。 SeerCuts 是一个通用的交互式框架，用户可以在其中指定要离散化的属性及其对所选下游任务的效用度量。它使用 GPT-4o 来评估候选分区的语义意义，并​​采用有效的搜索策略来探索离散化选项的广阔空间。通过分层聚类对相关分区进行分组，并使用多臂老虎机策略仅用少量样本来识别有用的分区，SeerCuts 可以快速找到有意义且有用的分区。在演示中，我们将提供 SeerCuts 的概述，并允许观众探索各种数据集和任务，包括数据可视化和综合建模。用户将能够评估 SeerCuts 如何识别有意义的离散化策略并比较不同离散化选项之间的权衡。

items.title: Graphy'our Data: Towards End-to-End Modeling, Exploring and Generating Report from Raw Data.
items.abstract: While Large Language Models (LLMs) excel at single-document queries and conversational workflows, they struggle with progressively exploring, analyzing, and synthesizing large unstructured document sets, such as in literature surveys. We address this challenge -- termed Progressive Document Investigation -- by introducing Graphy, an end-to-end platform that automates data modeling, exploration and high-quality report generation in a user-friendly manner. Graphy comprises an offline Scrapper that transforms raw documents into a graph, and an online Surveyor that enables iterative exploration and LLM-driven report generation. We showcase a pre-scrapped graph of over 50,000 papers, demonstrating how Graphy facilitates the literature-survey scenario, with video available at https://youtu.be/uM4nzkAdGlM.
items.abstract_zh: 虽然大型语言模型 (LLM) 擅长单文档查询和对话工作流程，但它们在逐步探索、分析和综合大型非结构化文档集（例如文献调查）方面遇到困难。我们通过引入 Graphy 来应对这一挑战（称为渐进式文档调查），Graphy 是一个端到端平台，可以以用户友好的方式自动进行数据建模、探索和高质量报告生成。 Graphy 包括一个离线 Scrapper（将原始文档转换为图表）和一个在线 Surveyor（支持迭代探索和 LLM 驱动的报告生成）。我们展示了超过 50,000 篇论文的预先废弃的图表，展示了 Graphy 如何促进文献调查场景，视频可在 https://youtu.be/uM4nzkAdGlM 上观看。

items.title: Transactional Cloud Applications: Status Quo, Challenges, and Opportunities.
items.abstract:
Transactional cloud applications such as payment, booking, reservation systems, and complex business workflows are currently being rewritten for deployment in the cloud. This migration to the cloud is happening mainly for reasons of cost and scalability. Over the years, application developers have used different migration approaches, such as microservice frameworks, actors, and stateful dataflow systems.

The migration to the cloud has brought back data management challenges traditionally handled by database management systems. Those challenges include ensuring state consistency, maintaining durability, and managing the application lifecycle. At the same time, the shift to a distributed computing infrastructure introduced new issues, such as message delivery, task scheduling, containerization, and (auto)scaling.

Although the data management community has made progress in developing analytical and transactional database systems, transactional cloud applications have received little attention in database research. This tutorial aims to highlight recent trends in the area and discusses open research challenges for the data management community.
items.abstract_zh:
目前正在重写支付、预订、预订系统和复杂业务工作流程等事务性云应用程序以在云中部署。这种向云的迁移主要是出于成本和可扩展性的原因。多年来，应用程序开发人员使用了不同的迁移方法，例如微服务框架、参与者和有状态数据流系统。

向云的迁移带来了传统上由数据库管理系统处理的数据管理挑战。这些挑战包括确保状态一致性、保持持久性和管理应用程序生命周期。与此同时，向分布式计算基础设施的转变带来了新的问题，例如消息传递、任务调度、容器化和（自动）扩展。

尽管数据管理社区在开发分析和事务数据库系统方面取得了进展，但事务云应用程序在数据库研究中却很少受到关注。本教程旨在强调该领域的最新趋势，并讨论数据管理社区的开放研究挑战。

items.title: Experimental Evaluation of Optimizing Memory Consumption in SAP HANA using PEOopt.
items.abstract:
Materialized intermediate results dominate memory consumption in query processing, which is a challenge in the age of Big Data workloads. Modern query processors use pipelines to produce materialized intermediate results. The order by which pipelines are executed influence the lifetime of materialized intermediate results, directly impacting the overall consumed memory. By scheduling the execution of pipelines, we can therefore save memory.

Our previous work provided a proof-of-concept for pipeline execution ordering to reduce memory consumption during runtime using a simple join query workload. This paper presents the experimental evaluation of an end-to-end implementation prototype called PEOopt within SAP HANA, which generalizes our previous work. For long running queries our prototype reduces the memory footprint by up to 36% without compromising any execution time performance. Hence, we prove the effectiveness of saving memory in real world database systems like SAP HANA.
items.abstract_zh:
物化的中间结果主导查询处理中的内存消耗，这在大数据工作负载时代是一个挑战。现代查询处理器使用管道来生成具体化的中间结果。管道的执行顺序会影响具体化中间结果的生命周期，从而直接影响总体消耗的内存。通过调度管道的执行，我们可以节省内存。

我们之前的工作为管道执行排序提供了概念验证，以使用简单的连接查询工作负载减少运行时的内存消耗。本文介绍了 SAP HANA 中名为 PEOopt 的端到端实施原型的实验评估，它概括了我们之前的工作。对于长时间运行的查询，我们的原型可将内存占用量减少高达 36%，而不会影响任何执行时间性能。因此，我们证明了在 SAP HANA 等现实数据库系统中节省内存的有效性。

items.title: Demonstrating SQLBarber: Leveraging Large Language Models to Generate Customized and Realistic SQL Workloads.
items.abstract: Database research and development require a large volume of SQL queries for benchmarking. However, it is difficult to obtain real SQL queries due to privacy issues, and existing SQL generation methods are limited in customization and satisfying realistic constraints. To address this problem, we propose SQLBarber, a novel system leveraging Large Language Models (LLMs) to generate customized and realistic SQL workloads. SQLBarber (a) eliminates the need for users to manually craft SQL templates in advance, while providing the flexibility to accept high-level natural language specifications to constrain the SQL templates, (b) scales efficiently to produce a large number of queries satisfying any user-defined cost distribution (e.g., cardinality, execution plan cost, or execution time), and (c) analyzes execution statistics obtained from Amazon Redshift and Snowflake to derive both the specifications for SQL templates and the cost distribution of SQL queries, ensuring that these constraints reflect real-world workloads. This demonstration allows the audience to experience SQLBarber in action: (1) provide their customized specifications on SQL templates, (2) gain insights on the effect of SQL template and predicate values on SQL costs, and (3) explore real-world specifications of templates as well as cost distributions of queries, and constrain their SQL queries to a desired specification and distribution, with the flexibility to try out different LLM and SQL cost types. A video demonstration is available at https://youtu.be/qOuAKVXXcdM.
items.abstract_zh: 数据库研发需要大量的SQL查询进行基准测试。然而，由于隐私问题，很难获得真实的SQL查询，并且现有的SQL生成方法在定制和满足现实约束方面受到限制。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了 SQLBarber，这是一种利用大型语言模型 (LLM) 来生成定制且真实的 SQL 工作负载的新颖系统。 SQLBarber (a) 消除了用户提前手动制作 SQL 模板的需要，同时提供了接受高级自然语言规范来约束 SQL 模板的灵活性，(b) 有效扩展以生成满足任何用户定义的成本分布（例如基数、执行计划成本或执行时间）的大量查询，以及 (c) 分析从 Amazon Redshift 和 Snowflake 获得的执行统计数据，以得出 SQL 模板的规范和 SQL 查询的成本分布，确保这些约束反映现实世界的工作负载。该演示使观众能够实际体验 SQLBarber：(1) 提供 SQL 模板的自定义规范，(2) 深入了解 SQL 模板和谓词值对 SQL 成本的影响，以及 (3) 探索模板的实际规范以及查询的成本分布，并将他们的 SQL 查询限制为所需的规范和分布，并可以灵活地尝试不同的 LLM 和 SQL 成本类型。视频演示请访问 https://youtu.be/qOuAKVXXcdM。

items.title: CauSumX: Summarized Causal Explanations For Group-By-Average Queries.
items.abstract: Group-by-average SQL queries are a cornerstone of data analysis, often employed to uncover patterns and trends within datasets. However, interpreting the results of these queries can be challenging and time-intensive, particularly when working with large, high-dimensional datasets. Automating the generation of explanations for such queries can greatly enhance analysts' ability to derive meaningful insights while reducing human effort. Effective explanations must balance succinctness and depth, offering insights into different patterns across aggregate results, while crucially reflecting cause-effect relationships rather than mere correlations. This ensures that users can make informed, data-driven decisions grounded in reality. In this demonstration, we present CauSumX, a system that produces concise and causal explanations for group-by-average queries. Leveraging background causal knowledge, CauSumX identifies the key causal factors driving variations in the outcome variable across different groups. The system employs an efficient algorithm based on a recently published paper. We will demonstrate the utility of CauSumX for generating useful summarized causal explanations by interacting with the SIGMOD'25 participants, who will act as data analysts aiming to explain their query results.
items.abstract_zh: 按平均值分组 SQL 查询是数据分析的基石，通常用于发现数据集中的模式和趋势。然而，解释这些查询的结果可能具有挑战性且耗时，特别是在处理大型高维数据集时。自动生成此类查询的解释可以极大地增强分析师获得有意义的见解的能力，同时减少人力。有效的解释必须平衡简洁性和深度，提供对总体结果中不同模式的洞察，同时关键地反映因果关系而不仅仅是相关性。这确保用户能够基于现实做出明智的、数据驱动的决策。在此演示中，我们展示了 CauSumX，这是一个为按平均值分组的查询生成简洁且因果解释的系统。利用背景因果知识，CauSumX 确定了驱动不同群体结果变量变化的关键因果因素。该系统采用基于最近发表的论文的高效算法。我们将演示 CauSumX 通过与 SIGMOD'25 参与者交互来生成有用的总结性因果解释的实用性，这些参与者将充当数据分析师，旨在解释他们的查询结果。

items.title: Eigen+: Memory Over-Subscription for Alibaba Cloud Databases.
items.abstract:
Memory is a critical bottleneck in cloud computing. While memory over-subscription enhances resource utilization by allowing more instances per machine, it increases the risk of Out of Memory (OOM) errors, potentially compromising service availability and violating Service Level Objectives (SLOs). Traditional methods predict memory utilization based on historical data and use bin packing algorithms for resource scheduling but often fail under high utilization due to inaccurate predictions. We observe that in cloud databases, the causes of OOM errors adhere to the Pareto Principle : the majority of OOM errors originate from a minority of instances. Leveraging this insight, we transform the complex prediction problem into a classification task by identifying and managing these problematic instances.

We present Eigen+, a cluster management system for Alibaba Cloud databases. Eigen+ profiles and classifies instances to identify transients, optimizes resource scheduling to balance efficiency and reliability. In addition, it models the impact of memory over-subscription on SLO compliance to provide a quantitative basis for determining precise bin-packing constraints and optimizing over-subscription levels. Evaluations on production clusters demonstrate that Eigen+ improves the memory allocation ratio of an online MySQL cluster by 36.21% (from 75.67% to 111.88%) on average, while maintaining SLO compliance with no OOM occurrences.
items.abstract_zh:
内存是云计算的关键瓶颈。虽然内存超额订阅通过允许每台计算机添加更多实例来提高资源利用率，但它会增加内存不足 (OOM) 错误的风险，可能会影响服务可用性并违反服务级别目标 (SLO)。传统方法根据历史数据预测内存利用率，并使用装箱算法进行资源调度，但在高利用率下常常因预测不准确而失败。我们观察到，在云数据库中，OOM 错误的原因遵循帕累托原则：大多数 OOM 错误源自少数实例。利用这种洞察力，我们通过识别和管理这些有问题的实例，将复杂的预测问题转化为分类任务。

我们推出 Eigen+，一个阿里云数据库集群管理系统。 Eigen+ 对实例进行分析和分类以识别瞬态，优化资源调度以平衡效率和可靠性。此外，它还模拟了内存超额订阅对 SLO 合规性的影响，为确定精确的装箱约束和优化超额订阅级别提供定量基础。对生产集群的评估表明，Eigen+将在线MySQL集群的内存分配率平均提高了36.21%（从75.67%到111.88%），同时保持了SLO合规性，没有发生OOM。

items.title: Demonstrating MAST: An Efficient System for Point Cloud Data Analytics.
items.abstract: The rapid growth of point cloud (PC) data calls for specialized solutions for data management and analytics. In this paper, we introduce MAST, a prototype system designed for efficient PC data analysis. MAST enables users to perform analytical queries efficiently, incorporating semantic and spatial predicates while ensuring high query accuracy. In this demonstration, we introduce the framework of the system prototype, including the storage layer, preprocessing layer, query processing layer, visualization and user feedback layer. We introduce the user interface of MAST and its workflow, and showcase the end-to-end usage of the MAST system with 2 analytical queries executed on a real PC dataset.
items.abstract_zh: 点云 (PC) 数据的快速增长需要专门的数据管理和分析解决方案。在本文中，我们介绍了 MAST，这是一个专为高效 PC 数据分析而设计的原型系统。 MAST 使用户能够高效地执行分析查询，结合语义和空间谓词，同时确保高查询准确性。在本次演示中，我们介绍了系统原型的框架，包括存储层、预处理层、查询处理层、可视化和用户反馈层。我们介绍了 MAST 的用户界面及其工作流程，并展示了 MAST 系统的端到端用法，以及在真实 PC 数据集上执行的 2 个分析查询。

items.title: Demo of Kishu: Time-Traveling for Computational Notebooks.
items.abstract:
Computational notebooks (e.g., Jupyter, Google Colab) enable the interactive computing model of iteratively executing cells (i.e., a set of statements) and observing the result (e.g., model or plot), and are widely used by data scientists. However, existing notebook systems do not offer time-traveling to past states , hence certain cell executions can irreversably modify the session state (e.g., accidentally overwriting a variable, leading to a loss of work progress.

In this demo, we introduce Kishu, a new notebook system facilitating time-traveling with lightweight incremental checkpoint and restore. Kishu's frontend allows users to create manual and automatic commits for session state snapshots at various points in time, and perform undos or checkouts to any past state. Kishu's backend transparently monitors user behavior (e.g., data operations) to create efficient incremental checkpoints, which support the frontend's (also incremental) sub-second checkouts to past states.
