Through this demo, we will show Kishu's ability to mitigate two common pain points of notebook usage through time-traveling: undoing cell executions and facilitating path-based exploration.
items.abstract_zh:
计算笔记本（例如 Jupyter、Google Colab）支持迭代执行单元（即一组语句）和观察结果（例如模型或绘图）的交互式计算模型，并被数据科学家广泛使用。然而，现有的笔记本系统不提供到过去状态的时间旅行，因此某些单元执行可以不可逆地修改会话状态（例如，意外覆盖变量，导致工作进度丢失。

在此演示中，我们介绍 Kishu，这是一种新的笔记本系统，通过轻量级增量检查点和恢复来促进时间旅行。 Kishu 的前端允许用户在不同时间点为会话状态快照创建手动和自动提交，并对任何过去的状态执行撤消或签出。 Kishu 的后端透明地监视用户行为（例如数据操作）以创建高效的增量检查点，这支持前端（也是增量的）对过去状态的亚秒检查。通过这个演示，我们将展示 Kishu 通过时间旅行缓解笔记本使用的两个常见痛点的能力：撤消单元执行和促进基于路径的探索。

items.title: NeutronRAG: Towards Understanding the Effectiveness of RAG from a Data Retrieval Perspective.
items.abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) has been widely used to enhance the generation quality of large language models (LLMs), particularly in domain-specific tasks. As application requirements become increasingly diverse, various RAG methods have been proposed to optimize the retrieval and generation quality of different task scenarios, such as VectorRAG, GraphRAG, and HybridRAG. However, RAG faces two key challenges in these retrieval methods: evaluating different retrieval methods and optimizing parameter configurations. First, different retrieval methods show different performances. How to uniformly compare and analyze the advantages and disadvantages of these retrieval methods remains an open research problem. Second, the effectiveness of RAG is highly sensitive to key parameter configurations. Optimizing these parameters is challenging due to the complexity of the parameter space and the difficulty in identifying the sources of errors in the generated responses. Existing RAG tools typically use a single retrieval method, lacking analytical capabilities and multi-strategy support. To address these challenges, we introduce NeutronRAG, a demonstration of understanding the effectiveness of RAG from a data retrieval perspective. NeutronRAG supports hybrid retrieval strategies and helps researchers iteratively refine RAG configuration to improve retrieval and generation quality through systematic analysis, visual feedback, and parameter adjustment advice. It facilitates data-driven decisions to enhance LLM generation quality while exploring effective retrieval strategies in RAG systems. The web app is available at: https://github.com/iDC-NEU/NeutronRAG.
items.abstract_zh: 检索增强生成（RAG）已被广泛用于提高大型语言模型（LLM）的生成质量，特别是在特定领域的任务中。随着应用需求变得日益多样化，人们提出了各种RAG方法来优化不同任务场景的检索和生成质量，例如VectorRAG、GraphRAG和HybridRAG。然而，RAG 在这些检索方法中面临两个关键挑战：评估不同的检索方法和优化参数配置。首先，不同的检索方法表现出不同的性能。如何统一比较和分析这些检索方法的优缺点仍然是一个开放的研究问题。其次，RAG 的有效性对关键参数配置高度敏感。由于参数空间的复杂性以及识别生成的响应中的错误来源的困难，优化这些参数具有挑战性。现有的RAG工具通常使用单一的检索方法，缺乏分析能力和多策略支持。为了应对这些挑战，我们引入了 NeutronRAG，它演示了从数据检索角度理解 RAG 的有效性。 NeutronRAG 支持混合检索策略，并帮助研究人员迭代完善 RAG 配置，通过系统分析、视觉反馈和参数调整建议来提高检索和生成质量。它有助于数据驱动的决策，以提高 LLM 生成质量，同时探索 RAG 系统中的有效检索策略。该网络应用程序位于：https://github.com/iDC-NEU/NeutronRAG。

items.title: Adaptive and Efficient Log Parsing as a Cloud Service.
items.abstract: Logs are a critical data source for cloud systems, enabling advanced features like monitoring, alerting, and root cause analysis. However, the massive scale and diverse formats of unstructured logs pose challenges for adaptable, efficient, and accurate parsing methods. This paper introduces ByteBrain-LogParser , an innovative log parsing framework designed specifically for cloud environments. ByteBrain-LogParser employs a hierarchical clustering algorithm to allow real-time precision adjustments, coupled with optimizations such as positional similarity distance, deduplication, and hash encoding to enhance performance. Experiments on large-scale datasets show that it processes 229,000 logs per second on average, achieving an 840% speedup over the fastest baseline while maintaining accuracy comparable to state-of-the-art methods. Real-world evaluations further validate its efficiency and adaptability, demonstrating its potential as a robust cloud-based log parsing solution.
items.abstract_zh: 日志是云系统的关键数据源，可实现监控、警报和根本原因分析等高级功能。然而，规模庞大、格式多样的非结构化日志对适应性、高效、准确的解析方法提出了挑战。本文介绍了 ByteBrain-LogParser ，一个专为云环境设计的创新日志解析框架。 ByteBrain-LogParser采用分层聚类算法来实现实时精度调整，并结合位置相似距离、重复数据删除和哈希编码等优化来增强性能。对大规模数据集的实验表明，它平均每秒处理 229,000 个日志，比最快的基线实现了 840% 的加速，同时保持了与最先进方法相当的准确性。现实世界的评估进一步验证了其效率和适应性，展示了其作为强大的基于云的日志解析解决方案的潜力。

items.title: Data+AI: LLM4Data and Data4LLM.
items.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have revolutionized traditional data management systems by their natural language processing capabilities (e.g., understanding, reasoning, generation, few-shot and zero-shot learning), while data management techniques play a vital role in optimizing AI models (e.g., data preparation, data efficient training and inference). This establishes a two-way relationship where AI enhances data management, and data boosts AI capabilities. This tutorial covers recent advancements and challenges at this intersection, focusing on LLM4Data and Data4LLM over four parts. Initially, we discuss the background and motivations for integrating data and AI, and present the challenges and principles of LLM4Data and Data4LLM. We then explore how LLMs optimize data management by offering practical applications like managing unstructured data. We also examine how data management optimizes AI, particularly in training and fine-tuning LLMs, showcasing techniques for data preparation and inference. Finally, we provide open challenges and future research directions, aiming to drive innovation in both fields.
items.abstract_zh: 大语言模型（LLM）凭借其自然语言处理能力（例如理解、推理、生成、小样本和零样本学习）彻底改变了传统的数据管理系统，而数据管理技术在优化人工智能模型（例如数据准备、数据高效训练和推理）方面发挥着至关重要的作用。这建立了一种双向关系，人工智能增强数据管理，数据增强人工智能能力。本教程涵盖了该交叉点的最新进展和挑战，重点关注 LLM4Data 和 Data4LLM 的四个部分。首先，我们讨论了数据与人工智能整合的背景和动机，并提出了LLM4Data和Data4LLM的挑战和原则。然后，我们探讨法学硕士如何通过提供管理非结构化数据等实际应用来优化数据管理。我们还研究数据管理如何优化人工智能，特别是在训练和微调法学硕士方面，展示数据准备和推理技术。最后，我们提供了开放的挑战和未来的研究方向，旨在推动这两个领域的创新。

items.title: KDSelector: A Knowledge-Enhanced and Data-Efficient Model Selector Learning Framework for Time Series Anomaly Detection.
items.abstract: Model selection has been raised as an essential problem in the area of time series anomaly detection (TSAD), because there is no single best TSAD model for the highly heterogeneous time series in real-world applications. However, despite the success of existing model selection solutions that train a classification model (especially neural network, NN) using historical data as a selector to predict the correct TSAD model for each series, the NN-based selector learning methods used by existing solutions do not make full use of the knowledge in the historical data and require iterating over all training samples, which limits the accuracy and training speed of the selector. To address these limitations, we propose KDSelector, a novel knowledge-enhanced and data-efficient framework for learning the NN-based TSAD model selector, of which three key components are specifically designed to integrate available knowledge into the selector and dynamically prune less important and redundant samples during the learning. We develop a TSAD model selection system with KDSelector as the internal, to demonstrate how users improve the accuracy and training speed of their selectors by using KDSelector as a plug-and-play module. Our demonstration video is hosted at https://youtu.be/2uqupDWvTF0.
items.abstract_zh: 模型选择已成为时间序列异常检测（TSAD）领域的一个重要问题，因为对于现实应用中高度异构的时间序列，没有单一的最佳 TSAD 模型。然而，尽管现有的模型选择解决方案成功地使用历史数据作为选择器来训练分类模型（尤其是神经网络，NN）来预测每个系列的正确TSAD模型，但现有解决方案所使用的基于NN的选择器学习方法并没有充分利用历史数据中的知识，并且需要迭代所有训练样本，这限制了选择器的准确性和训练速度。为了解决这些限制，我们提出了 KDSelector，一种新颖的知识增强和数据高效框架，用于学习基于神经网络的 TSAD 模型选择器，其中三个关键组件专门设计用于将可用知识集成到选择器中，并在学习过程中动态修剪不太重要和冗余的样本。我们开发了一个以 KDSelector 为内部的 TSAD 模型选择系统，来演示用户如何通过使用 KDSelector 作为即插即用模块来提高选择器的准确性和训练速度。我们的演示视频托管在 https://youtu.be/2uqupDWvTF0。

items.title: Advances in Designing Scalable Graph Neural Networks: The Perspective of Graph Data Management.
items.abstract:
Graph Neural Network (GNN) is a successful marriage of graph data management and deep learning, leading to notable improvements in learning quality over graphs. This advancement highly impacts graph-based applications in many areas, including computer vision, natural language processing, biology, medication, and social science. Despite the success, scaling up GNN models poses a formidable and long-lasting challenge, hindering the application to industrial-level graphs featuring millions or billions of nodes and edges. The rapid update of tasks and models requires continuous efforts in developing scalable GNN architectures. In specific, the scalability bottleneck of GNNs typically stem from graph-related computations, entailing more proficient processing and utilization of the unstructured graph data.

There has been a marked trend of incorporation between GNN and data management to tackle newly-emerged scalability challenges. This includes the utilization of graph algorithms such as Personalized PageRank (PPR) and subgraph discovery in GNN models, as well as exploring topics in graph domain including multi-scale representation and graph spectrum. This primer tutorial (3 hours) aims to provide a comprehensive overview of scalable GNN designs, highlighting the most recent and prominent models that focus on the scalability issue. We will also summarize the technical challenges and suggest potential future directions regarding the rapid developments in this field. We believe that this work can be used as one important reference for researchers looking to develop scalable GNN models.
items.abstract_zh:
图神经网络（GNN）是图数据管理和深度学习的成功结合，显着提高了图学习质量。这一进步极大地影响了许多领域基于图的应用，包括计算机视觉、自然语言处理、生物学、医学和社会科学。尽管取得了成功，但扩展 GNN 模型仍面临着巨大而持久的挑战，阻碍了其在具有数百万或数十亿节点和边的工业级图上的应用。任务和模型的快速更新需要不断努力开发可扩展的 GNN 架构。具体来说，GNN 的可扩展性瓶颈通常源于与图相关的计算，需要更熟练地处理和利用非结构化图数据。

GNN 和数据管理的结合已成为解决新出现的可扩展性挑战的明显趋势。这包括使用图算法，例如个性化 PageRank (PPR) 和 GNN 模型中的子图发现，以及探索图领域的主题，包括多尺度表示和图谱。本入门教程（3 小时）旨在提供可扩展 GNN 设计的全面概述，重点介绍关注可扩展性问题的最新且最突出的模型。我们还将总结技术挑战，并就该领域的快速发展提出潜在的未来方向。我们相信这项工作可以作为研究人员开发可扩展 GNN 模型的重要参考。

items.title: Qymera: Simulating Quantum Circuits using RDBMS.
items.abstract: Quantum circuit simulation is crucial for quantum computing such as validating quantum algorithms. We present Qymera, a system that repurposes relational database management systems (RDBMSs) for simulation by translating circuits into SQL queries, allowing quantum operations to run natively within an RDBMS. Qymera supports a wide range of quantum circuits, offering a graphical circuit builder and code-based interfaces to input circuits. With a benchmarking framework, Qymera facilitates comparison of RDBMS-based simulation against state-of-the-art simulation methods. Our demonstration showcases Qymera's end-to-end SQL-based execution, seamless integration with classical workflows, and its utility for development, benchmarking, and education in quantum computing and data management. A video demonstrating Qymera's key features can be found at https://youtu.be/j_fTKnVIV6c.
items.abstract_zh: 量子电路模拟对于验证量子算法等量子计算至关重要。我们推出了 Qymera，这是一个通过将电路转换为 SQL 查询来重新利用关系数据库管理系统 (RDBMS) 进行模拟的系统，允许量子操作在 RDBMS 内本机运行。 Qymera 支持广泛的量子电路，提供图形电路构建器和基于代码的输入电路接口。借助基准测试框架，Qymera 有助于将基于 RDBMS 的模拟与最先进的模拟方法进行比较。我们的演示展示了 Qymera 基于 SQL 的端到端执行、与经典工作流程的无缝集成，及其在量子计算和数据管理方面的开发、基准测试和教育的实用性。演示 Qymera 主要功能的视频可以在 https://youtu.be/j_fTKnVIV6c 上找到。

items.title: PalimpChat: Declarative and Interactive AI analytics.
items.abstract:
Thanks to the advances in generative architectures and large language models, data scientists can now code pipelines of AI operations to process large collections of unstructured data. Recent progress has seen the rise of declarative AI frameworks (e.g., P alimpzest , L otus , and D oc ETL) to build optimized and increasingly complex pipelines, but these systems often remain accessible only to expert programmers. In this demonstration, we present P alimpChat , a chat-based interface to P alimpzest that bridges this gap by letting users create and run sophisticated AI pipelines through natural language alone. By integrating A rchytas , a ReAct-based reasoning agent, and Palimpzest's suite of relational and LLM-based operators, P alimp C hat provides a practical illustration of how a chat interface can make declarative AI frameworks truly accessible to non-experts.

Our demo system is publicly available online. At SIGMOD'25, participants can explore three real-world scenarios-scientific discovery, legal discovery, and real estate search-or apply P alimp C hat to their own datasets. In this paper, we focus on how P alimp C hat , supported by the Palimpzest optimizer, simplifies complex AI workflows such as extracting and analyzing biomedical data.
items.abstract_zh:
由于生成架构和大型语言模型的进步，数据科学家现在可以对人工智能操作的管道进行编码，以处理大量非结构化数据。最近的进展见证了声明式 AI 框架（例如 P alimpzest、Lotus 和 D oc ETL）的兴起，用于构建优化且日益复杂的管道，但这些系统通常仍然只有专家程序员才能访问。在本次演示中，我们展示了 P alimpChat，这是一个基于聊天的 P alimpzest 界面，它允许用户仅通过自然语言创建和运行复杂的 AI 管道，从而弥补这一差距。通过集成 A rchytas（基于 ReAct 的推理代理）和 Palimpzest 的关系型和基于 LLM 的运算符套件，P alimp Chat 提供了一个实用的说明，说明聊天界面如何使声明式 AI 框架真正可供非专家使用。

我们的演示系统已在线公开提供。在 SIGMOD'25 上，参与者可以探索三个现实世界场景 - 科学发现、法律发现和房地产搜索 - 或将 P alimp C hat 应用于他们自己的数据集。在本文中，我们重点关注 Palimpzest 优化器支持下的 P alimp C hat 如何简化复杂的人工智能工作流程，例如提取和分析生物医学数据。

items.title: TigerVector: Supporting Vector Search in Graph Databases for Advanced RAGs.
items.abstract: In this paper, we introduce TigerVector, a system that integrates vector search and graph query within TigerGraph, a Massively Parallel Processing (MPP) native graph database. We extend the vertex attribute type with the embedding type. To support fast vector search, we devise an MPP index framework that interoperates efficiently with the graph engine. The graph query language GSQL is enhanced to support vector type expressions and enable query compositions between vector search results and graph query blocks. These advancements elevate the expressive power and analytical capabilities of graph databases, enabling seamless fusion of unstructured and structured data in ways previously unattainable. Through extensive experiments, we demonstrate TigerVector's hybrid search capability, scalability, and superior performance compared to other graph databases (including Neo4j and Amazon Neptune) and a highly optimized specialized vector database (Milvus). TigerVector was integrated into TigerGraph v4.2 , the latest release of TigerGraph, in December 2024.
items.abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们介绍了 TigerVector，这是一个在 TigerGraph（大规模并行处理（MPP）本机图形数据库）中集成矢量搜索和图形查询的系统。我们用嵌入类型扩展顶点属性类型。为了支持快速向量搜索，我们设计了一个与图引擎高效互操作的 MPP 索引框架。图查询语言GSQL得到增强，支持向量类型表达式，并支持向量搜索结果和图查询块之间的查询组合。这些进步提升了图数据库的表达能力和分析能力，能够以以前无法实现的方式无缝融合非结构化和结构化数据。通过大量的实验，我们展示了 TigerVector 的混合搜索能力、可扩展性以及与其他图数据库（包括 Neo4j 和 Amazon Neptune）和高度优化的专用向量数据库（Milvus）相比的卓越性能。 TigerVector 于 2024 年 12 月集成到 TigerGraph v4.2（TigerGraph 的最新版本）中。

items.title: Fourth International Workshop on Data Systems Education (DataEd'25).
items.abstract: Interest in data systems education is increasing, especially with the rise in demand for well-trained and re-trained data scientists. The database and computing education research communities have complementary perspectives and experiences to share with each other. The DataEd workshop is organized as a dedicated venue for these communities to come together to share findings, cross-pollinate perspectives and methods, and shed light on opportunities for mutual progress in data systems education. In the DataEd 2025 workshop, we will present and discuss data management systems education experiences and research with a keynote speaker, an industry speaker, and paper presentations followed by rich discussions.
items.abstract_zh: 人们对数据系统教育的兴趣与日俱增，尤其是随着对训练有素和再培训的数据科学家的需求不断增加。数据库和计算教育研究界具有互补的观点和经验可以相互分享。 DataEd 研讨会是为这些社区提供一个专门的场所，让他们聚集在一起分享研究成果、交叉传播观点和方法，并揭示数据系统教育方面共同进步的机会。在 DataEd 2025 研讨会上，我们将与主题演讲者、行业演讲者以及论文演示和丰富的讨论一起展示和讨论数据管理系统教育经验和研究。

items.title: A Modular Graph-Native Query Optimization Framework.
items.abstract: Complex Graph Patterns (CGPs), which combine pattern matching with relational operations, are widely used in real-world applications. Existing systems rely on monolithic architectures for CGPs, which restrict their ability to integrate multiple query languages and lack certain advanced optimization techniques. Therefore, to address these issues, we introduce GOpt, a modular graph-native query optimization framework with the following features: (1) support for queries in multiple query languages, (2) decoupling execution from specific graph systems, and (3) integration of advanced optimization techniques. Specifically, GOpt offers a high-level interface, GraphIrBuilder, for converting queries from various graph query languages into a unified intermediate representation (GIR), thereby streamlining the optimization process. It also provides a low-level interface, PhysicalConverter, enabling backends integration by converting the optimized plan into a backend-compatible execution plan. Moreover, GOpt employs a graph-native optimizer that encompasses extensive heuristic rules, an automatic type inference approach, and cost-based optimization techniques tailored for CGPs. Comprehensive experiments show that integrating GOpt significantly boosts performance, with Neo4j achieving an average speedup of 9.2x (up to 48.6x), and GraphScope achieving an average speedup of 33.4x (up to 78.7x), on real-world datasets.
items.abstract_zh: 复杂图模式（CGP）将模式匹配与关系操作相结合，广泛应用于实际应用中。现有系统依赖于 CGP 的整体架构，这限制了它们集成多种查询语言的能力，并且缺乏某些高级优化技术。因此，为了解决这些问题，我们引入了GOpt，一个模块化的图原生查询优化框架，具有以下功能：（1）支持多种查询语言的查询，（2）与特定图系统解耦执行，以及（3）集成高级优化技术。具体来说，GOpt 提供了一个高级接口 GraphIrBuilder，用于将各种图查询语言的查询转换为统一的中间表示（GIR），从而简化优化过程。它还提供了一个低级接口PhysicalConverter，通过将优化计​​划转换为后端兼容的执行计划来实现后端集成。此外，GOpt 采用了图形原生优化器，其中包含广泛的启发式规则、自动类型推断方法以及为 CGP 量身定制的基于成本的优化技术。综合实验表明，集成 GOpt 可以显着提升性能，在真实数据集上，Neo4j 平均加速 9.2 倍（最高 48.6 倍），GraphScope 平均加速 33.4 倍（最高 78.7 倍）。

items.title: SAP HANA Cloud: Data Management for Modern Enterprise Applications.
items.abstract: Cloud Computing environments are a fantastic foundation for unprecedented opportunities in the context of data management. Scalability and availability can be achieved by consequently disaggregating compute and storage and leveraging different data centers around the globe. Although conceptually infinitely available resources promise an easy game for database systems, leveraging those opportunities on the one side and providing enterprise-scale data management solutions for a wide range of extremely challenging business applications on the other side is by far not trivial. Within this paper we share the fundamental principles of SAP HANA Cloud---the data management backbone for all SAP applications---by providing insights into design criteria and technological centerpieces of SAP HANA Cloud as a result of a stringent evolutionary shift from a purely on-premise, in-memory database engine to an on-Cloud, memory-storage hierarchy-aware data management platform. We will motivate the transition and the decision of certain developments by sharing key characteristics of some key applications run by SAP, which require enterprise qualities like availability, cloud-capabilities like resource elasticity, while managing overall costs and the flexibility to adapt the underlying data management system to their specific needs. The overall goal of the paper is to provide insights into the ''story of SAP HANA Cloud'' by sharing challenges and lessons learned within the evolution towards a composable cloud-based data platform, thus strongly arguing for a ''one size fits all'' solution, if done right.
items.abstract_zh: 云计算环境是数据管理领域前所未有的机遇的绝佳基础。通过分解计算和存储并利用全球不同的数据中心可以实现可扩展性和可用性。尽管从概念上讲，无限可用的资源为数据库系统带来了轻松的游戏，但一方面利用这些机会，另一方面为各种极具挑战性的业务应用程序提供企业级数据管理解决方案，这并不是小事。在本文中，我们通过深入了解 SAP HANA Cloud 的设计标准和技术核心，分享 SAP HANA Cloud（所有 SAP 应用程序的数据管理主干）的基本原则，这是从纯粹的本地内存数据库引擎到云端内存存储层次结构感知数据管理平台的严格演进转变的结果。我们将通过共享 SAP 运行的一些关键应用程序的关键特征来推动某些开发的转变和决策，这些应用程序需要可用性等企业品质、资源弹性等云功能，同时管理总体成本以及使底层数据管理系统适应其特定需求的灵活性。本文的总体目标是通过分享在向可组合的基于云的数据平台的演进过程中遇到的挑战和经验教训，提供对“SAP HANA Cloud 的故事”的见解，从而强烈主张如果做得好的话，可以提供“一刀切”的解决方案。

items.title: Interactive Fairness Auditing: Leveraging AVOIR for Dynamic Evaluation and Mitigation.
items.abstract: Ensuring fairness in high-stakes machine learning (ML) applications remains a significant challenge. We introduce an interactive, Streamlit-based User Interface (UI) for AVOIR, an automated fairness monitoring framework. This UI allows users to select fairness metrics (e.g., Demographic Parity, Equalized Odds) and explore dataset attributes through dynamic visualizations. The UI supports iterative refinement, enabling users to define fairness constraints in a domain-specific language (DSL), evaluate models, and address violations via interactive charts. Leveraging AVOIR's inference and optimization capabilities, it provides probabilistic guarantees and detects biases at runtime. We demonstrate its utility on multiple datasets, showing how fairness violations are identified and mitigated. By combining declarative fairness specifications, rigorous optimization, and intuitive visualizations, our UI empowers stakeholders to deploy ML models responsibly.
items.abstract_zh: 确保高风险机器学习 (ML) 应用程序的公平性仍然是一项重大挑战。我们为 AVOIR 引入了一个基于 Streamlit 的交互式用户界面 (UI)，这是一个自动公平性监控框架。该 UI 允许用户选择公平性指标（例如人口统计、平等赔率）并通过动态可视化探索数据集属性。 UI 支持迭代细化，使用户能够以特定领域语言 (DSL) 定义公平性约束、评估模型并通过交互式图表解决违规问题。利用 AVOIR 的推理和优化功能，它提供概率保证并在运行时检测偏差。我们在多个数据集上展示了它的实用性，展示了如何识别和减轻公平违规行为。通过结合声明性公平规范、严格优化和直观可视化，我们的 UI 使利益相关者能够负责任地部署 ML 模型。

items.title: NebulaStream: An Extensible, High-Performance Streaming Engine for Multi-Modal Edge Applications.
items.abstract:
NebulaStream is a novel, open-source data stream processing system for massively distributed, heterogeneous data streams in the cloud-edge continuum. It adheres to the design goals of ease-of-use, extensibility, and efficiency to provide a framework for users and developers to implement diverse Internet of Things (IoT) use cases. Equipped with essential built-in functionalities, NebulaStream allows users to customize the system easily while ensuring efficient execution even on low-end devices.

In this demonstration, we highlight NebulaStream's ability to integrate and process multi-modal, multi-frequency data streams. We showcase its abilities through a real-world IoT scenario where NebulaStream is used to improve the health assessment of patients in a smart intensive care unit.
items.abstract_zh:
NebulaStream 是一种新颖的开源数据流处理系统，适用于云边缘连续体中的大规模分布式异构数据流。它秉承易用性、可扩展性和高效性的设计目标，为用户和开发人员提供实现各种物联网 (IoT) 用例的框架。 NebulaStream配备了必要的内置功能，允许用户轻松定制系统，同时确保即使在低端设备上也能高效执行。

在本次演示中，我们重点介绍 NebulaStream 集成和处理多模式、多频率数据流的能力。我们通过真实的物联网场景展示其能力，其中 NebulaStream 用于改善智能重症监护室患者的健康评估。

items.title: Dynamic Pruning for Recursive Joins.
items.abstract: This paper studies recursive join queries, which are witnessed in recent industrial applications such as event sequence analysis and traceability analysis. The recursive join query has the possibility to invoke deeply nested joins. Known pruning techniques generating pruning filters at compile time may not be able to exploit pruning opportunities, in particular for deep joins, at full. This paper explores dynamic pruning for recursive joins. Dynamic pruning generates pruning filters during query execution; a pruning filter is made based on the result of each recursive step, and it helps to more significantly reduce the amount of relation records to be accessed at the next recursive step, hence improving the query performance. This paper presents the intensive and extensive experiments that we have conducted with a commercial prototype database engine to clarify the performance benefit of dynamic pruning with four business benchmark scenarios. Our observation demonstrates that dynamic pruning significantly speeds up recursive join queries and non-recursive join queries having deeply nested joins; it works up to 135.6 times faster than the baseline case and 3.99 times faster even than the state-of-the-art pruning approach. In addition, our concise filter design only yielded negligibly small processing overheads for ineffective queries, even though the filter was intensively manipulated at runtime.
items.abstract_zh: 本文研究了递归连接查询，这种查询在最近的工业应用中得到了体现，例如事件序列分析和可追溯性分析。递归连接查询可以调用深度嵌套的连接。在编译时生成修剪过滤器的已知修剪技术可能无法充分利用修剪机会，特别是对于深度连接。本文探讨了递归连接的动态修剪。动态剪枝在查询执行期间生成剪枝过滤器；根据每个递归步骤的结果进行剪枝过滤器，有助于更显着地减少下一个递归步骤要访问的关系记录数量，从而提高查询性能。本文介绍了我们使用商业原型数据库引擎进行的密集和广泛的实验，以阐明动态修剪在四个业务基准场景下的性能优势。我们的观察表明，动态修剪显着加速了递归连接查询和具有深度嵌套连接的非递归连接查询；它的工作速度比基准情况快 135.6 倍，甚至比最先进的剪枝方法快 3.99 倍。此外，我们简洁的过滤器设计只为无效查询产生了可以忽略不计的小处理开销，即使过滤器在运行时被密集操作。

items.title: Grafixer: Enabling User-Centric Repairs for Property Graphs.
items.abstract: We introduce Grafixer, an innovative interactive tool that enables human-in-the-loop repair of property graphs. By leveraging user expertise, Grafixer allows collaborative identification and correction of data inconsistencies while ensuring an efficient repair process. Users can upload property graph datasets and define constraints using Cypher, a widely adopted query language. The system efficiently manages overlapping data violations, ensuring that multiple users can work simultaneously on independent repairs without conflicts. Additionally, Grafixer provides an interactive dashboard that allows administrators to monitor real-time statistics, track progress, and oversee the repair process. In our demo, we showcase the complete repair workflow from both the administrator's and users' perspectives. Administrators can configure the repair process, monitor ongoing corrections, and assess the overall status of the graph. Meanwhile, users engage in the repair process by reviewing violations, proposing corrections, and contributing to improving data quality through an intuitive and guided interface.
items.abstract_zh: 我们推出 Grafixer，这是一种创新的交互式工具，可以实现属性图的人机交互修复。通过利用用户的专业知识，Grafixer 可以协作识别和纠正数据不一致，同时确保高效的修复过程。用户可以使用 Cypher（一种广泛采用的查询语言）上传属性图数据集并定义约束。该系统有效地管理重叠的数据违规，确保多个用户可以同时进行独立修复而不会发生冲突。此外，Grafixer 还提供了一个交互式仪表板，允许管理员监控实时统计数据、跟踪进度并监督修复过程。在我们的演示中，我们从管理员和用户的角度展示了完整的修复工作流程。管理员可以配置修复过程、监控正在进行的纠正并评估图表的整体状态。同时，用户通过直观的引导界面审查违规行为、提出纠正建议并帮助提高数据质量，从而参与修复过程。

items.title: How to Build A Brain.
items.abstract: My previous talk at SIGMOD/PODS happened exactly three decades ago. That was the moment when the world of computation and the field of databases were transformed by the advent of the Internet. It was a change that went beyond mere paradigm. We realized that CS is not about the computer at all --- it is about computation, a sublime scientific phenomenon that permeates the universe. CS became a natural science because the Internet seemed to us as mysterious as the cell, the brain, the universe, and the market, and hence we had to approach it through experiments, falsifiable theories, and new applied math. And, because the Internet is about people in a more intimate way than the computer was, at the same time CS became a social science. A new mode of CS research emerged, often referred to as the lens of computation: since computation underlies everything, when computer scientists look at challenging problems in other sciences, unexpected progress often ensues. CS researchers thought productively about game theory and economics, the quantum universe, phase transitions, biology and evolution, social phenomena and the law, the brain. Finally, nearly two decades after that moment in the 1990s, AI happened, a new powerful intellectual tsunami and irresistible frame of mind. In my talk I will contemplate this fascinating story, connecting its twists and turns with the subject of databases. I will conclude with a snapshot of my work over the past decade on understanding how the brain begets the mind: how the activity of individual neurons and synapses results in cognition, behavior, intelligence, and ultimately language, arguably the crowning achievement of the animal brain.
items.abstract_zh: 我上一次在 SIGMOD/PODS 上的演讲发生在三十年前。那时，互联网的出现改变了计算世界和数据库领域。这是一个超越单纯范式的改变。我们意识到计算机科学根本与计算机无关——它与计算有关，这是一种遍布宇宙的崇高科学现象。计算机科学成为一门自然科学，因为互联网对我们来说就像细胞、大脑、宇宙和市场一样神秘，因此我们必须通过实验、可证伪的理论和新的应用数学来接近它。而且，由于互联网以比计算机更亲密的方式与人相关，因此计算机科学同时也成为一门社会科学。一种新的计算机科学研究模式出现了，通常被称为计算的透镜：由于计算是一切的基础，因此当计算机科学家研究其他科学中具有挑战性的问题时，往往会出现意想不到的进展。计算机科学研究人员对博弈论和经济学、量子宇宙、相变、生物学和进化论、社会现象和法律、大脑进行了富有成效的思考。最后，在 20 世纪 90 年代那一刻过去近二十年后，人工智能出现了，这是一场新的强大的知识海啸和不可抗拒的心态。在我的演讲中，我将思考这个引人入胜的故事，将其曲折与数据库主题联系起来。最后，我将概述我过去十年来关于大脑如何产生思维的工作：单个神经元和突触的活动如何导致认知、行为、智力以及最终的语言，这可以说是动物大脑的最高成就。

items.title: LLM-Matcher: A Name-Based Schema Matching Tool using Large Language Models.
items.abstract: We present LLM-Matcher, an interactive name-based schema matching system that utilizes large language models (LLMs) to identify correspondences between source and target schema elements relying solely on their names and descriptions. This tool is specifically designed for restricted environments where instance-based schema matching is not possible, such as the healthcare domain where instance access is often prohibited. LLM-Matcher is based on an extensive experimental study, showing the capabilities of LLMs in the schema matching task. Our system is specifically tailored towards users with sufficient domain knowledge and offers an interpretable initial mapping that can be further refined by providing textual feedback. This feedback allows to rectify model misconceptions as well as improve the quality of schema element descriptions. This paper provides a comprehensive overview of LLM-Matcher, explores its application in the healthcare domain, allows users to gain additional insight into our experimental study, and outlines different steps showcased in the demonstration.
items.abstract_zh: 我们提出了 LLM-Matcher，这是一种基于名称的交互式模式匹配系统，它利用大型语言模型 (LLM) 仅依赖于源模式元素和目标模式元素的名称和描述来识别源模式元素和目标模式元素之间的对应关系。该工具专为无法进行基于实例的架构匹配的受限环境而设计，例如经常禁止实例访问的医疗保健领域。 LLM-Matcher 基于广泛的实验研究，展示了 LLM 在模式匹配任务中的能力。我们的系统专门针对具有足够领域知识的用户量身定制，并提供可解释的初始映射，可以通过提供文本反馈进一步完善该映射。此反馈可以纠正模型误解并提高模式元素描述的质量。本文提供了 LLM-Matcher 的全面概述，探讨了其在医疗保健领域的应用，使用户能够进一步了解我们的实验研究，并概述了演示中展示的不同步骤。

items.title: Real Time Sentinel: An LLM Based PII Detector - A Streaming Integration and Intelligence Platform.
items.abstract: Today's data-driven AI applications need to process, analyze and act on real-time data as it arrives, not wait for it to traverse a slow and complex pipeline. The massive amount of data is continuously generated from multiple sources and they come in a streaming fashion with high volume and high velocity, which makes it hard to process and analyze in real time. We introduce novel Gen AI based enhancements to Striim, a distributed streaming platform that enables unified real-time data integration and intelligence. Striim provides high-throughput, low-latency event processing with a scale- out architecture. It can ingest streaming data from multiple sources, process data with a SQL-like continuous query language, analyze data with sophisticated machine learning and large language models, write data into a variety of targets, and visualize data to support real-time decision making. It guarantees Exactly-Once-Processing semantics and fault tolerance. In this demonstration, we showcase Striim's ability to identify sensitive data over fast-moving data streams in real time using LLMs.
items.abstract_zh: 当今数据驱动的人工智能应用程序需要在实时数据到达时对其进行处理、分析和采取行动，而不是等待它穿过缓慢而复杂的管道。海量数据是从多个来源连续产生的，并且以流的方式产生，数据量大、速度快，这使得实时处理和分析变得困难。我们为 Striim 引入了新颖的基于 Gen AI 的增强功能，Striim 是一个分布式流媒体平台，可实现统一的实时数据集成和智能。 Striim 通过横向扩展架构提供高吞吐量、低延迟的事件处理。它可以从多个来源摄取流数据，使用类似 SQL 的连续查询语言处理数据，使用复杂的机器学习和大型语言模型分析数据，将数据写入各种目标，以及可视化数据以支持实时决策。它保证了 Exactly-Once-Processing 语义和容错能力。在此演示中，我们展示了 Striim 使用 LLM 实时识别快速移动数据流中的敏感数据的能力。

items.title: Alpha Demo: A Hardware-Accelerated Data Model for Ad-Hoc Manipulation of Point Clouds.
items.abstract:
A point cloud is a collection of data points in a three-dimensional space that represents the external surface of an object or environment. This data type is typically generated by spatial sensing devices, like LiDAR (Light Detection and Ranging) systems. While these devices have revolutionized fields such as autonomous vehicles, urban traffic monitoring, and forestry management, the manipulation of point clouds still poses challenges. Applications often involve complex data manipulation and, in some cases, require very low latency. As a result, each application tends to develop its own methods for handling point clouds based on its unique requirements. The common belief is that only through specialized solutions can an application's requirements be met.

In this paper, we demonstrate that complex point cloud manipulations can be developed using generic primitives without compromising latency. To do so, we use a timeless data management approach: We establish a data model for point clouds. The model supports ad-hoc querying and transformations simply by combining the powerful operators we introduce. Along with the model, we also present a specially designed FPGA-based hardware unit that is capable of accelerating some of its critical operators. We call our data model Damocles, the hardware that supports it Palicus, and the system built on top of them Alpha. Our system works on an actual LiDAR sensor connected to Alpha and can run even on an affordable edge-sized device with no noticeable latency.
items.abstract_zh:
点云是三维空间中数据点的集合，表示对象或环境的外表面。这种数据类型通常由空间传感设备生成，例如 LiDAR（光探测和测距）系统。尽管这些设备彻底改变了自动驾驶汽车、城市交通监控和林业管理等领域，但点云的操纵仍然面临挑战。应用程序通常涉及复杂的数据操作，并且在某些情况下需要非常低的延迟。因此，每个应用程序都倾向于根据其独特的需求开发自己的处理点云的方法。人们普遍认为，只有通过专门的解决方案才能满足应用程序的要求。

在本文中，我们证明可以使用通用基元开发复杂的点云操作，而不会影响延迟。为此，我们使用永恒的数据管理方法：我们建立点云数据模型。该模型只需结合我们引入的强大运算符即可支持即席查询和转换。除了该模型之外，我们还展示了一个专门设计的基于 FPGA 的硬件单元，该单元能够加速其一些关键运算符。我们将我们的数据模型称为“达摩克里斯”，支持它的硬件称为“帕利克斯”，以及构建在它们之上的系统“阿尔法”。我们的系统在连接到 Alpha 的实际 LiDAR 传感器上运行，甚至可以在经济实惠的边缘尺寸设备上运行，没有明显的延迟。

items.title: MicroNN: An On-device Disk-resident Updatable Vector Database.
items.abstract: Nearest neighbour search over dense vector collections has important applications in information retrieval, retrieval augmented generation (RAG), and content ranking. Performing efficient search over large vector collections is a well studied problem with many existing approaches and open source implementations. However, most state-of-the-art systems are generally targeted towards scenarios using large servers with an abundance of memory, static vector collections that are not updatable, and nearest neighbour search in isolation of other search criteria. We present Micro Nearest Neighbour (MicroNN), an embedded nearest-neighbour vector search engine designed for scalable similarity search in low-resource environments. MicroNN addresses the problem of on-device vector search for real-world workloads containing updates and hybrid search queries that combine nearest neighbour search with structured attribute filters. In this scenario, memory is highly constrained and disk-efficient index structures and algorithms are required, as well as support for continuous inserts and deletes. MicroNN is an embeddable library that can scale to large vector collections with minimal resources. MicroNN is used in production and powers a wide range of vector search use-cases on-device. MicroNN takes less than 7 ms to retrieve the top-100 nearest neighbours with 90% recall on publicly available million-scale vector benchmark while using ~10 MB of memory.
items.abstract_zh: 密集向量集合上的最近邻搜索在信息检索、检索增强生成 (RAG) 和内容排名中具有重要应用。对大型向量集合执行高效搜索是许多现有方法和开源实现的一个经过充分研究的问题。然而，大多数最先进的系统通常针对使用具有大量内存的大型服务器、不可更新的静态向量集合以及与其他搜索条件隔离的最近邻居搜索的场景。我们推出了微型最近邻 (MicroNN)，这是一种嵌入式最近邻向量搜索引擎，专为在资源匮乏的环境中进行可扩展的相似性搜索而设计。 MicroNN 解决了​​现实世界工作负载的设备上矢量搜索问题，其中包含更新和将最近邻搜索与结构化属性过滤器相结合的混合搜索查询。在这种情况下，内存受到高度限制，需要磁盘高效的索引结构和算法，以及对连续插入和删除的支持。 MicroNN 是一个嵌入式库，可以用最少的资源扩展到大型向量集合。 MicroNN 用于生产并为设备上的各种矢量搜索用例提供支持。 MicroNN 在使用约 10 MB 内存的情况下，只需不到 7 毫秒即可检索前 100 个最近邻，在公开的百万级向量基准测试中召回率达到 90%。

items.title: Demo of LearnedWMP: Workload Memory Prediction Using Deep Query Template Representations.
items.abstract: The database management system (DBMS) relies on a limited amount of working memory for in-memory operations during query execution. Inaccurate estimations can result in suboptimal query performance and potential failures. Achieving precise working memory estimations is therefore crucial to enhance the DBMS efficiency. We present LearnedWMP, a novel approach for simultaneously estimating working memory for a set of queries (i.e., a workload), as opposed to the conventional practice of estimating resources separately for each query. In a series of comprehensive experiments, the LearnedWMP model demonstrates improved accuracy in memory estimation and faster performance during both training and inference when compared to standard methods. The findings highlight the effectiveness and potential impact of the LearnedWMP approach on optimizing query performance. The audience can explore LearnedWMP through various scenarios showcasing its estimation accuracy and internal model workings.
items.abstract_zh: 数据库管理系统 (DBMS) 依赖于有限数量的工作内存来执行查询执行期间的内存操作。不准确的估计可能会导致查询性能不佳和潜在的失败。因此，实现精确的工作记忆估计对于提高 DBMS 效率至关重要。我们提出 LearnedWMP，这是一种同时估计一组查询（即工作负载）的工作内存的新颖方法，而不是为每个查询单独估计资源的传统做法。在一系列综合实验中，与标准方法相比，LearnedWMP 模型证明了内存估计的准确性更高，训练和推理过程中的性能更快。研究结果强调了 LearnedWMP 方法对优化查询性能的有效性和潜在影响。观众可以通过各种场景探索 LearnedWMP，展示其估计准确性和内部模型的工作原理。

items.title: SecUREmatch: Integrating Clerical Review in Privacy-Preserving Record Linkage.
items.abstract: Data integration processes that combine sensitive personal information from different institutions are usually subject to strict privacy regulations. Privacy-preserving record linkage (PPRL) methods can be used in such projects to conceal the identities. However, the quality of such linkages may be low as the parametrization mostly must be done blindly or based on estimations from previous supposedly similar linkage problems. Our framework, SecUREmatch, integrates a multi-layer clerical review system to adapt the matching algorithm to the actual linked data in order to achieve high linkage quality while following the privacy-by-design principle.
items.abstract_zh: 结合来自不同机构的敏感个人信息的数据集成过程通常受到严格的隐私法规的约束。在此类项目中可以使用隐私保护记录链接（PPRL）方法来隐藏身份。然而，这种链接的质量可能较低，因为参数化大多必须盲目地完成或基于先前假定的类似链接问题的估计。我们的框架 SecUREmatch 集成了多层文书审查系统，使匹配算法适应实际链接数据，以实现高链接质量，同时遵循隐私设计原则。

items.title: TD-Join: Leveraging Temporal Dependencies in Time Series Joins.
items.abstract: Time series data is pervasive across domains such as healthcare, finance, and environmental monitoring, where understanding temporal relationships between events is crucial. Traditional time series analysis primarily focuses on numerical similarity measures, often overlooking qualitative temporal relationships. This demonstration introduces TD-Join, a novel tool for temporal reasoning on time series. TD-Join enhances time series subsequence joins by integrating Allen's Algebra, a widely adopted framework for temporal reasoning. Our approach enables users to efficiently identify, query, and interpret temporal relationships such as before, overlaps, meets and equal over similar time series subsequences. By leveraging such relations, our algorithm facilitates more interpretable and robust analysis, improving performance and augmenting the results set, facilitating decision-making processes. The demonstration will showcase real-world scenarios, i.e., healthcare and financial analyses. Attendees will experience interactive visualizations and real-time query capabilities allowing more explainable and insightful time series analysis.
items.abstract_zh: 时间序列数据普遍存在于医疗保健、金融和环境监测等领域，了解事件之间的时间关系至关重要。传统的时间序列分析主要关注数值相似性度量，通常忽略定性时间关系。该演示介绍了 TD-Join，这是一种用于对时间序列进行时间推理的新颖工具。 TD-Join 通过集成艾伦代数（一种广泛采用的时间推理框架）来增强时间序列子序列连接。我们的方法使用户能够有效地识别、查询和解释时间关系，例如相似时间序列子序列上的之前、重叠、相遇和相等。通过利用这种关系，我们的算法可以促进更具可解释性和稳健性的分析，提高性能并扩大结果集，从而促进决策过程。该演示将展示现实世界的场景，即医疗保健和财务分析。与会者将体验交互式可视化和实时查询功能，从而实现更具解释性和洞察力的时间序列分析。

items.title: PatternVis: A Tool for Relational Pattern Visualization.
items.abstract:
We demonstrate PatternVis, an interactive tool that-given a SQL query and its schema-can represent the relational pattern of the query in an extended representation of Relational Diagrams. Notably, PatternVis can represent groupings and aggregation in addition to the non-disjunctive fragment of Relational Diagrams.

Our interactive demo has two main contributions: (1) To the best of our knowledge, this is the first interactive SQL visualization tool that can give an unambiguous representation of nested SQL queries with GROUP BY clauses and aggregation. (2) We present a set of tasks involving multiple-choice questions in which the goal is to identify the correct output of a given aggregation query and database instance. Users can choose to complete each task in one of three modalities (SQL only, PatternVis only, or both) and experience the varying difficulties in each modality.
items.abstract_zh:
我们演示了 PatternVis，这是一个交互式工具，给定 SQL 查询及其模式，可以在关系图的扩展表示中表​​示查询的关系模式。值得注意的是，除了关系图的非分离片段之外，PatternVis 还可以表示分组和聚合。

我们的交互式演示有两个主要贡献：(1) 据我们所知，这是第一个交互式 SQL 可视化工具，可以通过 GROUP BY 子句和聚合给出嵌套 SQL 查询的明确表示。 (2) 我们提出了一组涉及多项选择问题的任务，其中的目标是识别给定聚合查询和数据库实例的正确输出。用户可以选择以三种模式之一（仅 SQL、仅 PatternVis 或两者）来完成每项任务，并体验每种模式中不同的困难。

items.title: Second Workshop on Quantum Computing and Quantum-Inspired Technology for Data-Intensive Systems and Applications (Q-Data).
items.abstract:
Quantum computing has seen significant advancements with the emergence of commercial quantum hardware from major tech companies and startups. These developments, alongside new classical hardware accelerators inspired by quantum technologies, facilitate the rapid solving of complex optimization problems and recently spawned a flurry of research in various communities.

We propose to hold the Q-Data workshop for the 2nd time. The primary objective of this workshop is to explore how quantum computing and related technologies can enhance data processing, data management, data analysis systems, and techniques. It also focuses on hybrid approaches integrating quantum and classical computing methodologies to enhance such data systems and techniques. This workshop will spur new research efforts in this emerging field and pave the way for building next-generation data-intensive systems with quantum computing support.
items.abstract_zh:
随着主要科技公司和初创公司商业量子硬件的出现，量子计算取得了显着进步。这些发展与受量子技术启发的新经典硬件加速器一起，促进了复杂优化问题的快速解决，并且最近在各个社区引发了一系列研究。

我们建议举办第二次 Q-Data 研讨会。本次研讨会的主要目标是探索量子计算和相关技术如何增强数据处理、数据管理、数据分析系统和技术。它还专注于集成量子和经典计算方法的混合方法，以增强此类数据系统和技术。本次研讨会将刺激这一新兴领域的新研究工作，并为构建具有量子计算支持的下一代数据密集型系统铺平道路。

items.title: The Case for Collaboration.
items.abstract:
It's 2025, and the answer to every database performance or optimization problem is ''machine learning.'' But what kinds of models are appropriate for these applications? I'm going to try to convince you that, as in good system design, ''simpler is better.'' And, in this case, simpler has many benefits: simpler models are typically more efficient in both space and time, they are frequently transparently interpretable, and in many domains, they produce accuracy and generalization equivalent to the fanciest deep learning model you can build.

At the same time, I'm going to explain what great collaboration looks like, how it can help you overcome imposter syndrome, and how it helps you find your own personal superpower.
items.abstract_zh:
到了 2025 年，每个数据库性能或优化问题的答案都是“机器学习”。但是什么样的模型适合这些应用程序呢？我将尝试让您相信，正如优秀的系统设计一样，“越简单越好”。而且，在这种情况下，更简单有很多好处：更简单的模型通常在空间和时间上都更高效，它们通常是透明可解释的，并且在许多领域，它们产生的准确性和泛化能力相当于您可以构建的最奇特的深度学习模型。

同时，我将解释什么是伟大的合作，它如何帮助您克服冒名顶替综合症，以及它如何帮助您找到自己的个人超能力。

items.title: Supporting Human-Centric Data Exploration Through Semantics and Natural Language Interaction.
items.abstract:
Data science plays an increasingly central role in decision-making across domains, yet the effectiveness of these decisions hinges not only on sophisticated algorithms but also on how well systems support human interpretation, exploration, and communication of data. This tutorial explores the intersection of semantics, natural language processing (NLP), and human-computer interaction in creating human-centric data exploration tools that promote accessibility, trust, and transparency. This includes techniques for generating perceptually meaningful visual encodings, using NLP for query interpretation and ambiguity resolution, and designing conversational interfaces that align with users' intent. The tutorial will highlight research from a broad set of contributors in the SIGMOD/VLDB, HCI, and visualization communities, spanning natural language interfaces for databases, multimodal interaction systems, semantic search for data repositories, and the use of AI and large language models to augment visual and textual analysis.

As part of this 1.5-hour session, we will present case studies and systems that demonstrate how human-centric design can be integrated into the data analysis pipeline, including tools that support mixed-initiative interaction, adaptive defaults, and subjective query interpretation. We will also discuss open challenges and research opportunities, such as semantic inferencing for unstructured data, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), and ethical considerations around fairness, explainability, and user agency. Drawing from principles in perception, linguistics, AI, and HCI, this tutorial aims to equip attendees with both conceptual frameworks and practical techniques to build more inclusive, interpretable, and intelligent data systems. It is intended for a broad audience in the SIGMOD/VLDB community interested in designing the next generation of data exploration tools that are aligned with human needs.
items.abstract_zh:
数据科学在跨领域的决策中发挥着越来越重要的作用，但这些决策的有效性不仅取决于复杂的算法，还取决于系统如何支持人类对数据的解释、探索和通信。本教程探讨了语义、自然语言处理 (NLP) 和人机交互的交叉点，以创建以人为中心的数据探索工具，以促进可访问性、信任和透明度。这包括生成感知上有意义的视觉编码、使用 NLP 进行查询解释和歧义解决以及设计符合用户意图的对话界面的技术。本教程将重点介绍 SIGMOD/VLDB、HCI 和可视化社区中众多贡献者的研究，涵盖数据库自然语言界面、多模式交互系统、数据存储库语义搜索，以及使用 AI 和大型语言模型来增强视觉和文本分析。

作为这个 1.5 小时会议的一部分，我们将介绍案例研究和系统，展示如何将以人为本的设计集成到数据分析管道中，包括支持混合主动交互、自适应默认值和主观查询解释的工具。我们还将讨论开放的挑战和研究机会，例如非结构化数据的语义推理、检索增强生成（RAG）以及围绕公平性、可解释性和用户代理的道德考虑。本教程借鉴感知、语言学、人工智能和人机交互的原理，旨在为参与者提供概念框架和实用技术，以构建更具包容性、可解释性和智能的数据系统。它面向 SIGMOD/VLDB 社区中对设计符合人类需求的下一代数据探索工具感兴趣的广大受众。

items.title: The 19th International Symposium on Database Programming Languages (DBPL).
items.abstract: Since 1987, DBPL is the home for research that is performed in the thrilling intersection of database query and programming languages. The 2025 edition of this workshop series continues this tradition and publishes work in which database research draws from ideas established in programming language theory, type systems, or compiler and interpreter construction. Likewise, you will find programming-language-centric contributions that bank on techniques originally conceived to design and build database query languages, their optimizers, and executors. Bringing the DB and PL communities together has repeatedly resulted in exciting and unforeseen advances in both fields. We are looking forward to see the same happening during and after DBPL 2025.
items.abstract_zh: 自 1987 年以来，DBPL 一直是数据库查询和编程语言令人兴奋的交叉领域的研究基地。该研讨会系列的 2025 年版本延续了这一传统，并出版了数据库研究借鉴编程语言理论、类型系统或编译器和解释器构造中建立的思想的著作。同样，您会发现以编程语言为中心的贡献，这些贡献依赖于最初设计和构建数据库查询语言、其优化器和执行器的技术。将 DB 和 PL 社区结合在一起已经多次在这两个领域带来令人兴奋和意想不到的进步。我们期待在 DBPL 2025 期间和之后看到同样的情况发生。

items.title: Data Storage and Management for Image AI Pipelines.
items.abstract:
Image AI is essential for various applications, such as self-driving cars, medical imaging, and smart farming. Data management is key for efficient image AI, from how to store images to how to manage data while processing the images. This tutorial overviews the emerging area of image AI pipelines by combining approaches from various cross-disciplinary areas such as data management, digital signal processing, computer vision, and machine learning. We specifically focus on image storage and data management.

The tutorial first gives an overview of image AI pipelines step by step, how they work and the main the challenges. We then describe the main approaches to making image AI pipelines more efficient. We first cover how image AI pipelines store images based on standard storage formats, learned formats, task-specific learned formats, and self-designed formats. Second, we cover how state-of-the-art approaches manage data within image AI pipelines. We identify and describe three main approaches to making image AI pipelines more efficient by efficiently managing data within the pipeline: (i) compressing intermediate data, (ii) materializing and re-using data objects, and (iii) parallelism for better hardware utilization. Lastly, the tutorial covers open data management and systems problems and future directions in making image AI pipelines more efficient.
items.abstract_zh:
图像人工智能对于自动驾驶汽车、医学成像和智能农业等各种应用至关重要。数据管理是高效图像人工智能的关键，从如何存储图像到如何在处理图像时管理数据。本教程结合数据管理、数字信号处理、计算机视觉和机器学习等各种跨学科领域的方法，概述了图像 AI 管道的新兴领域。我们特别关注图像存储和数据管理。

本教程首先逐步概述图像 AI 管道、它们的工作原理以及主要挑战。然后，我们描述了提高图像 AI 管道效率的主要方法。我们首先介绍图像人工智能管道如何基于标准存储格式、学习格式、特定于任务的学习格式和自行设计的格式来存储图像。其次，我们介绍最先进的方法如何管理图像人工智能管道中的数据。我们确定并描述了通过有效管理管道内的数据来提高图像人工智能管道效率的三种主要方法：（i）压缩中间数据，（ii）具体化和重用数据对象，以及（iii）并行性以提高硬件利用率。最后，本教程涵盖了开放数据管理和系统问题以及提高图像 AI 管道效率的未来方向。

items.title: Streaming Democratized: Ease Across the Latency Spectrum with Delayed View Semantics and Snowflake Dynamic Tables.
items.abstract:
Streaming data pipelines remain challenging and expensive to build and maintain, despite significant advancements in stronger consistency, event time semantics, and SQL support over the last decade. Persistent obstacles continue to hinder usability, such as the need for manual incrementalization, semantic discrepancies across SQL implementations, and the lack of enterprise-grade operational features (e.g. granular access control, disaster recovery). While the rise of incremental view maintenance (IVM) as a way to integrate streaming with databases has been a huge step forward, transaction isolation in the presence of IVM remains underspecified, which leaves the maintenance of application-level invariants as a painful exercise for the user. Meanwhile, most streaming systems optimize for latencies of 100 milliseconds to 3 seconds, whereas many practical use cases are well-served by latencies ranging from seconds to tens of minutes.

In this paper, we present delayed view semantics (DVS), a conceptual foundation that bridges the semantic gap between streaming and databases, and introduce Dynamic Tables, Snowflake's declarative streaming transformation primitive designed to democratize analytical stream processing. DVS formalizes the intuition that stream processing is primarily a technique to eagerly compute derived results asynchronously, while also addressing the need to reason about the resulting system end to end. Dynamic Tables then offer two key advantages: ease of use through DVS, enterprise-grade features, and simplicity; as well as scalable cost efficiency via IVM with an architecture designed for diverse latency requirements. We first develop extensions to transaction isolation that permit the preservation of invariants in streaming applications. We then detail the implementation challenges of Dynamic Tables and our experience operating it at scale. Finally, we share insights into user adoption and discuss our vision for the future of stream processing.
items.abstract_zh:
尽管过去十年在更强的一致性、事件时间语义和 SQL 支持方面取得了显着进步，但流数据管道的构建和维护仍然具有挑战性且昂贵。持续存在的障碍继续阻碍可用性，例如需要手动增量、SQL 实现之间的语义差异以及缺乏企业级操作功能（例如粒度访问控制、灾难恢复）。虽然增量视图维护 (IVM) 作为一种将流媒体与数据库集成的方式的兴起已经向前迈出了一大步，但 IVM 存在下的事务隔离仍然没有明确规定，这使得应用程序级不变量的维护对于用户来说是一项痛苦的练习。同时，大多数流媒体系统针对 100 毫秒到 3 秒的延迟进行优化，而许多实际用例的延迟范围从几秒到几十分钟不等。

在本文中，我们提出了延迟视图语义 (DVS)，这是弥合流处理和数据库之间语义差距的概念基础，并介绍了动态表，这是 Snowflake 的声明性流处理转换原语，旨在使分析流处理民主化。 DVS 形式化了这样的直觉：流处理主要是一种急切地异步计算派生结果的技术，同时还解决了对结果系统进行端到端推理的需要。动态表提供了两个关键优势：通过 DVS 的易用性、企业级功能和简单性；以及通过 IVM 和专为不同延迟要求设计的架构实现可扩展的成本效率。我们首先开发事务隔离的扩展，允许在流应用程序中保留不变量。然后，我们详细介绍动态表的实施挑战以及我们大规模运营它的经验。最后，我们分享了对用户采用情况的见解，并讨论了我们对流处理未来的愿景。

items.title: Asynchronous Replication Strategies for a Real-Time DBMS.
items.abstract:
Real-time mission-critical services that handle large-scale consumer facing applications typically use a real-time DBMS that can provide both high performance as well as high availability. The only sure way to ensure that such services can survive catastrophic infrastructure failures is to deploy the DBMS as well as the applications themselves on multiple sites that are hundreds (or even thousands) of miles apart from each other.

Although synchronous replication across sites can be used for some consumer services, most high-performance consumer applications cannot tolerate the high write latencies required for synchronizing writes across far-apart sites. Therefore, the use of asynchronous replication between geographically distributed sites becomes the only practical option for these applications. This means that any changes in the data must be replicated quickly across sites to keep the exposure window as low as possible in the case of a major site failure event.

In this paper, we describe a resource-optimized, asynchronous replication strategy called XDR that we developed for use with a real-time DBMS (Aerospike) that supports very high read and write throughput. XDR is resource optimized because it is co-designed within the same process as the core database. This enables replication between sites to proceed at a high throughput despite the high latency between sites that are thousands of miles apart, while not impacting the latency and throughput of the source transactions. Furthermore, this co-design allows for efficient implementation of several other features within XDR such as i) filtering data during replication and ii) allowing multiple sites to update the same record simultaneously while ensuring the sites converge on the content of the records quickly. XDR has been used to deploy more than 100 large-scale customer applications worldwide.
items.abstract_zh:
处理面向消费者的大规模应用程序的实时关键任务服务通常使用可以提供高性能和高可用性的实时 DBMS。确保此类服务能够在灾难性基础设施故障中幸存下来的唯一可靠方法是将 DBMS 以及应用程序本身部署在彼此相距数百（甚至数千）英里的多个站点上。

尽管跨站点同步复制可用于某些消费者服务，但大多数高性能消费者应用程序无法容忍跨相距较远的站点同步写入所需的高写入延迟。因此，在地理上分布的站点之间使用异步复制成为这些应用程序的唯一实用选择。这意味着数据中的任何更改都必须跨站点快速复制，以在发生重大站点故障事件时保持尽可能低的暴露窗口。

在本文中，我们描述了一种名为 XDR 的资源优化异步复制策略，该策略是我们开发的，用于支持极高读写吞吐量的实时 DBMS (Aerospike)。 XDR 是资源优化型的，因为它是在与核心数据库相同的流程中共同设计的。这使得站点之间的复制能够以高吞吐量进行，尽管相距数千英里的站点之间存在高延迟，同时不会影响源事务的延迟和吞吐量。此外，这种协同设计允许有效实现 XDR 中的几个其他功能，例如 i) 在复制期间过滤数据，以及 ii) 允许多个站点同时更新同一记录，同时确保站点快速收敛于记录的内容。 XDR 已用于在全球范围内部署 100 多个大型客户应用程序。

items.title: Database as Runtime: Compiling LLMs to SQL for In-database Model Serving.
items.abstract: Deploying large language models (LLMs) often requires specialized hardware and complex frameworks, creating barriers for CPU-based environments with resource constraints. These systems, common in air-gapped or edge scenarios, lack support for maintenance due to security, budget, or technical limits. To address this, we introduce TranSQL + , a compiler that translates LLM inference into SQL queries, enabling deployment on relational databases. By converting transformer operations into relational algebra, TranSQL + generates vector-oriented SQL queries that leverage native database features (buffer management, indexing) to manage computations without hardware accelerators or deep learning frameworks. Demonstrated with the LLaMA3.1 8B model on DuckDB, results show relational databases can effectively serve LLMs, reducing deployment barriers and expanding access to advanced AI.
items.abstract_zh: 部署大型语言模型 (LLM) 通常需要专门的硬件和复杂的框架，这为具有资源限制的基于 CPU 的环境造成了障碍。这些系统常见于气隙或边缘场景，由于安全、预算或技术限制而缺乏维护支持。为了解决这个问题，我们引入了 TranSQL +，这是一个将 LLM 推理转换为 SQL 查询的编译器，从而可以在关系数据库上进行部署。通过将转换器操作转换为关系代数，TranSQL + 生成面向向量的 SQL 查询，利用本机数据库功能（缓冲区管理、索引）来管理计算，无需硬件加速器或深度学习框架。通过 DuckDB 上的 LLaMA3.1 8B 模型进行演示，结果表明关系数据库可以有效地为法学硕士提供服务，减少部署障碍并扩大对高级人工智能的访问。

items.title: D-Bot: An LLM-Powered DBA Copilot.
items.abstract: Database administrators (DBAs) play an important role in maintaining the high performance and high availability of database systems. However, database diagnosis by DBAs often takes tedious efforts and time, which is insufficient for the large amount of database instances (e.g., millions of instances on the cloud). Besides, existing diagnosis tools built with rules and small-scale learned models lack the capability for flexible reasoning and report generation, and thus can only serve as ''one small piece of a bigger puzzle''. Recently, Large language models (LLMs) have exhibited superiority in natural language understanding, reasoning and generation, positioning them as a potential comprehensive copilot to DBAs to address these limitations. Thus, we introduce D-Bot , an LLM-powered DBA copilot that can automatically acquire pertinent knowledge from diagnostic documents, interact with users for self-refinement, and generate reasonable and well-founded diagnosis reports (i.e., specifying root causes, solutions, and references). We will show that D-Bot can effectively automate database diagnosis in real scenarios, even for complex anomalies.
items.abstract_zh: 数据库管理员（DBA）在维护数据库系统的高性能和高可用性方面发挥着重要作用。然而，DBA的数据库诊断往往需要繁琐的工作和时间，这对于大量数据库实例（例如云上数百万个实例）来说是不够的。此外，现有的基于规则和小规模学习模型构建的诊断工具缺乏灵活的推理和报告生成的能力，因此只能充当“更大拼图中的一小部分”。最近，大型语言模型 (LLM) 在自然语言理解、推理和生成方面表现出了优越性，将其定位为 DBA 解决这些局限性的潜在综合副驾驶。因此，我们引入了 D-Bot，一个由 LLM 驱动的 DBA 副驾驶，可以自动从诊断文档中获取相关知识，与用户交互进行自我完善，并生成合理且有依据的诊断报告（即指定根本原因、解决方案和参考）。我们将展示 D-Bot 可以在实际场景中有效地自动化数据库诊断，即使是复杂的异常情况。

items.title: CockroachDB Serverless: Sub-second Scaling from Zero with Multi-region Cluster Virtualization.
items.abstract: The Serverless database paradigm offers the promise of letting users pay for only what they consume, and scaling quickly and transparently as workload demands change. In this paper, we present novel extensions to CockroachDB that allow for the deployment of a multi-tenant, multi-region transactional database, which can be consumed by users in a Serverless fashion. The key architectural change enabling this new deployment model is a two-tiered approach that separates the SQL and key-value (KV) layers into different processes. This separation enables independent scaling, efficient resource sharing, and tenant isolation. We describe how we have deployed this system in a production service offering, generally available since 2022, and how we achieve scaling-to-zero, sub-second cold starts, and data and performance isolation.
items.abstract_zh: 无服务器数据库范式承诺让用户只为他们使用的内容付费，并随着工作负载需求的变化快速、透明地扩展。在本文中，我们提出了 CockroachDB 的新颖扩展，允许部署多租户、多区域事务数据库，用户可以以无服务器方式使用该数据库。支持这种新部署模型的关键架构变化是一种两层方法，它将 SQL 和键值 (KV) 层分离到不同的进程中。这种分离可以实现独立扩展、高效资源共享和租户隔离。我们描述了如何在自 2022 年起普遍提供的生产服务产品中部署该系统，以及如何实现零扩展、亚秒级冷启动以及数据和性能隔离。

items.title: First Workshop on Novel Optimizations for Visionary AI Systems (NOVAS).
items.abstract:
The first NOVAS workshop (Novel Optimizations for Visionary AI Systems) is aimed at hosting novel work at the intersection between artificial intelligence and data management. This area has emerged with the rise of transformer-based architectures, which have revolutionized data processing across modalities. While these models benefit from massive pre-training and large-context inference, there are significant challenges related to scalability, determinism, and resource constraints. These issues-long studied in the data management community-have sparked a convergence between generative AI and traditional database research.

The workshop will be held on June 22nd, in conjunction with SIGMOD/PODS 2025. The workshop solicits regular and short papers on topics including hardware and execution optimizations, high-level programming abstractions, integration of LLMs with relational databases, and new transformer architectures for structured data. By bridging together the different communities of machine learning, data systems, and information retrieval, NOVAS aims at becoming the venue to discuss, share ideas and early results, and spark new research collaborations for the next-generation of data-driven AI systems.
items.abstract_zh:
首届 NOVAS 研讨会（有远见的人工智能系统的新颖优化）旨在举办人工智能和数据管理交叉领域的新颖工作。随着基于变压器的架构的兴起，这一领域出现了，它彻底改变了跨模式的数据处理。虽然这些模型受益于大规模预训练和大上下文推理，但仍存在与可扩展性、确定性和资源限制相关的重大挑战。这些问题在数据管理社区中长期研究，引发了生成式人工智能和传统数据库研究之间的融合。

该研讨会将于 6 月 22 日与 SIGMOD/PODS 2025 同期举行。该研讨会征集定期论文和短论文，主题包括硬件和执行优化、高级编程抽象、法学硕士与关系数据库的集成以及结构化数据的新变压器架构。通过将机器学习、数据系统和信息检索的不同社区联系在一起，NOVAS 旨在成为讨论、分享想法和早期成果的场所，并为下一代数据驱动的人工智能系统激发新的研究合作。

items.title: Managed Resource Scaling in Amazon EMR.
items.abstract: Compute elasticity is a primary benefit of using cloud-based data processing platforms such as Amazon EMR, where clusters can be scaled both horizontally and vertically. For example, a query scanning petabytes of data can run faster in a cluster with thousands of nodes compared to one with only a few hundred. However, not all workloads require the same computational power or have the same resource utilization patterns throughout their lifetime. Optimizing solely for performance by over-provisioning clusters can result in extra costs for customers stemming from unused capacity. Under-provisioning clusters, on the other hand, will keep costs low but can delay job completion time and cause SLAs to be missed. To further complicate the problem, a single cluster may run concurrent workloads, each with different resource needs. Typical solutions to managing time-varying resource needs involve frequent manual cluster rescaling or using different-sized clusters for different types of queries, which increases the cluster administrator's workload. This paper presents Amazon EMR Managed Scaling, a feature that continuously and automatically resizes EMR clusters with a goal to optimize the cost/performance ratio, with minimal user input. We describe how we iteratively built our engine-agnostic scaling approach that uses a cluster's telemetry, topology and past behavior to adjust its resource utilization in response to changing resource requirements. We show how each of the individual techniques presented herein solve specific workload execution patterns, and how EMR combines them into a single, unified algorithm that can make correct scaling decisions within seconds.
items.abstract_zh: 计算弹性是使用基于云的数据处理平台（例如 Amazon EMR）的主要优势，其中集群可以水平和垂直扩展。例如，与只有几百个节点的集群相比，扫描 PB 数据的查询在具有数千个节点的集群中运行得更快。然而，并非所有工作负载在其整个生命周期中都需要相同的计算能力或具有相同的资源利用模式。仅通过过度配置集群来优化性能可能会因未使用的容量而给客户带来额外成本。另一方面，配置不足的集群可以保持较低的成本，但会延迟作业完成时间并导致错过 SLA。使问题进一步复杂化的是，单个集群可能运行并发工作负载，每个工作负载都有不同的资源需求。管理随时间变化的资源需求的典型解决方案涉及频繁的手动集群重新扩展或针对不同类型的查询使用不同大小的集群，这会增加集群管理员的工作量。本文介绍了 Amazon EMR Managed Scaling，这是一项能够连续自动调整 EMR 集群大小的功能，旨在以最少的用户输入优化成本/性能比。我们描述了如何迭代地构建与引擎无关的扩展方法，该方法使用集群的遥测、拓扑和过去的行为来调整其资源利用率，以响应不断变化的资源需求。我们展示了本文中介绍的每种单独技术如何解决特定的工作负载执行模式，以及 EMR 如何将它们组合成一个统一的算法，以便在几秒钟内做出正确的扩展决策。

items.title: Andromeda: Debugging Database Performance Issues with Retrieval-Augmented Large Language Models.
items.abstract: Debugging performance issues in a database management system (DBMS) is tedious and challenging, even for experienced database administrators (DBAs). Thus, with the rapid advancement of large language models (LLMs), developing an LLM-powered co-pilot to assist or even replace DBAs by automatically diagnosing issues and generating recommendations for resolution presents a promising direction. However, directly prompting LLMs for DBMS performance debugging often yields either generic or irrelevant responses, as LLMs may lack both domain knowledge in performance debugging and a deep understanding of DBMS internals. In this paper, we introduce Andromeda, a retrieval-augmented, LLM-powered system for automatic DBMS performance debugging. Andromeda enables users to pose natural language questions about various performance issues and provides context-aware and actionable recommendations for resolution. To achieve this, Andromeda first retrieves relevant evidence from multiple sources, including historical questions, troubleshooting manuals, DBMS telemetry data, and execution logs. It then leverages effective training strategies to adapt an open-source LLM, empowering it to diagnose and resolve issues based on the retrieved evidence. We have developed a web application for Andromeda and demonstrated its effectiveness in debugging real-world DBMS performance issues.
items.abstract_zh: 即使对于经验丰富的数据库管理员 (DBA) 来说，调试数据库管理系统 (DBMS) 中的性能问题也是乏味且具有挑战性的。因此，随着大语言模型（LLM）的快速发展，开发一个由 LLM 驱动的副驾驶，通过自动诊断问题并生成解决建议来协助甚至取代 DBA，这是一个有前途的方向。然而，直接提示 LLM 进行 DBMS 性能调试通常会产生通用或不相关的响应，因为 LLM 可能缺乏性能调试方面的领域知识和对 DBMS 内部结构的深入理解。在本文中，我们介绍了 Andromeda，这是一个由 LLM 驱动的检索增强系统，用于自动 DBMS 性能调试。 Andromeda 使用户能够提出有关各种性能问题的自然语言问题，并提供上下文感知和可操作的解决建议。为了实现这一目标，Andromeda 首先从多个来源检索相关证据，包括历史问题、故障排除手册、DBMS 遥测数据和执行日志。然后，它利用有效的培训策略来调整开源法学硕士，使其能够根据检索到的证据诊断和解决问题。我们为 Andromeda 开发了一个 Web 应用程序，并证明了其在调试实际 DBMS 性能问题方面的有效性。

items.title: TXSQL: Lock Optimizations Towards High Contented Workloads.
items.abstract:
Two-phase locking (2PL) is a fundamental and widely used concurrency control protocol. It regulates concurrent access to database data by following a specific sequence of acquiring and releasing locks during transaction execution, thereby ensuring transaction isolation. However, in strict 2PL, transactions must wait for conflicting transactions to commit and release their locks, which reduces concurrency and system throughput. We have observed this issue is exacerbated in high-contented workloads at Tencent, where lock contention can severely degrade system performance. While existing optimizations demonstrate some effectiveness in high-contention scenarios, their performance remains insufficient, as they suffer from lock contention and waiting in hotspot access.

This paper presents optimizations in lock management implemented in Tencent's database, TXSQL, with a particular focus on high-contention scenarios. First, we discuss our motivations and the journey toward general lock optimization, which includes lightweight lock management, a copy-free active transaction list, and queue locking mechanisms that effectively enhance concurrency. Second, we introduce a hotspot-aware approach that enables certain highly conflicting transactions to switch to a group locking method, which groups conflicting transactions at a specific hotspot, allowing them to execute serially in an uncommitted state within a conflict group without the need for locking, thereby reducing lock contention. Our evaluation shows that under high-contented workloads, TXSQL achieves performance improvements of up to 6.5x and up to 22.3x compared to state-of-the-art methods and systems, respectively.
items.abstract_zh:
两阶段锁定（2PL）是一种基本且广泛使用的并发控制协议。它通过在事务执行过程中遵循特定的获取和释放锁的顺序来调节对数据库数据的并发访问，从而保证事务隔离。然而，在严格的2PL中，事务必须等待冲突事务提交并释放其锁，这会降低并发性和系统吞吐量。我们观察到这个问题在腾讯的高负载工作负载中更加严重，锁争用会严重降低系统性能。虽然现有的优化在高争用场景中表现出一定的有效性，但它们的性能仍然不足，因为它们会遭受锁争用和热点访问等待的问题。

本文介绍了在腾讯数据库 TXSQL 中实现的锁管理优化，特别关注高争用场景。首先，我们讨论我们的动机和一般锁优化的历程，其中包括轻量级锁管理、无副本的活动事务列表以及有效增强并发性的队列锁定机制。其次，我们引入了一种热点感知方法，使某些高度冲突的事务能够切换到组锁定方法，该方法将冲突事务分组在特定的热点处，允许它们在冲突组内以未提交状态串行执行，而不需要锁定，从而减少锁争用。我们的评估表明，在高负载工作负载下，与最先进的方法和系统相比，TXSQL 的性能分别提高了 6.5 倍和 22.3 倍。

items.title: Automated Database Tuning vs. Human-Based Tuning in a Simulated Stressful Work Environment: A Demonstration of the Database Gym.
items.abstract: Machine learning (ML) has gained traction in academia and industry for database management system (DBMS) automation. Although studies demonstrate that ML-based tuning agents match or exceed human expert performance in optimizing DBMSs, researchers continue to build bespoke tuning pipelines from the ground up. The lack of a reusable infrastructure leads to redundant engineering effort and increased difficulty in comparing modeling methods. This paper demonstrates the database gym framework, a standardized training environment that provides a unified API of pluggable components. The database gym simplifies ML model training and evaluation to accelerate autonomous DBMS research. In this demonstration, we showcase the effectiveness of automated tuning and the gym's ease of use by allowing a human expert to compete against an ML-based tuning agent implemented in the gym.
items.abstract_zh: 机器学习 (ML) 在学术界和工业界的数据库管理系统 (DBMS) 自动化方面获得了广泛关注。尽管研究表明基于 ML 的调优代理在优化 DBMS 方面可达到或超过人类专家的表现，但研究人员仍在从头开始构建定制调优管道。缺乏可重用的基础设施会导致冗余的工程工作，并增加比较建模方法的难度。本文演示了数据库gym框架，这是一个标准化的训练环境，提供了可插拔组件的统一API。数据库健身房简化了 ML 模型训练和评估，以加速自主 DBMS 研究。在此演示中，我们通过允许人类专家与健身房中实施的基于 ML 的调整代理进行竞争，展示了自动调整的有效性和健身房的易用性。

items.title: SemExplorer: A User Interface for Semantic Approach to Customized Dataset Search.
items.abstract: In response to the increasing complexity of data and the widespread availability of diverse datasets for modeling complex relationships, there is a crucial need for efficient and customizable search capabilities. Many data scientists and researchers still need to manually browse through extensive catalogs to find suitable datasets for their studies. To address this challenge, we introduce SemExplorer, a framework designed to enhance semantic analysis and information retrieval for user-defined needs. Our system processes and interprets complex queries in natural language using large language models (LLMs), converting them into vector representations and statistical filters. When presenting search results, our system uses context dependencies to highlight important information, helping users quickly locate the results they need. Overall, SemExplorer is a robust tool that not only improves the efficiency of finding needed datasets but also enhances the precision of search outcomes by leveraging semantic analysis to deeply understand and filter network data. A demo of our system is available online.
items.abstract_zh: 为了应对日益复杂的数据以及用于建模复杂关系的各种数据集的广泛可用性，迫切需要高效且可定制的搜索功能。许多数据科学家和研究人员仍然需要手动浏览大量目录以找到适合其研究的数据集。为了应对这一挑战，我们引入了 SemExplorer，这是一个旨在增强语义分析和信息检索以满足用户定义需求的框架。我们的系统使用大型语言模型 (LLM) 处理和解释自然语言中的复杂查询，将其转换为向量表示和统计过滤器。在呈现搜索结果时，我们的系统使用上下文依赖关系来突出显示重要信息，帮助用户快速找到所需的结果。总体而言，SemExplorer 是一个强大的工具，不仅提高了查找所需数据集的效率，还通过利用语义分析深入理解和过滤网络数据来提高搜索结果的精度。我们的系统的演示可以在线获取。

items.title: OIE: An Interpretable System for Outlier Explanation and Summarization.
items.abstract:
Outlier detection is crucial for preventing financial fraud, network intrusions, and device failures. However, while existing methods excel at identifying outliers, they often fall short of providing clear and interpretable explanations. This limitation forces users to manually analyze numerous outliers, making the process both time-consuming and inefficient. Additionally, current summarization approaches often focus only on data attributes, ignoring that outliers in the same subspace may have different causes. Consequently,these methods produce broad summaries that make quick diagnosis difficult.

To address these challenges, we propose OIE, a system that generates interpretable, fine-grained rules to summarize and explain outlier detection results. OIE leverages decision trees to generate concise rules, balancing simplicity and classification accuracy. Additionally, it employs dynamic data partitioning and a boundary stabilizer to efficiently handle high-dimensional and complex datasets.Through multiple real-world scenarios, OIE demonstrates effective anomaly detection and summarization, providing actionable insights and enhancing the efficiency of outlier analysis. A demonstration video of OIE is available at:https://youtu.be/YzfDdF9f5HI.
items.abstract_zh:
异常值检测对于防止金融欺诈、网络入侵和设备故障至关重要。然而，虽然现有方法擅长识别异常值，但它们往往无法提供清晰且可解释的解释。这种限制迫使用户手动分析大量异常值，使得该过程既耗时又低效。此外，当前的汇总方法通常只关注数据属性，而忽略了同一子空间中的异常值可能有不同的原因。因此，这些方法产生广泛的总结，导致快速诊断变得困难。

为了应对这些挑战，我们提出了 OIE，这是一个生成可解释的细粒度规则来总结和解释异常值检测结果的系统。 OIE 利用决策树生成简洁的规则，平衡简单性和分类准确性。此外，它还采用动态数据分区和边界稳定器来有效处理高维和复杂的数据集。通过多个现实场景，OIE 展示了有效的异常检测和总结，提供了可操作的见解并提高了异常值分析的效率。 OIE 的演示视频请访问：https://youtu.be/YzfDdF9f5HI。

items.title: DataDazzle: Intelligent Data Exploration through Natural Language.
items.abstract: For over five decades, enabling non-technical users to access databases without SQL has been a major research focus. Despite recent advances in Text-to-SQL, the primary focus remains on accuracy, often overlooking real-world deployment challenges. This demo presents DataDazzle, a natural language data exploration system. It allows users to query data, review and verify answers, and benefit from recommendations, offering a comprehensive data exploration experience.
items.abstract_zh: 五十多年来，使非技术用户无需 SQL 即可访问数据库一直是主要的研究焦点。尽管文本到 SQL 领域最近取得了进展，但主要关注点仍然是准确性，往往忽视了现实世界的部署挑战。该演示展示了 DataDazzle，一个自然语言数据探索系统。它允许用户查询数据、查看和验证答案并从建议中受益，从而提供全面的数据探索体验。

items.title: Unlocking the Potential of CXL for Disaggregated Memory in Cloud-Native Databases.
items.abstract:
Memory disaggregation has become a major trend in cloud-native databases. However, most existing memory disaggregation solutions suffer from read/write amplification, limited bandwidth, inefficient recovery, and challenges in data sharing. Fortunately, the emerging CXL technology introduces new opportunities for memory disaggregation design in cloud-native databases.

To overcome these challenges, we leverage the CXL switch to design PolarCXLMem , a CXL switch-based disaggregated memory system for cloud-native databases. To the best of our knowledge, PolarCXLMem is the first CXL switch-based disaggregated memory. Building on PolarCXLMem , we propose a novel instant recovery scheme, PolarRecv, which enables instant recovery and fast buffer pool warm-up after a crash. To further support PolarCXLMem in multi-primary databases, we design a new cache coherency protocol that facilitates data sharing between database nodes based on PolarCXLMem . Finally, we evaluate PolarCXLMem with PolarDB, a widely deployed cloud-native database, under various workloads. This is the first study, to our knowledge, that investigates the performance of CXL-based disaggregated memory in a commercially deployed cloud-native database. Our evaluation shows that PolarCXLMem can improve throughput by up to 2.1× in pooling scenarios and 1.55× in sharing scenarios compared to RDMA-based systems.
items.abstract_zh:
内存分解已成为云原生数据库的一大趋势。然而，大多数现有的内存分解解决方案都存在读/写放大、带宽有限、恢复效率低下以及数据共享方面的挑战等问题。幸运的是，新兴的 CXL 技术为云原生数据库中的内存分解设计带来了新的机会。

为了克服这些挑战，我们利用 CXL 交换机设计了 PolarCXLMem，这是一种用于云原生数据库的基于 CXL 交换机的分类内存系统。据我们所知，PolarCXLMem 是首款基于 CXL 开关的分解内存。在 PolarCXLMem 的基础上，我们提出了一种新颖的即时恢复方案 PolarRecv，它可以在崩溃后实现即时恢复和快速缓冲池预热。为了进一步支持多主数据库中的PolarCXLMem，我们设计了一种新的缓存一致性协议，以促进基于PolarCXLMem的数据库节点之间的数据共享。最后，我们使用广泛部署的云原生数据库 PolarDB 在各种工作负载下评估 PolarCXLMem。据我们所知，这是第一项研究商业部署的云原生数据库中基于 CXL 的分类内存的性能。我们的评估表明，与基于 RDMA 的系统相比，PolarCXLMem 在池化场景中可以将吞吐量提高高达 2.1 倍，在共享场景中可以将吞吐量提高高达 1.55 倍。

items.title: Virtualizing Cloud Data Infrastructures with BRAD.
items.abstract: Organizations usually manage their data using multiple specialized cloud database engines (e.g., Aurora, BigQuery, etc.). However, designing and managing multi-engine infrastructures is hard; there can be many designs, each with different performance and costs. Changing the design afterwards (e.g., due to growth) is even more challenging since application code usually ends up tightly coupled to the engines. We propose data infrastructure virtualization . The key idea is to declare a set of virtual database engines (VDBEs), which specify an engine's application-facing properties (e.g., query interface, performance) and its tables, but do not prescribe a concrete engine. An automated planner then decides how to best realize the VDBEs onto physical engines based on the workload. Clients connect to VDBE endpoints and are oblivious to the underlying physical engines-allowing for seamless infrastructure changes. We implemented VDBEs and an automated planner in BRAD: the first data infrastructure virtualization runtime. Our demo will showcase VDBEs and BRAD's automated planner under different workloads.
items.abstract_zh: 组织通常使用多个专用云数据库引擎（例如 Aurora、BigQuery 等）来管理数据。然而，设计和管理多引擎基础设施很困难；可以有多种设计，每种设计都有不同的性能和成本。之后更改设计（例如，由于增长）甚至更具挑战性，因为应用程序代码通常最终与引擎紧密耦合。我们提出数据基础设施虚拟化。关键思想是声明一组虚拟数据库引擎（VDBE），它指定引擎面向应用程序的属性（例如查询接口、性能）及其表，但不规定具体的引擎。然后，自动规划器根据工作负载决定如何在物理引擎上最好地实现 VDBE。客户端连接到 VDBE 端点，并且不关心底层物理引擎 - 允许无缝的基础设施更改。我们在 BRAD 中实现了 VDBE 和自动规划器：第一个数据基础设施虚拟化运行时。我们的演示将展示不同工作负载下的 VDBE 和 BRAD 的自动规划器。

items.title: Including Bloom Filters in Bottom-up Optimization.
items.abstract: Bloom filters are used in query processing to perform early data reduction and improve query performance. The optimal query plan may be different when Bloom filters are used, indicating the need for Bloom filter-aware query optimization. To date, Bloom filter-aware query optimization has only been incorporated in a top-down query optimizer and limited to snowflake queries. In this paper, we show how Bloom filters can be incorporated in a bottom-up cost-based query optimizer. We highlight the challenges in limiting optimizer search space expansion, and offer an efficient solution. We show that including Bloom filters in cost-based optimization can lead to better join orders with effective predicate transfer between operators. On a 100 GB instance of the TPC-H database, our approach achieved a 32.8% further reduction in latency for queries involving Bloom filters, compared to the traditional approach of adding Bloom filters in a separate post-optimization step. Our method applies to all query types, and we provide several heuristics to balance limited increases in optimization time against improved query latency.
items.abstract_zh: 布隆过滤器用于查询处理中，以执行早期数据缩减并提高查询性能。当使用布隆过滤器时，最优查询计划可能会有所不同，这表明需要布隆过滤器感知的查询优化。迄今为止，布隆过滤器感知的查询优化仅被纳入自上而下的查询优化器中，并且仅限于雪花查询。在本文中，我们展示了如何将布隆过滤器合并到自下而上的基于成本的查询优化器中。我们强调限制优化器搜索空间扩展的挑战，并提供有效的解决方案。我们证明，在基于成本的优化中包含布隆过滤器可以通过运算符之间的有效谓词传输来实现更好的连接顺序。在 TPC-H 数据库的 100 GB 实例上，与在单独的后优化步骤中添加布隆过滤器的传统方法相比，我们的方法将涉及布隆过滤器的查询延迟进一步减少了 32.8%。我们的方法适用于所有查询类型，并且我们提供了几种启发式方法来平衡优化时间的有限增加与查询延迟的改善。

items.title: Doctopus: A System for Budget-aware Structural Data Extraction from Unstructured Documents.
items.abstract: Extracting structured data from unstructured documents is essential for applications like analytical SQL queries and decision-making. Strategies such as pre-trained language models (PLMs) can be employed, but they often fall short in quality. Large language models (LLMs) have shown effectiveness in attribute extraction but are costly, making them impractical for large-scale document sets. To best trade off quality and cost, we present Doctopus, a system designed for accurate and cost-effective attribute extraction. Overall, Doctopus combines LLMs with non-LLM strategies to achieve an optimal quality-cost balance. First, the system employs an index-based approach to efficiently identify and process only relevant chunks. Afterwards, it further estimates the quality of multiple strategies for each attribute. Finally, based on the cost and estimated quality, Doctopus dynamically selects the optimal strategies through budget-aware optimization. With a real-life scenario, we demonstrate that Doctopus allows users to extract attributes accurately and affordably. The corresponding video is available at https://youtu.be/Cxl_PfvZY10?si=NYoHt2SyD9KHqd6V.
items.abstract_zh: 从非结构化文档中提取结构化数据对于分析 SQL 查询和决策等应用程序至关重要。可以采用预训练语言模型 (PLM) 等策略，但它们的质量往往较差。大型语言模型 (LLM) 在属性提取方面已显示出有效性，但成本高昂，因此对于大规模文档集来说不切实际。为了最好地权衡质量和成本，我们推出了 Doctopus，这是一个专为准确且经济高效的属性提取而设计的系统。总体而言，Doctopus 将法学硕士与非法学硕士策略相结合，以实现最佳的质量成本平衡。首先，系统采用基于索引的方法来有效地识别和处理相关的块。然后，它进一步估计每个属性的多个策略的质量。最后，根据成本和估计质量，Doctopus 通过预算感知优化动态选择最佳策略。通过现实生活场景，我们证明 Doctopus 允许用户准确且经济地提取属性。相应的视频可在 https://youtu.be/Cxl_PfvZY10?si=NYoHt2SyD9KHqd6V 上观看。

items.title: RedTAO: A Trillion-edge High-throughput Graph Store.
items.abstract:
With the explosive growth of daily active users, the social graph data of Xiaohongshu has scaled to trillions of edges, imposing high pressure on our storage system. Current state-of-the-art systems struggle to address the issue, primarily due to: (1) Traditional relational databases as the back-end storage require frequent scaling, incurring high cost and stability risks. (2) Most graph databases focus on complex multi-hop queries. Redundant components in these systems make them difficult to take advantage when processing our workloads dominated by one-hop queries. (3) Using cache systems like Redis or Memcache often struggles to ensure consistency between the cache and storage.

In this paper, we propose RedTAO, which has a scalable and efficient graph cache layer optimized for social scenarios.
